target.title: fix(telegram): cache outbound replies for prompt context
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram prompt context cached inbound messages but not OpenClaw's own outbound Telegram replies, so follow-up turns could see multiple user messages without the assistant answer between them.
- Problem: normal streamed Telegram assistant finals are finalized through the draft-stream/edit path, so caching only `sendMessageTelegram` sends would still miss the central streamed-final path.
- Solution: record successful Telegram outbound sends and preview-finalized streamed finals into the Telegram message-context cache.
- Storage: move the persisted observed-message cache to plugin runtime state / SQLite KV, scoped by session-store path, with one-time import from the legacy `.telegram-messages.json` sidecar and JSONL fallback only when plugin state is unavailable.
- Cache accuracy: long streamed finals can split across the preview message and follow-up sends; the prompt-context cache records the actual text visible on the preview message id instead of attributing the full final to that first message.
- Scope boundary: this PR is Telegram-only. It does not change Codex context-engine projection or tool payload rendering.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram outbound assistant replies now survive into later Telegram prompt-context windows, including text sends, media sends, and streamed assistant finals. The persisted Telegram observed-message cache now uses the plugin SQLite KV store when available instead of writing a separate sidecar file.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/codex-context-tool-payloads`, rewritten head `ebef8fb80b6376b4e44d2d11f17adaf6c666222e`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: `Test Files 4 passed (4)`, `Tests 232 passed (232)`. The tests exercise direct Telegram sends, persisted message-cache selection for later Telegram context, plugin-state persistence, legacy sidecar migration, cross-scope isolation, streamed-final prompt-context recording, and long streamed-final preview cache accuracy.

Observed result after fix: Later Telegram context includes the assistant outbound reply body between earlier and later user messages. Long streamed finals record the text attached to the preview message id, avoiding duplicate/misattributed full-final context when follow-up chunks are sent separately. Existing legacy sidecar cache rows are imported into the scoped SQLite KV bucket without overriding newer plugin-state rows.

What was not tested: A full deployed Telegram-to-Codex production turn was not exercised in this rewrite. The proof uses the production Telegram message-cache helper and dispatch/send seams under focused tests.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: successful Telegram sends and preview-finalized streamed finals are recorded into the persisted prompt-context cache; plugin-state persistence is scoped and survives reload; legacy sidecar migration does not overwrite newer state; long streamed finals cache the actual preview message body.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it covers both Telegram delivery paths that can produce visible assistant replies, the storage migration path, and the selected-context path that later turns consume.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Future Telegram turns should be able to see OpenClaw's own recent Telegram replies in selected chat context, including streamed assistant finals, so a follow-up should not see only the user's earlier requests without the bot answer between them. The Telegram observed-message cache now uses the existing plugin SQLite KV store when available; deleting that state loses only derived Telegram prompt context, not the session transcript.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/send.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/lane-delivery.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `git diff --check origin/main`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

target.title: fix(channels): honor /verbose in group sessions
target.body:
## Summary

This fixes normal group verbose tool/progress summary delivery by honoring explicit group `/verbose` state.

- Problem: normal group sessions suppressed text-only tool/progress summaries even when `/verbose on` was enabled for that group.
- Solution: make shared group dispatch respect explicit group `/verbose` session state.
- What changed: normal groups remain quiet by default, but `/verbose on` now allows tool/progress summaries; `/verbose off` hides them again.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no new config flag, no new permissions, no token handling changes, no command/tool execution behavior changes, and inherited verbose defaults do not opt normal groups into visible summaries.

## Motivation

Private/group QA needs to show what the agent actually did when verbose mode is explicitly enabled. `/verbose` already controls that visibility, but normal group dispatch ignored it.

Although the live proof uses Telegram because that is the configured QA environment, the implementation is intentionally in shared group/ACP dispatch code so group-capable channels use the same verbose gating policy.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
  `/verbose on` did not show tool/progress summaries in a normal Telegram group.

- Real environment tested:
  Private Telegram QA supergroup with a Driver bot prompting a SUT bot running OpenClaw from this PR branch. Tokens, chat IDs, and private config are omitted.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Built current PR worktree at `207742e506`.
  2. Started the isolated QA SUT gateway from that build.
  3. Sent `/verbose@qa_sut_bot off`.
  4. Prompted: `Use exactly one shell command: pwd. Then reply exactly: qa-current-verbose-off-done.`
  5. Sent `/verbose@qa_sut_bot on`.
  6. Prompted: `Use exactly one shell command: pwd. Then reply exactly: qa-current-verbose-on-done.`

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  Copied live output from the Telegram QA group after the fix:

  ```text
  /verbose@qa_sut_bot off
  QA SUT: ⚙️ Verbose logging disabled.

  @qa_sut_bot Use exactly one shell command: pwd. Then reply exactly: qa-current-verbose-off-done.
  QA SUT: qa-current-verbose-off-done

  /verbose@qa_sut_bot on
  QA SUT: ⚙️ Verbose logging enabled.

  @qa_sut_bot Use exactly one shell command: pwd. Then reply exactly: qa-current-verbose-on-done.
  QA SUT: 🛠️ pwd
  QA SUT: qa-current-verbose-on-done
  ```

- Observed result after fix:
  `/verbose off` hides the tool summary. `/verbose on` shows `🛠️ pwd` before the final reply.

- What was not tested:
  I did not run the full repository test suite on this low-memory VPS. I do not have live Slack/Matrix/WhatsApp bots configured for proof, so non-Telegram channels are covered by focused shared-dispatch tests rather than live channel proof.

- Before evidence:
  Copied live Telegram transcript from `origin/main` showed that `/verbose on` still produced only the final marker in a normal group:

  ```text
  /verbose@qa_sut_bot off
  QA SUT: ⚙️ Verbose logging disabled.

  @qa_sut_bot Use exactly one shell command: pwd. Then reply exactly: qa-main-verbose-off-done.
  QA SUT: qa-main-verbose-off-done

  /verbose@qa_sut_bot on
  QA SUT: ⚙️ Verbose logging enabled.

  @qa_sut_bot Use exactly one shell command: pwd. Then reply exactly: qa-main-verbose-on-done.
  QA SUT: qa-main-verbose-on-done
  ```

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause:
  Shared group dispatch had a normal-group suppression path for text-only tool/progress summaries that did not account for explicit `/verbose` session state.

- Missing detection / guardrail:
  Existing tests covered default group suppression and topic/non-DM behavior, but not explicit `/verbose on` in normal groups.

- Contributing context:
  Telegram QA exposed the issue, but the fix is in shared group dispatch/projection logic.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient

- Target test or file:
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/acp-projector.test.ts`
  - `src/plugin-sdk/acp-runtime.test.ts`

- Scenario the test should lock in:
  Groups suppress tool summaries by default, allow them only when explicit group `/verbose on` is active, respect live `/verbose off`, preserve hidden-tool text boundaries, and keep existing topic/non-DM behavior unchanged.

- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:
  The bug is in shared dispatch/projection gates, so focused tests cover it without requiring live credentials for every channel.

- Existing test that already covers this (if any):
  Existing coverage was partial; new focused tests cover this case.

- If no new test is added, why not:
  N/A. New focused tests are included.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`/verbose on` now shows tool/progress summaries in normal group sessions. Groups remain quiet by default, with inherited verbose defaults, and with `/verbose off`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
group + /verbose on -> tool summary suppressed -> final reply only

After:
group + default/off -> final reply only
group + explicit /verbose on -> tool summary -> final reply
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No execution behavior changed; visible summary output can change only after explicit group `/verbose on`.
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  Risk: visible tool/progress summaries can expose command names or arguments to group members. Mitigation: normal groups remain quiet by default; inherited verbose defaults do not opt normal groups into visible summaries; users must explicitly enable `/verbose on` in that group session, and `/verbose off` hides summaries again.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux VPS
- Runtime/container: OpenClaw gateway built from this PR branch
- Model/provider: existing QA SUT config
- Integration/channel: Telegram normal supergroup
- Relevant config (redacted): private QA group with Driver bot and SUT bot

### Steps

1. Start QA SUT from this PR branch.
2. Send `/verbose@qa_sut_bot off`.
3. Prompt SUT to run `pwd` and reply with a marker.
4. Confirm only the final marker appears.
5. Send `/verbose@qa_sut_bot on`.
6. Prompt SUT to run `pwd` and reply with a marker.
7. Confirm `🛠️ pwd` appears before the final marker.

### Expected

Group tool/progress summaries are hidden with default/off state and visible with explicit `/verbose on`.

### Actual

Matches expected in live Telegram QA proof.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets / copied live output
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- AI assistance disclosure: Codex helped implement, review, and test this PR; I reviewed the diff and verified the behavior in a live OpenClaw Telegram QA setup.

- Verified scenarios:
  - `origin/main`: `/verbose on` still produced final reply only in a normal Telegram group.
  - This PR at `207742e506`: `/verbose off` produced final reply only.
  - This PR at `207742e506`: `/verbose on` produced visible `🛠️ pwd`, then final reply.

- Local checks:
  - `git diff --check`
  - `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
  - `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts -t "group tool summaries|group progress callbacks|live group tool-summary state|verbose progress"` -> `10 passed | 135 skipped`
  - `OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` -> `145 passed`

- Codex review:
  - Local Codex review passed before the final explicit-group-verbose tightening.
  - After the final tightening, focused tests and live Telegram proof above were rerun on `207742e506`.

- Edge cases checked:
  - Groups stay quiet by default.
  - Inherited `agents.defaults.verboseDefault: "on"` does not opt normal groups into visible summaries.
  - Explicit group `/verbose on` enables summaries.
  - `/verbose off` hides them again.

- What you did **not** verify:
  - Full repository suite.
  - Live non-Telegram channels.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: group members can see tool/progress summaries when verbose mode is enabled.
  - Mitigation: normal groups remain quiet by default; inherited verbose defaults do not enable normal group summaries; visible summaries require explicit `/verbose on` in that group session, and `/verbose off` hides them again.

target.title: fix(browser): validate current tab before snapshots
target.body:
## Summary
- Validates the selected browser tab URL before returning snapshot data.
- Adds route-level regression coverage for local-managed direct-CDP snapshot paths.

## Changes
- Applies the existing browser navigation result guard before `/snapshot` branches read page content.
- Keeps the existing request validation order for incompatible snapshot options.
- Covers ARIA and AI role snapshot CDP paths when the current tab is blocked by policy.

## Validation
- Ran `corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/agent.snapshot.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/agent.snapshot.local-managed.test.ts`
- Ran `corepack pnpm exec oxlint extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/agent.snapshot.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/agent.snapshot.local-managed.test.ts`
- Ran a static source-path check confirming the current-tab validation appears before direct-CDP snapshot calls.
- Attempted `corepack pnpm test -- extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/agent.snapshot.local-managed.test.ts`; local execution is blocked because the git-hosted `@openclaw/fs-safe` package was installed without built `dist` output after its prepack failed on a missing `JSX` namespace from `@types/mdx`.
- Attempted local agentic review with `claude -p "/review"`; the command stayed silent for multiple minutes and was terminated.

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `/snapshot` now validates the selected tab URL before any branch returns page snapshot data from ChromeMCP, Playwright, or direct CDP.
- **Real environment tested:** Local checkout of branch `570` at `a4fcffbfaf` on Node `v22.22.2`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a local `node` source-path check against `extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/agent.snapshot.ts` after the patch.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the after-fix `node` command:

```json
{
  "snapshotCurrentUrlCheckBeforeBranches": true,
  "snapshotPolicyContextBeforeBranches": true,
  "aiCdpCallsSnapshotRoleViaCdp": true,
  "ariaCdpCallsSnapshotAria": true
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The output shows `/snapshot` performs current-tab validation before the route reaches the direct-CDP AI and ARIA snapshot branches, while those CDP branches still exist and remain reachable only after the guard.
- **What was not tested:** Full browser-control runtime request proof was blocked locally because dependency installation cannot build the git-hosted `@openclaw/fs-safe` package; the test runner fails before test discovery with `Cannot find package '@openclaw/fs-safe/config'`.

## Notes
- No changelog entry; this is an internal browser-route behavior fix.
- Local install note: `corepack pnpm install` failed while preparing `@openclaw/fs-safe@0.1.2` from the git tarball with `TS2503: Cannot find namespace 'JSX'` in `@types/mdx`.

target.title: Fix WebChat dispatch failure session status
target.body:
## Summary

- Persist a terminal failed session lifecycle event when WebChat dispatch rejects before an assistant reply.
- Preserve terminal lifecycle errors from embedded-agent/provider failures when model fallback is exhausted, so fallback assistant text no longer clears the pending failure before the grace timer finalizes the session.
- Broadcast the matching `sessions.changed` payload with `status: "failed"` and accurate `hasActiveRun` so Control UI can clear the active run state.
- Add regression tests for pre-agent dispatch rejection, aborted dispatch rejection, embedded lifecycle-error finalization, exhausted fallback handling, and legacy session-key persistence.

## Root Cause

The WebChat `chat.send` async dispatch error path cleaned up the active run and broadcast a chat error, but it did not update persisted session lifecycle state. That left `sessions.list` able to return `status: "running"` with `hasActiveRun: false`, which kept the Control UI in a permanent running state.

For the real embedded-agent provider rejection path, the embedded run emitted `lifecycle:error`, but a later non-terminal fallback/assistant event cleared the pending terminal lifecycle error before the grace timer finalized it. The session therefore stayed persisted as `running` even though the active run had ended.

## Validation

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.list-changed.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/gateway/server-chat.ts src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server.chat.gateway-server-chat.test.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.ts src/gateway/session-lifecycle-state.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: WebChat sessions no longer remain `status: "running"` with no active run after an embedded AIGW provider 400 before reply.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw Gateway from this PR worktree using `atlassian-aigw/openclaw-localtest-missing-model-84352`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: started `openclaw-local-test --repo /Users/jmerhi/repos/claw-working-space/openclaw-pr-84352-review --state-dir ~/.openclaw-local-test-pr84352-provider-400-final --model openclaw-localtest-missing-model-84352 --no-channels --ttl 2h`, sent a `chat.send` WebSocket request to the local Gateway, then waited for the lifecycle grace window.
- Evidence after fix: local logs showed `400 "Bad Request"`, `LLM request failed: provider rejected the request schema or tool payload.`, and `Embedded agent failed before reply: LLM request failed: provider rejected the request schema or tool payload.`
- Observed result after fix: `~/.openclaw-local-test-pr84352-provider-400-final/agents/super/sessions/sessions.json` showed the affected session with `status: "failed"`, non-null `endedAt`, and non-null `runtimeMs`.
- What was not tested: Slack/Discord channel delivery; local proof used WebChat/Gateway only with channels disabled.


- Video evidence: `/Users/jmerhi/Downloads/Fixing UI Run Status Check Timing.mp4` captures the fixed WebChat run-status behavior locally. This is local evidence and is not committed.

target.title: i18n(zh-CN): improve Chinese translations for Control UI
target.body: Improve Chinese (Simplified) translations across the Control UI, covering common UI strings, agent tabs, dreaming mode messages, cron job forms, and usage metrics. Replace ambiguous or overly technical translations with clearer alternatives. Key changes: - Replace "Dreaming" with "梦境" for all Dreaming-related strings - Improve jitter/stagger related translations for clarity - Fix untranslated strings in agent tabs, notes, and stats sections - Localize Tailscale serve references - Standardize zh-CN language display names - Update common section values for consistency ## Summary - Problem: Many Control UI strings remained in English or used awkward translations when viewed in zh-CN locale - Solution: Rewrote 30+ translations with clearer, more natural Chinese alternatives; added translations for previously untranslated strings - What changed: Updated `ui/src/i18n/locales/zh-CN.ts` - What did NOT change: All other locale files, non-locale source code, functionality or behavior ## Motivation Chinese-speaking users of the Control UI frequently encountered English strings or confusing translations (e.g. "Dreaming" left untranslated, "抖动" for stagger/jitter which has a different meaning in Chinese). This PR improves the overall zh-CN user experience by replacing ambiguous or overly technical translations with clearer alternatives more natural for native speakers. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [x] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR N/A ## Real behavior proof - **Steps after patch:** 1. Loaded the modified zh-CN.ts into the gateway static assets 2. Set Control UI language to 简体中文 3. Refreshed the browser with cache cleared 4. target.title: feat: add verified plugin approval resolution
target.body:
## Review context Concrete plugin: [Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit](https://github.com/Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit). The intent is to keep World ID / AgentKit out of OpenClaw core while letting external HITL plugins use the existing approval flow end-to-end. This PR's role: the narrow verified resolver and ownership-check trust boundary; this is the most security-sensitive part. Bundle: - [#82431](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82431) exposes approval action metadata/UI rendering. - [#82434](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82434) adds verified approval resolution with plugin ownership checks. - [#82471](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82471) adds chat/status metadata and idempotency for durable approval updates. ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Agent participant Gateway participant Plugin as HITL plugin participant UI as TUI/Web UI participant Verifier as External verifier Agent->>Gateway: protected tool call Gateway->>Plugin: approval hook/request Gateway->>UI: approval card + plugin actions UI->>Plugin: user chooses verify once / trust session / deny Plugin->>Verifier: external proof flow Verifier-->>Plugin: verified result Plugin->>Gateway: resolve verified approval Gateway->>Agent: allow/deny blocked tool Plugin->>UI: status/update messages with idempotency ``` ```mermaid flowchart LR A["Approval action surface
#82431"] --> B["Verified resolver
#82434"] B --> C["Status/chat idempotency
#82471"] C --> D["Usable external HITL plugin flow"] ``` Trust boundary to review: should a plugin that already owns a pending approval be allowed to complete that specific approval after external verification, through a typed SDK/Gateway path with admin scope and ownership checks? ## Summary - Add an admin-scoped `plugin.approval.resolveVerified` Gateway method for plugin-owned verified HITL resolution. - Require the caller to provide `pluginId` and reject resolution when the pending request is owned by a different plugin. - Keep the existing `plugin.approval.resolve` path approval-scoped and allowed-decision-enforced. - Expose only a narrow `resolveVerifiedPluginApprovalOverGateway` helper through the plugin SDK runtime subpath; generic admin Gateway helpers stay internal. - Update docs, method scopes, protocol schema, generated Swift protocol model, and the plugin SDK API baseline. ## Verification - `pnpm protocol:check` - `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` - `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke` - `pnpm docs:list` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts src/gateway/method-scopes.test.ts --testTimeout 120000 --reporter dot` - `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/gateway-runtime.ts` - `git diff --check` - GitHub PR checks green at `c0f46d9ce09c773976687b06b5b4527ebde5a5e9`. ## Real behavior proof Behavior addressed: a plugin that has independently verified a human approval proof can resolve only its own pending plugin approval without weakening the normal user-facing approval resolver or exposing a generic admin Gateway client through the public plugin SDK. Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, rebased onto current `origin/main` on May 16, 2026, plus GitHub Actions for the PR head. Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran protocol generation/checking, plugin SDK API baseline checking, plugin SDK strict declaration smoke, docs inventory, focused Gateway approval/method-scope/operator-client Vitest coverage, targeted formatter checking, whitespace diff checking, and watched the GitHub PR check rollup. Evidence after fix: ```text $ pnpm protocol:check wrote dist/protocol.schema.json wrote apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Sources/OpenClawProtocol/GatewayModels.swift $ pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256 $ pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke (exit 0) $ pnpm docs:list (exit 0) $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts src/gateway/method-scopes.test.ts --testTimeout 120000 --reporter dot Test Files 7 passed (7) Tests 300 passed (300) $ node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.ts src/gateway/operator-approvals-client.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/gateway-runtime.ts (exit 0) $ git diff --check (no output) $ gh pr checks 82434 --repo openclaw/openclaw (all required OpenClaw checks pass at c0f46d9ce09c773976687b06b5b4527ebde5a5e9) ``` Observed result after fix: the normal resolver remains approval-scoped and allowed-decision-enforced, the verified resolver is admin-scoped and plugin-owner-checked, the public SDK exposes a narrow verified approval helper rather than a generic admin client, and the protocol/SDK/docs surfaces describe the new method. What was not tested: full release suite and live World ID callback flow. This PR intentionally depends on #82431 for custom plugin approval command actions and no-route pending behavior. Refs #82336. ## External plugin integration proof Behavior addressed: the external ClawHub AgentKit plugin can consume the combined temporary OpenClaw host API branch that includes this PR's narrow verified plugin approval resolver. Real environment tested: GitHub Actions in `Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit` on May 16, 2026, checking out `Guardiola31337/openclaw@agentkit/external-plugin-host-apis` and linking it into the external plugin package. Exact steps or command run after this patch: the remote workflow checked out the temporary OpenClaw host branch, installed with frozen lockfiles, ran `pnpm build gatewayWatch`, ran `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke`, linked the host checkout into the AgentKit plugin, then ran `pnpm test:hitl` and `pnpm pack:check`. Evidence after fix: ```text $ gh run watch 25972624778 --repo Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit --exit-status ✓ main CI · 25972624778 ✓ build in 1m46s ✓ Run pnpm build gatewayWatch ✓ Run pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke ✓ Run pnpm dev:link-openclaw .openclaw-source ✓ Run pnpm test:hitl ✓ Run pnpm pack:check ``` Observed result after fix: the external plugin builds against the split OpenClaw host APIs, uses the narrow `resolveVerifiedPluginApprovalOverGateway` helper instead of a generic admin client, the AgentKit HITL proof passes, and the package dry-run includes the expected ClawHub plugin artifact. What was not tested: production World ID credential verification. This remote proof exercises the integrated temporary host branch because the external plugin requires this PR together with #82431 and #82471 for the full HITL path. ## Additional real Gateway proof (May 17, 2026) Behavior addressed: `plugin.approval.resolveVerified` resolves a real pending plugin approval through the Gateway method path, using an admin-scoped verifier client and the plugin ownership check, without mocking the resolver. Real environment tested: local #82434 checkout on macOS, real loopback Gateway from `startGatewayServer`, three real WebSocket `GatewayClient` connections: watcher with `operator.approvals`, requester with `operator.approvals`, and verifier with `operator.admin`. Bundled plugins/channels/providers were disabled to keep the proof scoped to the Gateway approval surface. Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module` with an inline script that created a temporary OpenClaw home/config, started the loopback Gateway, connected the three clients, called `plugin.approval.request` with `pluginId: "agentkit"` and `twoPhase: true`, called `plugin.approval.resolveVerified` from the admin client with the same `pluginId`, listed pending approvals afterward, and attempted a wrong-plugin verified resolve to prove the ownership guard is active. Evidence after fix: ```text [gateway] http server listening (0 plugins) [gateway] ready accepted_status=accepted approval_id=plugin:3e0b4e02-2563-4d52-a41a-ecfaab2bc9e5 resolve_verified_ok=true pending_after=0 events=admin:plugin.approval.requested:,requester:plugin.approval.resolved:allow-once,admin:plugin.approval.resolved:allow-once post_resolve_second_attempt=GatewayClientRequestError: plugin approval is not owned by the requested plugin ``` Observed result after fix: the real Gateway accepted a pending AgentKit-owned plugin approval, the admin-scoped verified resolver resolved it once, pending approvals dropped to zero, resolved events were emitted to connected clients, and the wrong-plugin verified resolve was rejected by the plugin ownership guard. What was not tested: production World ID credential verification and full release suite; this proof intentionally isolates the Gateway resolver added by this PR.

target.title: Fix/spawned by cache leak
target.body:
# fix(gateway): evict `spawnedByCache` on lifecycle end to prevent memory leak

## Summary

- **Problem:** `spawnedByCache` — a `Map<string, string | null>` inside the `createAgentEventHandler` closure — has no eviction. Every unique subagent / ACP sessionKey is cached on first event and never removed. Because each subagent spawn generates a new `crypto.randomUUID()` key, the Map grows by one entry per run and is never collected.
- **Solution:** call `spawnedByCache.delete(sessionKey)` inside `finalizeLifecycleEvent` at both exit paths (early return + main cleanup block). This is the existing function that already clears all other per-run state on `lifecycle: end` / `lifecycle: error`.
- **What changed:** 2 lines added in [`src/gateway/server-chat.ts`](src/gateway/server-chat.ts); 1 new test describe-block in [`src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts`](src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts).
- **What did NOT change:** cache semantics (still caches per sessionKey on hot path), `resolveSpawnedBy` call sites (lines 517, 583, 629, 829), all public APIs, all other Maps in the handler.

## Motivation

Issue [#54155](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/54155): gateway RSS grows from 389 MB to 14.7 GB over 4 days under normal subagent workloads. Three prior fix commits ([`820dc38525`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/820dc3852530bffd2451bb1a6ddbcdd9f6324d66), [`ae57eb635c`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/ae57eb635c), [`761cdc967d`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/761cdc967d)) addressed `seqByRun`, `runContextById`, the session store cache, and `stateByNodeId`. The issue stayed open because `spawnedByCache` was introduced the same day as those fixes (commit [`443ca4865d`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/443ca4865d610333a57721c30f14ee1925947be8), PR [#63244](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/63244)) without eviction, and was not caught in review.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Memory / storage

## Linked Issue/PR

- Contributes to #54155
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `spawnedByCache` retaining all unique subagent sessionKeys indefinitely inside the `createAgentEventHandler` closure.
- **Real environment tested:** vitest unit test using a single `createAgentEventHandler` instance (same as production: one handler for all runs).
- **Exact command run after this patch:**
  ```
  npx vitest run src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts -t "memory leak"
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```
  ✓ spawnedByCache memory leak > spawnedByCache size stays at 0 after N runs with unique sessionKeys (84ms)
  Tests: 1 passed
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** `spawnedByCache.size === 0` after 500 runs with 500 unique sessionKeys.
- **What was not tested:** sustained multi-day RSS measurement in a real gateway process.
- **Before evidence:**
  ```
  × spawnedByCache memory leak > spawnedByCache size stays at 0 after N runs with unique sessionKeys
    AssertionError: expected 500 to be +0
    - Expected: 0
    + Received: 500
  ```

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** PR [#63244](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/63244) introduced `spawnedByCache = new Map()` inside the `createAgentEventHandler` closure to avoid repeated store reads on the hot event path. The cache was add-only: `.set()` was called on every new sessionKey, `.delete()` was never called. Because subagent spawns generate a new `crypto.randomUUID()` per invocation ([`subagent-spawn.ts:853`](src/gateway/subagent-spawn.ts#L853)), the key space is unbounded.
- **Missing detection / guardrail:** Existing tests create a fresh `createAgentEventHandler` instance per `it()` (via `createHarness()`), so the Map is destroyed after each test. The leak is only visible in production where one handler processes thousands of runs over days.
- **Contributing context:** The feature PR landed the same day as [`820dc38525`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/820dc3852530bffd2451bb1a6ddbcdd9f6324d66) ("add TTL cleanup for 3 Maps causing OOM") but was reviewed independently. The two PRs were not coordinated, and the eviction pattern established in the fix was not applied to the new Map.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Coverage level:**
  - [x] Unit test
- **Target file:** [`src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts`](src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts) — `describe("spawnedByCache memory leak")`
- **Scenario locked in:** one `createAgentEventHandler` instance processes 500 runs with 500 unique subagent sessionKeys; after all `lifecycle: end` events, `spawnedByCache.size` must equal 0.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** directly measures the leaked collection without heap-measurement noise. Intercepts `global.Map` during handler construction to capture the closure-internal Map, then checks `.size` after the loop. No GC dependency, deterministic.
- **Existing test that already covers this:** none — prior tests used a fresh harness per `it()`, which masked the leak.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None. `spawnedBy` is immutable once set; re-fetching after eviction returns the identical value. The cache remains functionally correct across both `mode="run"` (one-shot, key never reused) and `mode="session"` (persistent, key reused — costs one extra store read per new run, value is always the same).

## Diagram

**Before:**
```
[subagent run N starts]  →  resolveSpawnedBy()  →  spawnedByCache.set(key-N, value)
[subagent run N ends]    →  finalizeLifecycleEvent()  →  (no delete)
                                                          spawnedByCache.size = N
[after 10 000 runs]      →  spawnedByCache.size = 10 000  →  growing RSS
```

**After:**
```
[subagent run N starts]  →  resolveSpawnedBy()  →  spawnedByCache.set(key-N, value)
[subagent run N ends]    →  finalizeLifecycleEvent()  →  spawnedByCache.delete(key-N)
                                                          spawnedByCache.size ≤ 1
[after 10 000 runs]      →  spawnedByCache.size = 0  →  stable RSS
```

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Windows 11 / any (test is process-local)
- Runtime: Node.js v22+
- Model/provider: N/A (unit test, no LLM calls)
- Integration/channel: N/A

### Steps

1. Check out this branch.
2. Run `npx vitest run src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts --reporter=verbose`.
3. Observe all 69 tests pass, including `spawnedByCache memory leak`.

### Expected

`spawnedByCache.size === 0` after 500 runs with unique sessionKeys.

### Actual (before fix)

`spawnedByCache.size === 500` — one retained entry per run.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test before + passing after

**Before (without fix):**
```
× spawnedByCache memory leak > spawnedByCache size stays at 0 after N runs with unique sessionKeys
  AssertionError: expected 500 to be +0
  - Expected: 0
  + Received: 500
  Tests: 1 failed | 68 passed (69)
```

**After (with fix):**
```
✓ spawnedByCache memory leak > spawnedByCache size stays at 0 after N runs with unique sessionKeys (84ms)
  Tests: 69 passed (69)
```

## Human Verification

- **Verified scenarios:**
  - Both delete paths in `finalizeLifecycleEvent` are exercised: main path (registry entry present) and early-return path (registry shift returns null).
  - `spawnedBy` is immutable — confirmed in [`subagent-spawn.ts`](src/agents/subagent-spawn.ts): written once at spawn, never patched after.
  - `mode="session"` (persistent subagent session): same sessionKey may appear in subsequent runs. Deletion causes one extra store read per new run. Value is always identical — no correctness impact.
  - `mode="run"` (one-shot): sessionKey is never reused after `lifecycle:end`. Deletion is free.
- **Edge cases checked:** out-of-order `lifecycle:end` (handled by `scheduleTerminalLifecycleError` grace period — delete still runs inside `finalizeLifecycleEvent`); aborted runs (take the aborted branch in `finalizeLifecycleEvent`, reach the `if (sessionKey)` block where delete now lives).
- **What you did not verify:** RSS reduction in a live gateway process running for multiple days.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** `mode="session"` persistent subagents incur one extra `loadGatewaySessionRow` call at the start of each new run after the first.
  - **Mitigation:** `spawnedBy` is immutable, so the re-fetched value is always correct. The extra read is synchronous and local — cost is negligible compared to the alternative (unbounded Map growth causing OOM).

target.title: fix(config): do not suppress recovery retry after failed backup restore
target.body:
## Summary

`maybeRecoverSuspiciousConfigRead` (and its sync variant) unconditionally record `lastObservedSuspiciousSignature` in the config health state after attempting a backup restore, even when `restoredFromBackup` is `false` (i.e., `copyFile` failed with EACCES, disk full, etc.).

The dedup guard in `resolveConfigReadRecoveryContext` (line 495) compares the current suspicious signature against the stored one and returns `null` (skip recovery) when they match. This means a single failed restore permanently suppresses all future recovery attempts for that config file, silently accepting the clobbered config on every subsequent launch.

## Fix

Wrap the `lastObservedSuspiciousSignature` recording in `if (restoredFromBackup)` so the dedup guard only activates when the backup was actually restored. Applied to both the async path (line 683) and the sync path (line 793).

When the restore fails, the signature is not recorded, so the next launch retries recovery. Once a retry succeeds, the signature is recorded normally and dedup resumes.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Failed config backup restore permanently suppresses future recovery attempts via unconditional dedup signature recording.

Real environment tested: macOS, Node 24, pnpm, Vitest 4.1.7, from a fresh `upstream/main` worktree.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
cd /tmp/fix-config-recovery-retry
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/io.observe-recovery.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

Evidence after fix:
```
 ✓ records copyFile failure instead of falsely claiming restore succeeded 2ms
 ✓ sync recovery records copyFileSync failure instead of falsely claiming restore succeeded 2ms
 ✓ retries recovery on next launch after a failed copyFile restore 3ms
 ✓ sync recovery retries on next launch after a failed copyFileSync restore 3ms
 ✓ dedupes repeated suspicious hashes 2ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  20 passed (20)
```

The two new tests (`retries recovery on next launch after a failed copyFile restore` and `sync recovery retries on next launch after a failed copyFileSync restore`) exercise the exact bug scenario:
1. Seed a good config with backup, clobber the config
2. First call: `copyFile` rejects with EACCES, `restoredFromBackup = false`
3. Verify 1 audit event with `restoredFromBackup: false`
4. Second call: `copyFile` succeeds (simulating next launch)
5. Verify 2 audit events, second with `restoredFromBackup: true`
6. Verify on-disk config is now the restored backup content

Before this patch, step 4 would return early at `resolveConfigReadRecoveryContext` (signature matched) and never attempt recovery, leaving only 1 audit event.

Observed result after fix: 20/20 tests pass. The existing `dedupes repeated suspicious hashes` test confirms that successful restores still record the signature for normal dedup. tsgo clean, oxlint 0 warnings.

What was not tested: Live `pnpm dev` with a real clobbered config file, because the recovery path requires a config with `gateway.mode` set and a `.bak` file with matching structure. The function is exercised via its injected `deps` interface using real filesystem operations (not mocks) in a temp directory.

target.title: fix(ui): show failed tool results as errors
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: failed tool results in chat could look like successful tool output with a checkmark.
- Solution: propagate explicit `isError` / `is_error` flags into tool cards and add bounded fallback detection for common error payloads.
- What changed: tool cards, standalone tool-result summaries, sidebar content, and expanded output labels now render failed tool results with error styling and copy.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no agent execution, gateway protocol, tool runtime, or NemoClaw code paths were changed.

## Motivation

- NVIDIA/NemoClaw#2621 showed that an OpenClaw UI surface could display a tool failure as normal output, which hid the real failure mode from users during triage.
- This should be fixed in OpenClaw because the underlying tool result already carries error information or an error payload; the UI was not consistently surfacing it.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related NVIDIA/NemoClaw#2621
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: chat tool cards and standalone tool-result summaries now show failed tool results as `Tool error` with error styling instead of `Tool output` / `View` with a success checkmark.
- Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw worktree; Vite UI harness with system Chrome; focused Vitest UI tests.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/tool-cards.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.test.ts`

target.title: fix(plugins): return plugin gateway method results
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: plugin gateway method handlers that completed by returning a value could leave `openclaw gateway call` waiting until timeout when the handler did not explicitly call `respond(...)`.
- Solution: wrap plugin-registered gateway handlers so non-`undefined` return values are converted into successful gateway responses.
- What changed: plugin gateway registry entries and descriptors now use the wrapped handler, with regression coverage for returned values and explicit `respond(...)` precedence.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): handlers that return `undefined` and never call `respond(...)` still time out, preserving callback-style asynchronous semantics.

## Motivation

- Fixes user-visible gateway call timeouts for plugin gateway methods that successfully finish work but use return-value style completion.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #59470
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: plugin gateway methods that return a non-`undefined` value now complete the gateway RPC instead of leaving callers to time out.
- Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu-24.04 source checkout on Node/pnpm repo dependencies.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/loader.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- Evidence after fix: focused loader regression test passed with 136 tests, including `adapts returned plugin gateway method values into responses` and `keeps explicit plugin gateway responses authoritative`.
- Observed result after fix: returned `{ ok: true, status: "ready" }` produced one successful gateway response, while a handler that both called `respond(...)` and returned a value kept the explicit response authoritative.
- What was not tested: a packaged end-to-end plugin invoked through a live gateway process.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: plugin gateway handlers were stored as callback-style `GatewayRequestHandler`s, so a handler that returned a value without calling `respond(...)` completed locally but never sent an RPC response.
- Missing detection / guardrail: loader coverage did not exercise return-value style plugin gateway methods.
- Contributing context (if known): the SDK-facing handler shape allowed async return values even though the gateway dispatch path only observed `respond(...)`.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/plugins/loader.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: config-loaded plugins can register gateway methods that complete by returning a non-`undefined` value, while explicit `respond(...)` still wins.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises the plugin registration seam that stores gateway handlers without needing a full gateway process.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Plugin gateway methods that return a non-`undefined` value now complete `openclaw gateway call` successfully instead of timing out.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[plugin gateway handler returns value] -> [no respond call] -> [gateway caller times out]

After:
[plugin gateway handler returns value] -> [registry adapter responds] -> [gateway caller receives result]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: WSL Ubuntu-24.04
- Runtime/container: Node 22 repo checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): plugin gateway method registration
- Relevant config (redacted): config-loaded local plugin path in test fixture

### Steps

1. Register a config-loaded plugin gateway method that returns `{ ok: true, status: "ready" }`.
2. Invoke the registered gateway handler through the plugin registry.
3. Repeat with a handler that explicitly calls `respond(...)` and also returns another value.

### Expected

- Return-value handlers produce a successful response.
- Explicit `respond(...)` handlers are not overwritten by returned values.

### Actual

- Matches expected after this patch.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: returned gateway method values are adapted into responses; explicit responses remain authoritative.
- Edge cases checked: `undefined` returns still do not auto-respond, preserving callback-style async behavior.
- What you did **not** verify: live packaged gateway call against an installed third-party plugin.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a plugin that accidentally returns a non-`undefined` internal value without calling `respond(...)` will now send that value to the caller.
  - Mitigation: the adapter only applies to plugin gateway methods, keeps explicit `respond(...)` authoritative, and ignores `undefined` so existing callback-style handlers keep their current behavior.

target.title: fix(telegram): deliver deferred long-turn replies
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Telegram direct-message turns that run past the inbound handler deadline can leave the user without a durable final/failure response, and older foreground reply freshness can cancel valid deferred finals.
- Solution: Add explicit Telegram long-turn delivery state that sends a soft-deadline deferral, lets the run continue in the background, and delivers a terminal final/failure/silent-completion message back to the same chat/thread.
- What changed: Telegram dispatch gates deferral notices, protects deferred reply fences from ordinary supersedes, still honors explicit aborts, and uses a private internal foreground-freshness symbol so deferred finals can survive shared reply suppression without exposing a public SDK option.
- CI parity: The plugin activation boundary fixture now passes its manifest model-normalization policy directly into static normalization, matching the current pruned model catalog behavior without loading plugin runtime.
- Scope boundary: Group turns are not deferred in this PR, so existing group silence/NO_REPLY behavior stays intact; /status, /abort command UX, dedupe, and live Telegram proof remain follow-up work.

## Motivation

This is PR B from the upstream reliability handoff for OPS-0pgd, after replay-invalid recovery in #85362. It closes the direct Telegram long-turn delivery gap before the later auth-profile, config-overlay, and observability PRs.

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #85362
- Related OPS-0pgd

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: Telegram direct-message turns that exceed the soft deadline now send a run-id deferral, continue dispatch outside the inbound handler, and resolve the visible deferral with a final, failure, or silent-completion terminal message unless explicitly aborted.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS source checkout, Node v26.0.0, pnpm 11.2.2, branch head `09aaed2f51d7315b7d0403f794e0cdb8e253d0c7` rebased onto `origin/main` `86a0502711`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff --check`; `pnpm lint --threads=8`; `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`; `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions.tsbuildinfo`; `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`; `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/telegram-reply-fence.test.ts src/auto-reply/dispatch.freshness.test.ts src/plugin-activation-boundary.test.ts`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`.
- Evidence after fix: `git diff --check`, lint, and all four tsgo graphs exited 0. Focused regression output:

```text
Test Files  5 passed (5)
     Tests  119 passed (119)
  Start at  21:19:44
  Duration  6.90s (transform 2.66s, setup 229ms, import 1.69s, tests 4.67s, environment 0ms)
```

Autoreview reported `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.
- Observed result after fix: Regression coverage shows deferred direct turns wait for the deferral notice before final delivery, survive later ordinary same-session messages, still stop on explicit abort, release reply-fence state if fallback delivery throws, preserve group silence, and resolve deferred NO_REPLY completion with a terminal completion message instead of leaving the initial deferral hanging. The boundary test now proves static manifest normalization for the pruned xAI alias without activating plugin runtime.
- What was not tested: A live Telegram bot with real Telegram/OpenAI credentials was not run. Pre-submit broad remote `pnpm check:changed` was attempted but blocked locally: Blacksmith Testbox needed a missing `blacksmith` CLI, and direct AWS Crabbox failed because AWS SSO credentials were expired. PR CI should provide the broad Linux gate.
- Before evidence: Handoff OPS-0pgd documents the missing durable final delivery after the soft deadline; previous code returned from the inbound handler without a durable continuation/final-delivery contract for direct Telegram turns.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Telegram processing was bounded by the inbound handler lifecycle and reply-freshness fences that treated all older foreground replies as stale, with no explicit long-turn state tying a visible deferral to a later terminal delivery.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There were no Telegram tests for soft-deadline deferral ordering, deferred final freshness, abort-vs-normal supersede, fallback-send failures, or intentional silent completion after a deferral notice.
- Contributing context: Shared foreground freshness is correct for ordinary turns but needed an internal channel-owned escape hatch for already-deferred runs.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level that should have caught this: unit and seam/integration coverage.
- Target tests: `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`, `extensions/telegram/src/telegram-reply-fence.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/dispatch.freshness.test.ts`, `src/plugin-activation-boundary.test.ts`.
- Scenario locked in: long direct turn defers after the soft deadline, final/failure/silent-completion arrives after the deferral notice, later ordinary messages do not cancel deferred finals, explicit aborts still cancel, groups remain silent, and static manifest model normalization remains runtime-cold.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: These files exercise the Telegram processor, dispatch delivery seam, Telegram reply-fence state, shared foreground freshness gate, and plugin activation boundary without needing live credentials.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Direct Telegram users can now see `Still working on this... Run: <id>` after a long turn exceeds the soft deadline. That visible deferral is followed by the eventual assistant response, a failure message with run/session/target context, or a terminal silent-completion message when the run intentionally produced no visible reply. Group chats keep existing non-deferred silence behavior.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: A visible deferral could be sent for a run that later intentionally produces no visible assistant reply.
  - Mitigation: Once a deferral is visible, deferred silent completion now sends a terminal completion message instead of leaving the chat hanging.
- Risk: Deferred reply protection could accidentally ignore real aborts.
  - Mitigation: Reply-fence tests cover ordinary supersede protection separately from explicit abort behavior.

This PR improves the overall zh-CN user experience by replacing ambiguous or overly technical translations with clearer alternatives more natural for native speakers. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [x] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR N/A ## Real behavior proof - **Steps after patch:** 1. Loaded the modified zh-CN.ts into the gateway static assets 2. Set Control UI language to 简体中文 3. Refreshed the browser with cache cleared 4. Navigated through Dreaming, Cron Jobs, and agent settings tabs - **Observed result after fix:** All targeted strings now display in improved Chinese (e.g., "梦境" instead of "Dreaming", "定时任务" instead of "Cron Jobs") - **Evidence after fix:** (attach one screenshot of the Control UI showing the new Chinese translations) - **What was not tested:** The locale is a build-time artifact — syntax validation confirms structural integrity ## Root Cause N/A — not a bug fix. ## Regression Test Plan N/A — not a bug fix. ## User-visible / Behavior Changes zh-CN Control UI users will see: - "Dreaming" → "梦境" throughout dreaming section - "Cron Jobs" → "定时任务" in tabs, stats, and form fields - "Tailscale serve" → "Tailscale 服务" - "Browser enabled" → "浏览器已启用" - "Tool profile" → "工具配置" - Improved stagger/jitter terminology for clarity - Previously untranslated strings in restart confirmation dialog, empty job states, and job form now in Chinese ## Security Impact - New permissions/capabilities? **No** - Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No** - New/changed network calls? **No** - Command/tool execution surface changed? **No** - Data access scope changed? **No** ## Human Verification - Verified scenarios: Compared each changed string against the English source (en.ts) to ensure accuracy - Edge cases checked: Template interpolation patterns ({count}, {value}, {time}) preserved correctly; braces balanced; TypeScript structure intact - What I did **not** verify: Visual rendering (locale is compiled by Vite at build time) ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? **Yes** - Config/env changes? **No** - Migration needed? **No** ## Risks and Mitigations None — locale data change only, no logic, no API surface, no new dependencies.

The PR persists explicit configured-default model selections as user session pins across reply, Gateway, agent-command, reset, status, docs, and tests while keeping `/model default`, `model=default`, and `model:null` as clear paths.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main clears configured-default selections in `applyModelOverrideToSessionEntry`, while current status/docs expose that as a clear path. I did not run a live session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: Maintainers need to decide the compatibility contract for configured-default chat selections and require real behavior proof before merge; this should not be rewritten automatically without that decision.
