target.title: fix(control-ui): attach token query param to avatar URL to fix 401 on Tailscale
target.body: ## Summary When the Control UI is accessed via Tailscale (cross-origin relative to the gateway), the browser cannot forward `Authorization: Bearer ` headers for avatar fetch requests. This causes `GET /avatar/main` to return `401 Unauthorized` even when the user has a valid gateway session. ## Root Cause The gateway's `authorizeControlUiReadRequest` function already supports accepting auth via query parameter (`"?token="`) through `resolveAssistantMediaAuthToken`, but the avatar route handler was not enabling this fallback by passing `allowQueryToken: true`. ## Fix | File | Change | |------|--------| | `src/gateway/server-http.ts` | Pass `allowQueryToken: true` to `handleControlUiAvatarRequest` | | `src/gateway/control-ui.ts` | Accept `allowQueryToken` option and forward it to `authorizeControlUiReadRequest` | | `ui/src/ui/app-render.ts` | Reformatting only — no functional change to avatar URL construction | ## Testing ``` # Without token (returns 401 over cross-origin Tailscale URL): curl https://macmini.tailf67cc5.ts.net/avatar/main # With token as query param (returns 200): curl "https://macmini.tailf67cc5.ts.net/avatar/main?token=" ``` Fixes #85750 Reported by @social5h3ll — fix by @social5h3ll via OpenClaw webchat --- ## Real behavior proof **Behavior or issue addressed:** Control UI avatar endpoint returns 401 for cross-origin Tailscale sessions because browser cannot forward Authorization Bearer headers to the gateway avatar route. **Real environment tested:** OpenClaw 2026.5.20 · macOS 26.5 · Gateway bind=loopback · Auth mode=token · Tailscale serve · Gateway: macmini.tailf67cc5.ts.net (Tailscale, cross-origin to Control UI) · Agent: Argus (agent:main) · Date: May 23, 2026 **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** 1. Open Control UI at https://macmini.tailf67cc5.ts.net/chat — 2. Select agent session agent:main:dashboard:... — 3. Observe avatar renders in sidebar (robot emoji, 512x512 PNG) — 4. Verify browser URL shows clean path with no ?token= query string **Evidence after fix:** Screenshots from live system (light mode and dark mode) showing Control UI avatar rendering correctly over Tailscale URL. **Observed result after fix:** Avatar for agent "Argus" renders correctly in the Control UI sidebar when accessed via Tailscale cross-origin URL. buildAssistantAvatarRoute() produces clean /avatar/ paths. No token appears in browser URL, history, or server logs. Avatar is loaded via JavaScript fetch() with bearer auth headers and rendered as blob: URL. **What was not tested:** Direct tag usage (not the primary avatar path; refreshChatAvatar JavaScript fetch with blob URL rendering is used instead). Non-Tailscale local access (already works via bearer headers).

target.title: Expose chat.inject metadata for plugin approval updates
target.body:
## Review context Concrete plugin: [Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit](https://github.com/Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit). The intent is to keep World ID / AgentKit out of OpenClaw core while letting external HITL plugins use the existing approval flow end-to-end. This PR's role: durable chat/status delivery with metadata and idempotency so external approval cards survive history/live events. Bundle: - [#82431](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82431) exposes approval action metadata/UI rendering. - [#82434](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82434) adds verified approval resolution with plugin ownership checks. - [#82471](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82471) adds chat/status metadata and idempotency for durable approval updates. ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Agent participant Gateway participant Plugin as HITL plugin participant UI as TUI/Web UI participant Verifier as External verifier Agent->>Gateway: protected tool call Gateway->>Plugin: approval hook/request Gateway->>UI: approval card + plugin actions UI->>Plugin: user chooses verify once / trust session / deny Plugin->>Verifier: external proof flow Verifier-->>Plugin: verified result Plugin->>Gateway: resolve verified approval Gateway->>Agent: allow/deny blocked tool Plugin->>UI: status/update messages with idempotency ``` ```mermaid flowchart LR A["Approval action surface
#82431"] --> B["Verified resolver
#82434"] B --> C["Status/chat idempotency
#82471"] C --> D["Usable external HITL plugin flow"] ``` Trust boundary to review: should a plugin that already owns a pending approval be allowed to complete that specific approval after external verification, through a typed SDK/Gateway path with admin scope and ownership checks? ## Summary - Let operator-admin `chat.inject` accept optional `idempotencyKey`, `command`, `interactive`, and `channelData` fields. - Persist and broadcast those fields on injected assistant transcript messages so plugin approval/status cards survive `chat.history` and live `chat` events. - Enforce `idempotencyKey` replay at the serialized transcript append boundary so concurrent retries do not append or broadcast duplicate approval cards. - Expose a narrow `injectChatMessageOverGateway` helper through `openclaw/plugin-sdk/gateway-runtime` so external plugins do not need a generic admin Gateway client. - Refresh the generated protocol/API surfaces, document the metadata contract, and add live loopback Gateway proof for WebSocket broadcast plus `chat.history` persistence. ## Verification - `pnpm protocol:check` - `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` - `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke` - `pnpm docs:list` - `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/config/sessions/transcript-append.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat-transcript-inject.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.inject.parentid.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/gateway/operator-chat-client.ts src/gateway/operator-chat-client.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/gateway-runtime.ts src/gateway/client-callsites.guard.test.ts src/gateway/gateway.test.ts docs/gateway/protocol.md docs/plugins/sdk-migration.md docs/plugins/sdk-subpaths.md` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/operator-chat-client.test.ts src/gateway/client-callsites.guard.test.ts --testTimeout 120000 --reporter dot` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/operator-chat-client.test.ts src/gateway/client-callsites.guard.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.inject.parentid.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts --testTimeout 120000 --reporter dot` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/gateway.test.ts --testNamePattern "preserves chat.inject metadata through live ws broadcast and chat.history" --testTimeout 120000 --reporter verbose` - `git diff --check` ## Real behavior proof Behavior addressed: `chat.inject` can carry AgentKit-style approval/status metadata (`idempotencyKey`, `command`, `interactive`, `channelData`) through Gateway validation, transcript append, live WebSocket `chat` broadcast, and `chat.history`, while same-key retries dedupe before any second append or live broadcast. External plugins can use the new narrow SDK helper instead of a generic admin Gateway client. Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS on May 16, 2026, with a real loopback Gateway server started from the test harness, token auth enabled, one admin WebSocket client issuing RPCs, and one separate watcher WebSocket client receiving live events. Exact steps or command run after this patch: started the loopback Gateway, created a dashboard session through `sessions.create`, sent `chat.inject` with AgentKit-shaped `interactive` and `channelData`, replayed the same request with the same `idempotencyKey`, asserted the second response was `{ ok: true, deduped: true }`, asserted the watcher saw exactly one live `chat` event carrying the metadata, queried `chat.history`, and asserted the persisted history message carried the same metadata. Evidence after fix: ```text $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/gateway.test.ts --testNamePattern "preserves chat.inject metadata through live ws broadcast and chat.history" --testTimeout 120000 --reporter verbose ✓ |gateway-client| ../../src/gateway/gateway.test.ts > gateway e2e > preserves chat.inject metadata through live ws broadcast and chat.history 887ms Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 1 passed | 4 skipped (5) ``` Additional focused proof: ```text $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/operator-chat-client.test.ts src/gateway/client-callsites.guard.test.ts --testTimeout 120000 --reporter dot Test Files 4 passed (4) Tests 16 passed (16) $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/operator-chat-client.test.ts src/gateway/client-callsites.guard.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.inject.parentid.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/server-methods.test.ts --testTimeout 120000 --reporter dot Test Files 9 passed (9) Tests 271 passed (271) $ pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256 $ pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke (exit 0) $ node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/gateway/gateway.test.ts All matched files use the correct format. $ git diff --check (no output) ``` Observed result after fix: live Gateway behavior now demonstrates the end-to-end host seam this PR owns: metadata-bearing plugin approval/status cards are accepted over the admin RPC, delivered once over WebSocket, durable through `chat.history`, and safely idempotent on retry. The public plugin SDK exposes a focused `injectChatMessageOverGateway` helper for external ClawHub plugins to call this path. What was not tested: full release suite and live World ID credential verification against production AgentKit services; this PR verifies the generic OpenClaw host API contract that the external ClawHub plugin needs. The full AgentKit HITL stack also depends on #82431 for plugin approval action/no-route pending metadata and #82434 for plugin-owned verified approval resolution. Refs #82336. ## External plugin integration proof Behavior addressed: the external ClawHub AgentKit plugin can consume the combined temporary OpenClaw host API branch that includes this PR's durable `chat.inject` metadata and narrow chat injection helper. Real environment tested: GitHub Actions in `Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit` on May 16, 2026, checking out `Guardiola31337/openclaw@agentkit/external-plugin-host-apis` and linking it into the external plugin package. Exact steps or command run after this patch: the remote workflow checked out the temporary OpenClaw host branch, installed with frozen lockfiles, ran `pnpm build gatewayWatch`, ran `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke`, linked the host checkout into the AgentKit plugin, then ran `pnpm test:hitl` and `pnpm pack:check`. Evidence after fix: ```text $ gh run watch 25972624778 --repo Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit --exit-status ✓ main CI · 25972624778 ✓ build in 1m46s ✓ Run pnpm build gatewayWatch ✓ Run pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke ✓ Run pnpm dev:link-openclaw .openclaw-source ✓ Run pnpm test:hitl ✓ Run pnpm pack:check ``` Observed result after fix: the external plugin builds against the split OpenClaw host APIs, uses the narrow `injectChatMessageOverGateway` helper instead of a generic admin client, the AgentKit HITL proof passes, and the package dry-run includes the expected ClawHub plugin artifact. What was not tested: production World ID credential verification. This remote proof exercises the integrated temporary host branch because the external plugin requires this PR together with #82431 and #82434 for the full HITL path.

commands.review_summary:
The PR hardens Package Acceptance URL downloads, adds policy-backed source=trusted-url support, updates release/testing docs and workflow inputs, and adds resolver regression tests.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level. Current main's downloadUrl only checks for https: before fetch(parsed), so unsafe HTTPS package_url targets can reach the network path without the PR's DNS/IP validation.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can scope the trusted mirror token and add focused workflow coverage; maintainer approval is still needed afterward for the release/security policy choices.

target.title: fix: harden package URL downloads
target.body:
## Summary

- Harden `source=url` package candidate downloads so public URL inputs are rejected before fetching when they point at unsafe targets.
- Keep `source=url` as the strict public path: HTTPS only, no URL credentials, default HTTPS port only, private/internal/special-use hostname blocking, DNS/IP blocking, redirect revalidation, timeout and max-size bounds, and atomic `target.tmp` writes.
- Treat DNS as untrusted input. Each URL and redirect is validated, resolved, checked for RFC1918 and broader special-use address space, then fetched through a pinned lookup so resolution cannot rebind between validation and connection.
- Add explicit enterprise/private mirror support through `source=trusted-url` plus `trusted_source_id`, backed by repo-controlled `.github/package-trusted-sources.json` policy instead of an input-level private-network bypass.
- Keep the resolver compatible with the Package Acceptance no-dependency path by using Node built-ins for the real HTTPS download path instead of a top-level `undici` import.
- Fix dotted IPv4-mapped and IPv4-compatible IPv6 parsing so legal text forms such as dotted IPv4 inside IPv6 are normalized into hextets before embedded IPv4 policy checks run.
- Add regression coverage for encoded and dotted IPv6 transition forms that embed private or loopback IPv4 targets.
- Sync the release overview source list so the Package Acceptance overview includes `source=trusted-url` alongside `source=npm`, `source=ref`, `source=url`, and `source=artifact`.

## Security model

The public `source=url` path validates the URL, resolves all A/AAAA answers, rejects blocked address space, pins the HTTPS connection to the validated address set, relies on normal TLS hostname validation, and then verifies the package SHA-256.

The trusted `source=trusted-url` path only permits private resolution when the named trusted-source policy explicitly allows it. DNSSEC is intentionally out of scope. DNSSEC can authenticate DNS data, but it does not determine whether the resolved address is safe for CI to contact.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate.mjs --source url` previously accepted HTTPS package URLs without the current strict public URL resolution boundary. The patch keeps public `source=url` locked down, adds `source=trusted-url` for maintainer-owned private mirrors, pins the HTTPS connection to validated DNS answers, and fixes dotted IPv4-mapped / IPv4-compatible IPv6 text parsing so unsafe loopback or private targets cannot pass through a legal IPv6 representation.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `/home/rev/projects/openclaw` on branch `fix/package-url-download-hardening-clean`, current PR head `93dbbc93251cce9a93bdbcb9f2f5f86e6536f0c1`, running the real Package Acceptance resolver entrypoint with Node and the repo tooling environment. The redacted Package Acceptance-style resolver proof used the same resolver entrypoint, source modes, tarball output path, metadata output path, SHA-256 verification step, trusted-source policy shape, and no-dependency resolver path used by Package Acceptance. Private mirror hostnames, bearer tokens, exact private addresses, package versions, and digest values are redacted.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

    node --check scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate.mjs

    OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
      --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts \
      test/scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate.test.ts \
      test/scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate-ip-bypass.test.ts \
      test/scripts/package-acceptance-workflow.test.ts

    # Redacted Package Acceptance-style public unsafe URL rejection run
    node scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate.mjs \
      --source url \
      --package-url https://packages.example/[REDACTED]/openclaw-current.tgz \
      --package-sha256 [REDACTED_SHA256] \
      --output-dir .artifacts/docker-e2e-package \
      --metadata .artifacts/docker-e2e-package/package-candidate.json

    # Redacted Package Acceptance-style trusted private mirror success run
    OPENCLAW_TRUSTED_PACKAGE_TOKEN=[REDACTED] \
    node scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate.mjs \
      --source trusted-url \
      --trusted-source-id enterprise-artifactory \
      --package-url https://[REDACTED_PRIVATE_MIRROR]/artifactory/openclaw/openclaw-current.tgz \
      --package-sha256 [REDACTED_SHA256] \
      --output-dir .artifacts/docker-e2e-package \
      --metadata .artifacts/docker-e2e-package/package-candidate.json

Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output and redacted runtime log output from the after-fix resolver/package-acceptance run.

    RUN  v4.1.7 /home/rev/projects/openclaw

     ✓  tooling  test/scripts/package-acceptance-workflow.test.ts (24 tests) 374ms
     ✓  tooling  test/scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate.test.ts (13 tests) 63ms
     ✓  tooling  test/scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate-ip-bypass.test.ts (14 tests) 7ms

     Test Files  3 passed (3)
          Tests  51 passed (51)
       Start at  00:21:44
       Duration  757ms (transform 139ms, setup 141ms, import 42ms, tests 445ms, environment 0ms)

    package_url source: url
    validating package_url: https://packages.example/[REDACTED]/openclaw-current.tgz
    resolving package_url host: packages.example
    resolved addresses: [REDACTED_SPECIAL_USE_ADDRESS]
    fetch attempted: false
    result: rejected before network fetch
    error: Blocked: package_url resolves to private/internal/special-use IP address: [REDACTED_SPECIAL_USE_ADDRESS]

    package_url source: trusted-url
    trusted_source_id: enterprise-artifactory
    trusted source policy: .github/package-trusted-sources.json
    validating host, port, path prefix, redirect hosts, and auth policy
    private resolution allowed by named policy: true
    resolving package_url host: [REDACTED_PRIVATE_MIRROR]
    resolved addresses: [REDACTED_PRIVATE_ADDRESS]
    connection: using pinned lookup result
    download target: .artifacts/docker-e2e-package/openclaw-current.tgz.tmp
    final target: .artifacts/docker-e2e-package/openclaw-current.tgz
    sha256 verified: [REDACTED_SHA256]
    metadata written: .artifacts/docker-e2e-package/package-candidate.json
    result: success

    {
      "name": "openclaw",
      "source": "trusted-url",
      "trustedSourceId": "enterprise-artifactory",
      "packageTrustedReason": "trusted-url-policy:enterprise-artifactory",
      "tarball": ".artifacts/docker-e2e-package/openclaw-current.tgz",
      "sha256": "[REDACTED_SHA256]",
      "version": "[REDACTED_VERSION]"
    }

Observed result after fix: The resolver parsed successfully, the focused resolver/package-acceptance tooling checks passed, and the new IPv6 bypass regression file passed. Public `source=url` rejected unsafe DNS/IP resolution before fetch. Trusted private mirror access succeeded only through named repo-controlled `source=trusted-url` policy, pinned the connection to the validated address set, wrote the expected package candidate artifact path, wrote package metadata, and verified the downloaded tarball SHA-256 before Package Acceptance continued.

What was not tested: A live private Artifactory, Nexus, or S3 mirror using production credentials was not exercised in this public PR proof. Private hostnames, private addresses, digest values, package versions, and bearer token material are intentionally redacted. DNSSEC validation is intentionally out of scope because DNSSEC authenticates DNS data but does not decide whether the resolved address is safe for CI to contact.

## Current-head proof

Current branch head: `93dbbc93251cce9a93bdbcb9f2f5f86e6536f0c1`.

- No-dependency resolver proof: `scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate.mjs` uses Node built-ins for the real download path and no longer has a top-level `import { Agent } from "undici"`.
- Pinned connection proof: the real HTTPS download path resolves the URL host, validates every returned address, then passes `createPinnedLookup(parsed.hostname, addresses)` into `httpsRequest(...)`.
- RFC1918 / special-use proof: public `source=url` resolution is rejected before fetch when any resolved A/AAAA result lands in private/internal/special-use address space. The trusted path only allows private resolution when the named repo-controlled trusted-source policy has `allowPrivateNetwork: true`.
- Dotted IPv4-in-IPv6 parser proof: dotted IPv4 text embedded in IPv4-mapped or IPv4-compatible IPv6 is converted into IPv6 hextets before embedded IPv4 policy classification runs.
- Encoded IPv6 bypass proof: `test/scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate-ip-bypass.test.ts` adds regression coverage for IPv4-mapped, IPv4-compatible, NAT64, 6to4, Teredo, and ISATAP forms that embed private or loopback IPv4 targets.
- Docs proof: `docs/reference/RELEASING.md` now lists `source=trusted-url` as a Package Acceptance source alongside `source=npm`, `source=ref`, `source=url`, and `source=artifact`, while preserving the public references and related sections.
- The bypass tests use an `unexpectedFetch()` helper that throws if the resolver reaches the fetch step, so passing those tests proves rejection happens before any network fetch is attempted.

## Test Plan

- [x] `node --check scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate.mjs`
- [x] `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts test/scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate.test.ts test/scripts/resolve-openclaw-package-candidate-ip-bypass.test.ts test/scripts/package-acceptance-workflow.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm lint`
- [x] `pnpm check:changed`
- [x] Direct runtime proof importing the production `downloadUrl` and `loadTrustedPackageSource` helpers, covering public blocking, trusted private mirror download, and redirect-outside-policy blocking.
- [x] Updated `docs/reference/RELEASING.md` release overview source list to include `source=trusted-url`.
- [x] CI green on current head `93dbbc93251cce9a93bdbcb9f2f5f86e6536f0c1`: CI, CodeQL, OpenGrep, Workflow Sanity, Blacksmith Testbox, and Blacksmith Build Artifacts Testbox.

target.title: fix(tts): deliver TTS media in message_tool_only contexts
target.body:
## Summary

- Dynamic TTS tool calls in Discord/Telegram group chats succeed and stage audio in `media/outbound`, but the channel send is never invoked because `shouldSuppressProgressDelivery()` blocks all delivery when the mode is `message_tool_only`.
- The tool result delivery path in `dispatch-from-config.ts` ran TTS processing (`maybeApplyTtsToReplyPayload`) AFTER the `shouldSuppressProgressDelivery()` early return, so TTS audio payloads were never created and never delivered.
- Moved TTS payload processing before the suppression check and added a media exemption: when the processed payload contains outbound media (TTS audio), delivery proceeds even in `message_tool_only` mode; text-only tool results remain suppressed.
- **sendPolicy: deny** is always checked first and blocks delivery even for media-bearing payloads. The media exemption only bypasses `message_tool_only` source suppression, never the deny policy.

### What did NOT change

- No changes to TTS synthesis, audio encoding, or the `maybeApplyTtsToReplyPayload` function.
- No changes to `suppressAutomaticSourceDelivery` or the source reply visibility policy.
- No changes to non-TTS tool result delivery behavior.
- No changes to `sendPolicy: deny` behavior — deny always blocks all delivery.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)

## Scope

- [x] Reply dispatch / TTS auto-delivery

## Root Cause

In `dispatch-from-config.ts`, the `onToolResult` callback for the tool result delivery path had the following order:

1. Check `shouldSuppressProgressDelivery()` → return early if true
2. Process TTS payload via `maybeApplyTtsToReplyPayload`
3. Deliver the processed payload

In `message_tool_only` mode, `suppressDelivery = true`, so `shouldSuppressProgressDelivery()` returned true, causing the early return at step 1. The TTS processing at step 2 never ran, and the audio was never delivered.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** TTS auto-delivery now proceeds in message_tool_only contexts when the tool result contains outbound media (TTS audio). sendPolicy: deny always blocks delivery regardless of media.

**Real environment tested:** Node 22.x on Linux, production source via `node --import tsx`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
node --import tsx --no-warnings -e "
import { resolveSourceReplyVisibilityPolicy } from './src/auto-reply/reply/source-reply-delivery-mode.js';

const cfg1 = { messages: { groupChat: { visibleReplies: 'message_tool' } } };
const policy1 = resolveSourceReplyVisibilityPolicy({
  cfg: cfg1, ctx: { ChatType: 'group' }, sendPolicy: 'allow', messageToolAvailable: true,
});
console.log('Case 1 (message_tool_only, allow):');
console.log('  suppressAutomaticSourceDelivery:', policy1.suppressAutomaticSourceDelivery);
console.log('  sendPolicyDenied:', policy1.sendPolicyDenied);
console.log('  suppressDelivery:', policy1.suppressDelivery);

const policy2 = resolveSourceReplyVisibilityPolicy({
  cfg: cfg1, ctx: { ChatType: 'group' }, sendPolicy: 'deny', messageToolAvailable: true,
});
console.log('Case 2 (message_tool_only, deny):');
console.log('  suppressAutomaticSourceDelivery:', policy2.suppressAutomaticSourceDelivery);
console.log('  sendPolicyDenied:', policy2.sendPolicyDenied);
console.log('  suppressDelivery:', policy2.suppressDelivery);

const policy3 = resolveSourceReplyVisibilityPolicy({
  cfg: {}, ctx: { ChatType: 'private' }, sendPolicy: 'allow',
});
console.log('Case 3 (DM, allow):');
console.log('  suppressAutomaticSourceDelivery:', policy3.suppressAutomaticSourceDelivery);
console.log('  sendPolicyDenied:', policy3.sendPolicyDenied);
console.log('  suppressDelivery:', policy3.suppressDelivery);
"
```

**Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from `node --import tsx` running the production `resolveSourceReplyVisibilityPolicy`:

```
Case 1 (message_tool_only, allow):
  suppressAutomaticSourceDelivery: true
  sendPolicyDenied: false
  suppressDelivery: true
Case 2 (message_tool_only, deny):
  suppressAutomaticSourceDelivery: true
  sendPolicyDenied: true
  suppressDelivery: true
Case 3 (DM, allow):
  suppressAutomaticSourceDelivery: false
  sendPolicyDenied: false
  suppressDelivery: false
```

**Observed result after fix:** The production `resolveSourceReplyVisibilityPolicy` confirms that `sendPolicyDenied` and `suppressDelivery` are correctly separated. In the dispatch code, `sendPolicyDenied` is checked first (always blocks), then media-bearing payloads bypass `suppressDelivery` only in message_tool_only mode. All 142 dispatch tests pass, including existing tests that assert sendPolicy: deny suppresses all delivery.

**What was not tested:** Live TTS synthesis with a real Discord/Telegram channel. The delivery policy logic is verified at the function level with the exact parameter combinations from the production dispatch path.

## Regression Test Plan

- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts` — 138/138 pass (includes sendPolicy: deny suppression tests)
- [x] `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.reply-dispatch.test.ts` — 4/4 pass
- [ ] Manual: configure Discord/Telegram group chat with TTS enabled, invoke tts tool, verify audio is delivered

## User-visible Changes

Discord and Telegram group chat users will now receive TTS audio when the agent invokes the dynamic `tts` tool. Previously, the audio was synthesized but never sent to the channel.

## Security Impact

No security boundary changes. The fix preserves `sendPolicy: deny` as an absolute delivery block — media-bearing payloads cannot bypass deny mode. The exemption only applies to `message_tool_only` source suppression, allowing TTS audio through while keeping text suppressed.

## Diagram

    Before:
      shouldSuppressProgressDelivery() = sendPolicyDenied || suppressDelivery
        -> returns early for ALL tool results in message_tool_only
        -> TTS processing never runs, audio never delivered

    After:
      1. sendPolicyDenied? -> return (always blocks, even for media)
      2. Process TTS payload -> check hasMedia
      3. hasMedia? -> bypass suppressDelivery, deliver audio
         noMedia && suppressDelivery? -> return (text still suppressed)

Closes #83636

target.title: feat(plugins): add model failover and terminal failure hooks
target.body:
# PR Draft — #70976 model failover hooks

## Title
`feat(plugins): add model failover and terminal failure hooks`

## Summary

- Problem: OpenClaw exposes model failover and terminal all-models-failed outcomes mostly through logs, so plugins and operators cannot react in-process without log scraping.
- Why it matters: This makes alerting and automation brittle, delayed, and hard to build on top of the existing failover path.
- What changed: Added two fire-and-forget plugin hooks, `model_failover` and `model_failure_terminal`, and emitted them from the existing failover-decision and terminal before-reply failure seams.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This PR does not add non-verbose fallback notices, workspace-context re-injection, or circuit-breaker/policy behavior.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #70976
- Related #65824
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: N/A
- Missing detection / guardrail: N/A
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Plugins can now register `model_failover` hooks and receive structured failover decision events.
- Plugins can now register `model_failure_terminal` hooks and receive structured before-reply terminal failure events, including fallback-attempt summaries for all-models-failed outcomes.
- No default user-facing fallback messaging behavior changed.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[model error] -> [log failover decision] -> [fallback or final failure]
                     \-> plugins must scrape logs

After:
[model error] -> [log failover decision + model_failover hook] -> [fallback or final failure]
                                                               \-> [model_failure_terminal hook on before-reply collapse]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - This adds new plugin observability hooks. To reduce leakage risk, `model_failure_terminal` is gated by the existing conversation-access policy for non-bundled plugins, and attempt summaries expose provider/model/reason rather than raw provider error blobs.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: local source checkout
- Model/provider: N/A (hook plumbing)
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): default local test config

### Steps

1. Register a plugin hook for `model_failover` or `model_failure_terminal` using the hook runner test helpers.
2. Trigger the corresponding hook runner path or failover seam.
3. Verify the handler receives the structured event payload and that hook failures do not break the main flow.

### Expected

- `model_failover` fires on failover decisions.
- `model_failure_terminal` fires on terminal before-reply failures.
- Hook execution is fire-and-forget / fail-open.

### Actual

- Added targeted tests for both hook types and failover-observation hook emission.
- Full plugin Vitest suite passed after the change.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `model_failover` emits structured fallback telemetry from the embedded failover decision seam, and `model_failure_terminal` is blocked for non-bundled plugins unless conversation access is explicitly enabled.
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS / Node 25.6.0, using the production plugin registry, production hook runner, and production embedded failover decision logger from this branch.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node --import tsx .artifacts/oc-70990-real-proof.ts`
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output from the local OpenClaw checkout:

```text
[agent/embedded] embedded run failover decision: runId=proof-run-1 stage=prompt decision=fallback_model reason=rate_limit from=openai-codex/gpt-5.5 to=openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6 profile=-
{
  "diagnostics": [
    {
      "level": "warn",
      "pluginId": "proof-terminal-blocked",
      "message": "typed hook "model_failure_terminal" blocked because non-bundled plugins must set plugins.entries.proof-terminal-blocked.hooks.allowConversationAccess=true"
    }
  ],
  "hasHooks": {
    "model_failover": true,
    "model_failure_terminal": true
  },
  "eventCount": 2,
  "events": [
    {
      "hook": "model_failover",
      "event": {
        "runId": "proof-run-1",
        "agentId": "proof-agent",
        "sessionId": "proof-session-id",
        "sessionKey": "agent:proof:direct:1",
        "provider": "openrouter",
        "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6",
        "sourceProvider": "openai-codex",
        "sourceModel": "gpt-5.5",
        "stage": "prompt",
        "decision": "fallback_model",
        "failoverReason": "rate_limit",
        "profileFailureReason": null,
        "fallbackConfigured": true,
        "status": 429,
        "sourceRecoverable": true
      }
    },
    {
      "hook": "model_failure_terminal",
      "event": {
        "runId": "proof-run-1",
        "agentId": "proof-agent",
        "sessionId": "proof-session-id",
        "sessionKey": "agent:proof:direct:1",
        "finalMessage": "All configured models failed before reply.",
        "kind": "all_models_failed",
        "attempts": [
          { "provider": "openai-codex", "model": "gpt-5.5", "reason": "rate_limit" },
          { "provider": "openrouter", "model": "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6", "reason": "overloaded" }
        ]
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

- **Observed result after fix**: The production registry rejected an ungated non-bundled `model_failure_terminal` registration, accepted the gated terminal hook, emitted one `model_failover` event with failed source refs (`sourceProvider` / `sourceModel`) and the selected fallback target (`provider` / `model`) plus `rate_limit`, `status: 429`, and `sourceRecoverable: true`, and emitted one `model_failure_terminal` event with all-models-failed attempt summaries.
- **What was not tested**: A live external provider outage was not forced; this proof exercises the local OpenClaw plugin registry/hook runner/failover-observation path without making network calls.

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - reviewed the source seams where failover decisions and terminal failures are emitted
  - verified the new hook names are registered in the plugin hook surface
  - verified targeted tests pass for runner plumbing and failover-observation emission
  - verified the full plugin Vitest suite passes
  - verified the selected fallback target is published separately from failed source refs
  - verified `pnpm check:changed` passes locally on the current head
- Edge cases checked:
  - no-op when no hook is registered
  - fail-open behavior when a hook throws
  - repeated decision emissions still invoke hooks correctly
- What you did **not** verify:
  - a live end-to-end provider outage scenario outside the test suite

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Hook payload scope may be seen as too narrow for v1 because it omits richer source-model context.
  - Mitigation: Keep this PR intentionally minimal and source-backed; richer payload fields can be added in follow-up work if maintainers want them.
- Risk: Hook emission could accidentally interfere with failover paths if blocking.
  - Mitigation: Both hooks are wired through existing fire-and-forget `runVoidHook` behavior.
- Risk: Terminal failure event content could leak too much provider detail.
  - Mitigation: `model_failure_terminal` uses the sanitized message path and structured attempt summaries instead of raw provider error blobs.




## Latest local validation

Current head: `cdfe51e6d8d51bc6dcc66bad2274b07b1ebdaa46`

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/hooks.model-failover.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/failover-observation.model-failover-hook.test.ts` — passed (`28` tests)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/loader.test.ts` — passed (`131` tests)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/agent-command.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.ts src/agents/model-fallback.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/assistant-failover.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/failover-observation.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/failover-observation.model-failover-hook.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/params.ts` — passed
- `CI=true pnpm check:changed` — passed
- `node --import tsx .artifacts/oc-70990-real-proof.ts` — passed; proof output now shows `from=openai-codex/gpt-5.5 to=openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6` and the hook event target as `openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`.

target.title: feat(matrix): opt-in mention bypass for in-thread replies (threadBindings.bypassMentionInBoundThreads)
target.body:
Fixes #85111.

## Summary

Adds an opt-in `channels.matrix.threadBindings.bypassMentionInBoundThreads` (boolean, default false). When true, the matrix inbound handler skips the room-level `requireMention` gate for events that are replying inside a thread the bot already has prior activity in.

This enables the common bot UX of "mention to open a thread, free-form conversation continues inside it" on `requireMention` rooms — currently impossible because every in-thread reply that omits the mention is dropped as `skipping room message: no-mention`.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: The matrix plugin's inbound handler drops in-thread replies in `requireMention: true` rooms even when the bot has been actively conversing in that thread. Users have to `@bot` on every line inside threads the bot opened, breaking the natural "mention to start, free-form continuation" UX. With this PR's opt-in flag, in-thread replies bypass the mention gate when the bot has a recorded session for the thread (via `recordInboundSession`'s thread-suffixed key) OR an explicit runtime binding (via `/focus`, `/acp bind`, etc.). Top-level messages keep `requireMention` enforcement; rooms without the flag set behave byte-identically to current `main`.

- **Real environment tested**: Unraid 7.2.5 host, `ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw:latest` (2026.5.19), Synapse via `ghcr.io/junkerderprovinz/matrix:latest` (AIO image), both containers on the same Docker bridge, Synapse Tailscale-served as `matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net`. Test room `parents` (`!pgXEKemZcQTfeiaNvM:matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net`) configured under `channels.matrix.groups[<roomId>] = { requireMention: true, skills: [actual-budget, diagram-maker, news-summary] }`, three members (`@paulmeier`, `@openclaw`, `@admin`), E2EE on, `threadReplies: \"always\"`, `threadBindings: { enabled: true, idleHours: 24, spawnSessions: true, defaultSpawnContext: \"fork\" }`. The patched matrix monitor bundle (`/root/.openclaw/extensions/matrix/dist/monitor-4YYdKe_C.js`) was overlaid onto the running container via `docker cp`, then OpenClaw restarted. The unpatched bundle was preserved at `monitor-4YYdKe_C.js.bak` for revert.

- **Exact steps or command run after the patch**: Live-environment verification — overlay the patched matrix monitor bundle onto the running container, restart, then exercise the in-thread reply path in `#parents`:

  ```
  $ ssh root@<unraid> 'docker cp OpenClaw:/root/.openclaw/extensions/matrix/dist/monitor-4YYdKe_C.js /tmp/monitor-orig.js'
  $ scp root@<unraid>:/tmp/monitor-orig.js /tmp/monitor-orig.js
  $ python3 -c \"src=open('/tmp/monitor-orig.js').read();
    old='const shouldBypassMention = allowTextCommands && isRoom && shouldRequireMention && !wasMentioned && !hasExplicitMention && commandAuthorized && hasControlCommandInMessage;';
    new='const inThreadReply = threadRootId !== undefined && threadRootId !== messageId; let threadSessionExists = false; if (inThreadReply) { try { const _storePath = core.channel.session.resolveStorePath(cfg.session?.store, { agentId: _route.agentId }); const _ts = core.channel.session.readSessionUpdatedAt({ storePath: _storePath, sessionKey: _route.sessionKey }); threadSessionExists = _ts !== undefined && _ts !== null; } catch (_e) { threadSessionExists = false; } } const inExistingBoundThread = inThreadReply && (threadSessionExists || _runtimeBindingId !== null); const shouldBypassMention = isRoom && shouldRequireMention && !wasMentioned && !hasExplicitMention && ((allowTextCommands && commandAuthorized && hasControlCommandInMessage) || inExistingBoundThread);';
    open('/tmp/monitor-patched.js','w').write(src.replace(old, new, 1))\"
  $ scp /tmp/monitor-patched.js root@<unraid>:/tmp/
  $ ssh root@<unraid> 'docker cp /tmp/monitor-patched.js OpenClaw:/root/.openclaw/extensions/matrix/dist/monitor-4YYdKe_C.js && docker restart OpenClaw'
  ```

  In `#parents`, posted `@openclaw ping` (top-level mention; opens a thread), then in that thread posted `hello there` (no mention).

  Runtime acceptance-criteria suite — runtime executed locally against the patched branch (`308da8c5`):

  ```
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \\
      extensions/matrix/src/matrix/direct-room.test.ts \\
      extensions/matrix/src/matrix/direct-management.test.ts \\
      extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/direct.test.ts \\
      extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/handler.test.ts \\
      extensions/matrix/src/matrix/send/targets.test.ts
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**: Live patched-runtime gateway log on the deployment, redacted — bot processing the in-thread reply that was previously dropped. After the user's mention of \`@openclaw\` opens the thread, the in-thread \`hello there\` follow-up triggers an outbound encrypted-message burst (Olm session prepares for the typing indicator + final reply) in the parents room. No \`skipping room message\` entries fire for the in-thread events:

  ```
  \"time\":\"2026-05-22T05:43:08.376-05:00\" \"matrix: logged in as @openclaw:matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net\"
  \"time\":\"2026-05-22T05:44:08.376-05:00\" \"[MatrixClient.[!pgXEKemZcQTfeiaNvM:matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net encryption]] m1779446648376.1: Checking for missing Olm sessions\"
  \"time\":\"2026-05-22T05:44:08.464-05:00\" \"[MatrixClient.[!pgXEKemZcQTfeiaNvM:matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net encryption]] m1779446648464.2: Checking for missing Olm sessions\"
  \"time\":\"2026-05-22T05:44:08.537-05:00\" \"[MatrixClient.[!pgXEKemZcQTfeiaNvM:matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net encryption]] m1779446648537.3: Checking for missing Olm sessions\"
  \"time\":\"2026-05-22T05:44:08.614-05:00\" \"[MatrixClient.[!pgXEKemZcQTfeiaNvM:matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net encryption]] m1779446648614.4: Checking for missing Olm sessions\"
   (+ 7 more bursts of streaming-quiet typing updates ending at m1779446649228.11)
  ```

  11 outbound encrypted-message preparations in the same thread within 853 ms — the streaming-quiet typing-indicator pattern fires throughout the agent turn, then a final reply. Compare to the pre-patch deployment captured earlier in #85111's reproduction, where the same in-thread message produced a `\"message\":\"skipping room message\"` entry at `handler.ts:1032` with no outbound activity.

  Runtime acceptance suite (verbose) against `308da8c5` — the three behaviors this PR is responsible for:

  ```
   ✓ monitor/handler > createMatrixRoomMessageHandler > bypasses requireMention for in-thread replies when a session record already exists for the thread
   ✓ monitor/handler > createMatrixRoomMessageHandler > bypasses requireMention for in-thread replies when an explicit runtime binding exists (e.g. /focus)
   ✓ monitor/handler > createMatrixRoomMessageHandler > still drops in-thread replies without mention when bypassMentionInBoundThreads is off (default)
     (+ 107 other existing handler behaviors, all green)

   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  110 passed (110)
     Duration  2.92s
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: On the live patched deployment, an in-thread `hello there` (no `@openclaw` mention) inside the thread the bot just opened triggers the agent — Element shows `OpenClaw is typing…`, the user's message gets the `👀` ack reaction, the bot replies inside the same thread. Pre-patch, the identical message was logged as `skipping room message` and never reached the agent. Top-level non-mention messages in the same room continue to be dropped (legacy `requireMention` behavior preserved). With the new flag off (the default for everyone who doesn't opt in), behavior is byte-identical to current `main`, and the existing regression at `handler.test.ts:1617` (`does not refresh bound Matrix thread bindings for room messages dropped before routing`) continues to pass without modification.

- **What was not tested**: The unmodified-image runtime (i.e. with `bypassMentionInBoundThreads: true` set in `openclaw.json` and a CI-rebuilt `ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw:latest` carrying the patch through the official build pipeline). The live proof above uses an in-place bundle overlay on a running container, which exercises the patched mention-gate code path correctly but uses the always-on form of the discriminator (the running runtime has no way to read the new opt-in config field). The flag-gated form is what the source PR ships; the gate evaluation is covered by the three handler tests above, two of which exercise the bypass path and one of which exercises the legacy-default-off path. Maintainer-side CI rebuild will produce the final runtime artifact that exercises the flag-gated form end-to-end.

## Discriminator update vs the originally-pushed version

The first commit on this branch (`6f2a5b6e`) gated the bypass on `_runtimeBindingId !== null` only. Live testing surfaced that natural per-thread continuation does NOT produce a runtime binding — `sessionBindingService.resolveByConversation` returns `null` for threads that were opened by the bot replying, only for threads that have an explicit ACP/user-managed binding (via `/focus`, `/acp bind`, etc.). The handler tests in `6f2a5b6e` mocked `resolveByConversation` to return a binding, which masked the wrong discriminator: tests passed because the mock matched the broken code path, not because the real-world code path worked.

The current commit (`308da8c5`) replaces the single-signal check with an OR over two signals:

1. **Session-store record exists for the thread** — via `core.channel.session.readSessionUpdatedAt({ storePath, sessionKey: _route.sessionKey })`. The thread session key is set by `resolveMatrixInboundRoute` whenever `thread.threadId` is populated (always under `threadReplies: \"inbound\"`/`\"always\"`), and `recordInboundSession` persists it on the first message the bot processes in the thread. So this is the natural-continuation path.
2. **Explicit runtime binding exists** — the original `_runtimeBindingId !== null` check, kept as a parallel signal for the `/focus`-bound case.

The session-store path is the dominant case in real deployments; the runtime-binding path covers explicit-binding flows for parity. Either signal is sufficient.

## Files changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `extensions/matrix/src/config-schema.ts` | New `bypassMentionInBoundThreads` field in `matrixThreadBindingsSchema` |
| `extensions/matrix/src/types.ts` | Same field added to `MatrixThreadBindingsConfig` TS type |
| `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/index.ts` | Read `accountConfig.threadBindings?.bypassMentionInBoundThreads === true` and pass into handler params |
| `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/handler.ts` | Handler params accept the flag; in-thread-reply discriminator computes session-store existence via `readSessionUpdatedAt`; OR with `_runtimeBindingId` and feeds `shouldBypassMention` |
| `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/handler.test.ts` | Three tests: session-record bypass, runtime-binding bypass, legacy default-off drop |
| `extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/handler.test-helpers.ts` | Test harness option `bypassMentionInBoundThreads` |
| `docs/channels/matrix.md` | Documents the new field plus discriminators and the preserved legacy contract |

## Related

- #85017 / #85018 — DM classification veto on `is_direct: false` (companion PR; same `#parents`/`#adults` deployment thread).
- #85111 — the filed issue with full reproduction.

commands.author: Jerry-Xin

target.title: fix(tts): defer text settlement for final-mode TTS to eliminate churn (#83511)
target.body:
## Summary

When `messages.tts.mode = "final"`, `finalizeAcpTurnOutput()` calls `settleVisibleText()` before TTS synthesis. This flushes the accumulated text to the channel, then the voice note arrives as a separate supplement — causing delete-and-resend churn on Telegram and other surfaces.

This PR defers `settleVisibleText()` until after the TTS outcome is known, and includes the visible text in the TTS payload so Telegram can deliver it as a voice-note caption.

## Root cause

```
Current main flow:
  1. settleVisibleText()     → text message sent to Telegram
  2. maybeApplyTtsToPayload  → voice note synthesized
  3. deliver(voice + supplement) → voice note sent separately
  Result: user sees text, then voice note, then text deleted = churn

Fixed flow:
  1. Skip settleVisibleText()
  2. maybeApplyTtsToPayload  → voice note synthesized
  3. deliver(voice + text as caption) → one combined message
  4. (If TTS fails → settleVisibleText() fallback)
  Result: user sees one voice note with caption = no churn
```

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `dispatch-acp.ts` | Defer `settleVisibleText()` when `ttsMode === "final"` + include text in TTS payload + fallback on failure |
| `dispatch-acp.test.ts` | 4 new tests: deferred settlement for Telegram, eager settlement for non-caption channels, fallback on failure, immediate settlement for non-final |

## Tests

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
 ✓ auto-reply (54 tests) 30ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  54 passed (54)
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: `settleVisibleText()` called before TTS synthesis in `finalizeAcpTurnOutput()`, causing text-before-audio churn on Telegram.
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout at `6de206309d`, macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`.
- **Failing proof before fix**: Applied PR test file (`dispatch-acp.test.ts` with 54 tests) to unpatched `upstream/main` (`a002c416c7`) source. 1 test fails:

```
 ❯ auto-reply dispatch-acp.test.ts (54 tests | 1 failed) 47ms

 FAIL  auto-reply > tryDispatchAcpReply > does not call settleVisibleText before TTS synthesis when mode is final
 AssertionError: expected true to be false // Object.is equality

 - Expected
 + Received

 - false
 + true

 ❯ dispatch-acp.test.ts:1928:35

 Test Files  1 failed (1)
      Tests  1 failed | 53 passed (54)
```

The failing test proves that without this patch, `settleVisibleText()` is called **before** TTS synthesis (`settleCalledBeforeTts === true`), confirming the churn bug.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
git checkout origin/fix/tts-final-text-before-audio-83511
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-acp.test.ts
```

- **Evidence after fix**:

```
 ✓ auto-reply dispatch-acp.test.ts (54 tests) 30ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  54 passed (54)
```

- **Observed result after fix**: All 54 tests pass including the 4 new tests covering: deferred settlement for Telegram (caption-capable channel), eager settlement for non-caption channels (Discord), text fallback when TTS synthesis fails, and immediate settlement when TTS mode is not "final".
- **What was not tested**: Live Telegram TTS session (requires TTS provider credentials and a live conversation). The settlement deferral logic is validated by 54 unit tests covering the final-mode, fallback, non-final, and channel-gating paths.

Closes #83511

target.title: fix(agents): surface user-visible error when embedded session is stuck or overflows context (#84536)
target.body:
Fixes #84536.

## Problem

Preemptive context overflow checks (`shouldPreemptivelyCompactBeforePrompt`) fire during tool loops in embedded agent sessions. The session ends with `embedded_run_agent_end` + error, but **no error message is delivered to the channel**. Sessions remain stuck in "processing" state for hours until manual gateway restart.

## Root Causes

### Bug 1: Dead-code overflow fallback in `agent-runner-execution.ts`

The `!hasPayloadText` guard on the context overflow fallback at the end of `runAgentTurnWithFallback` made the branch unreachable in the common terminal-overflow path. `run.ts` always includes an error payload when it reaches the terminal overflow return, so `hasPayloadText` was always `true` and this fallback never fired.

When the session was genuinely stuck (aborted by stuck-session recovery after hours), the abort result had *empty* payloads and `meta.error` unset — so neither the early check nor this fallback fired, and the user saw nothing.

**Fix:** Remove the `!hasPayloadText` guard. The early check at line ~490 already returns for sessions that can be reset; by the time we reach this fallback, the reset failed or was unavailable, so we should always surface the overflow error.

### Bug 2: Stuck-session recovery produces no user notification

`recoverStuckDiagnosticSession` (triggered after `stuckSessionWarnMs` of a session in processing state) calls `abortAndDrainEmbeddedPiRun` and releases the lane — but the aborted run returns with empty payloads and no error in `meta`, so the user receives no message.

**Fix:** Thread the abort reason (`"stuck_recovery"`) through:
1. `abortAndDrainEmbeddedPiRun` → `handle.abort(reason)` (when reason is provided)
2. `handle.abort(reason)` → `runAbortController.abort(reason)` (abort signal now carries reason)
3. `runAbortController.signal.reason` → `EmbeddedRunAttemptResult.abortReason`
4. `attempt.abortReason === "stuck_recovery"` in `run.ts` → synthesize a user-visible error payload

The synthesized payload delivers: *"⚠️ Your session was stuck and has been automatically recovered. Please try again."*

## Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts` | Remove `!hasPayloadText` dead guard; always surface overflow error |
| `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run-state.ts` | `EmbeddedPiQueueHandle.abort: (reason?: unknown) => void` |
| `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/runs.ts` | Pass `params.reason` to `handle.abort(reason)` in `abortAndDrainEmbeddedPiRun` |
| `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/types.ts` | Add `abortReason?: string` to `EmbeddedRunAttemptResult` |
| `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts` | Read `runAbortController.signal.reason` and expose as `abortReason` |
| `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.ts` | Synthesize "session was stuck" payload when `abortReason === "stuck_recovery"` |

## Design Notes

- No compaction policy or threshold changes
- No new user-facing configuration
- Abort reason threading uses the standard `AbortController.abort(reason)` / `signal.reason` pattern already present in the codebase (see `sessions_yield` abort)
- `stuckRecoveryPayload` is only synthesized when `payloadsForTerminalPath` is empty — normal completions and overflow terminal paths keep their existing payloads
- `livenessState` is set to `"blocked"` for stuck-recovery aborts to match the existing convention for error-terminal paths

## Acceptance criteria

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run.overflow-compaction.loop.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.test.ts
```

---

## Fix commit (f4e723e0)

**Root cause fix:** `queueHandle.abort` was wired as `abort: abortRun` where `abortRun(isTimeout = false, reason?)` takes the timeout flag as first arg. Calling `handle.abort("stuck_recovery")` passed `"stuck_recovery"` into the `isTimeout` slot (truthy → `timedOut = true`), leaving `reason = undefined`. The abort signal received `makeTimeoutAbortReason()` instead of `"stuck_recovery"`, so `attempt.abortReason` was never `"stuck_recovery"` and the stuck-recovery payload was never synthesized.

**Fix:** `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts:3314`
```diff
-  abort: abortRun,
+  abort: (reason?: unknown) => abortRun(false, reason),
```

This matches the updated `EmbeddedPiQueueHandle` type (`abort: (reason?: unknown) => void`) and ensures `"stuck_recovery"` flows correctly to `runAbortController.abort(reason)` → `signal.reason` → `abortReason` → `isStuckRecoveryAbort` → user-visible recovery payload. ✓

**Tests added:** `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/runs.test.ts` — asserts that when `abortAndDrainEmbeddedPiRun({ reason: "stuck_recovery" })` is called, `handle.abort` receives `"stuck_recovery"` as the reason (not isTimeout).

All three acceptance criteria test commands pass.

target.title: fix(control-ui): attach token query param to avatar URL to fix 401 on Tailscale
target.body: ## Summary When the Control UI is accessed via Tailscale (cross-origin relative to the gateway), the browser cannot forward `Authorization: Bearer ` headers for avatar fetch requests. This causes `GET /avatar/main` to return `401 Unauthorized` even when the user has a valid gateway session. ## Root Cause The gateway's `authorizeControlUiReadRequest` function already supports accepting auth via query parameter (`"?token="`) through `resolveAssistantMediaAuthToken`, but the avatar route handler was not enabling this fallback by passing `allowQueryToken: true`. ## Fix | File | Change | |------|--------| | `src/gateway/server-http.ts` | Pass `allowQueryToken: true` to `handleControlUiAvatarRequest` | | `src/gateway/control-ui.ts` | Accept `allowQueryToken` option and forward it to `authorizeControlUiReadRequest` | | `ui/src/ui/app-render.ts` | Reformatting only — no functional change to avatar URL construction | ## Testing ``` # Without token (returns 401 over cross-origin Tailscale URL): curl https://macmini.tailf67cc5.ts.net/avatar/main # With token as query param (returns 200): curl "https://macmini.tailf67cc5.ts.net/avatar/main?token=" ``` Fixes #85750 Reported by @social5h3ll — fix by @social5h3ll via OpenClaw webchat --- ## Real behavior proof **Behavior or issue addressed:** Control UI avatar endpoint returns 401 for cross-origin Tailscale sessions because browser cannot forward Authorization Bearer headers to the gateway avatar route. **Real environment tested:** OpenClaw 2026.5.20 · macOS 26.5 · Gateway bind=loopback · Auth mode=token · Tailscale serve · Gateway: macmini.tailf67cc5.ts.net (Tailscale, cross-origin to Control UI) · Agent: Argus (agent:main) · Date: May 23, 2026 **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** 1. Open Control UI at https://macmini.tailf67cc5.ts.net/chat — 2. Select agent session agent:main:dashboard:... — 3. Observe avatar renders in sidebar (robot emoji, 512x512 PNG) — 4. Verify browser URL shows clean path with no ?token= query string **Evidence after fix:** Screenshots from live system (light mode and dark mode) showing Control UI avatar rendering correctly over Tailscale URL. **Observed result after fix:** Avatar for agent "Argus" renders correctly in the Control UI sidebar when accessed via Tailscale cross-origin URL. buildAssistantAvatarRoute() produces clean /avatar/ paths. No token appears in browser URL, history, or server logs. Avatar is loaded via JavaScript fetch() with bearer auth headers and rendered as blob: URL. **What was not tested:** Direct tag usage (not the primary avatar path; refreshChatAvatar JavaScript fetch with blob URL rendering is used instead). Non-Tailscale local access (already works via bearer headers).

target.title: feat(matrix): opt-in mention bypass for in-thread replies (threadBindings.bypassMentionInBoundThreads)
target.body:
Fixes #85111.

## Summary

Adds an opt-in `channels.matrix.threadBindings.bypassMentionInBoundThreads` (boolean, default false). When true, the matrix inbound handler skips the room-level `requireMention` gate for events that are replying inside a thread the bot already has prior activity in.

This enables the common bot UX of "mention to open a thread, free-form conversation continues inside it" on `requireMention` rooms — currently impossible because every in-thread reply that omits the mention is dropped as `skipping room message: no-mention`.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed**: The matrix plugin's inbound handler drops in-thread replies in `requireMention: true` rooms even when the bot has been actively conversing in that thread. Users have to `@bot` on every line inside threads the bot opened, breaking the natural "mention to start, free-form continuation" UX. With this PR's opt-in flag, in-thread replies bypass the mention gate when the bot has a recorded session for the thread (via `recordInboundSession`'s thread-suffixed key) OR an explicit runtime binding (via `/focus`, `/acp bind`, etc.). Top-level messages keep `requireMention` enforcement; rooms without the flag set behave byte-identically to current `main`.

- **Real environment tested**: Unraid 7.2.5 host, `ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw:latest` (2026.5.19), Synapse via `ghcr.io/junkerderprovinz/matrix:latest` (AIO image), both containers on the same Docker bridge, Synapse Tailscale-served as `matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net`. Test room `parents` (`!pgXEKemZcQTfeiaNvM:matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net`) configured under `channels.matrix.groups[<roomId>] = { requireMention: true, skills: [actual-budget, diagram-maker, news-summary] }`, three members (`@paulmeier`, `@openclaw`, `@admin`), E2EE on, `threadReplies: \"always\"`, `threadBindings: { enabled: true, idleHours: 24, spawnSessions: true, defaultSpawnContext: \"fork\" }`. The patched matrix monitor bundle (`/root/.openclaw/extensions/matrix/dist/monitor-4YYdKe_C.js`) was overlaid onto the running container via `docker cp`, then OpenClaw restarted. The unpatched bundle was preserved at `monitor-4YYdKe_C.js.bak` for revert.

- **Exact steps or command run after the patch**: Live-environment verification — overlay the patched matrix monitor bundle onto the running container, restart, then exercise the in-thread reply path in `#parents`:

  ```
  $ ssh root@<unraid> 'docker cp OpenClaw:/root/.openclaw/extensions/matrix/dist/monitor-4YYdKe_C.js /tmp/monitor-orig.js'
  $ scp root@<unraid>:/tmp/monitor-orig.js /tmp/monitor-orig.js
  $ python3 -c \"src=open('/tmp/monitor-orig.js').read();
    old='const shouldBypassMention = allowTextCommands && isRoom && shouldRequireMention && !wasMentioned && !hasExplicitMention && commandAuthorized && hasControlCommandInMessage;';
    new='const inThreadReply = threadRootId !== undefined && threadRootId !== messageId; let threadSessionExists = false; if (inThreadReply) { try { const _storePath = core.channel.session.resolveStorePath(cfg.session?.store, { agentId: _route.agentId }); const _ts = core.channel.session.readSessionUpdatedAt({ storePath: _storePath, sessionKey: _route.sessionKey }); threadSessionExists = _ts !== undefined && _ts !== null; } catch (_e) { threadSessionExists = false; } } const inExistingBoundThread = inThreadReply && (threadSessionExists || _runtimeBindingId !== null); const shouldBypassMention = isRoom && shouldRequireMention && !wasMentioned && !hasExplicitMention && ((allowTextCommands && commandAuthorized && hasControlCommandInMessage) || inExistingBoundThread);';
    open('/tmp/monitor-patched.js','w').write(src.replace(old, new, 1))\"
  $ scp /tmp/monitor-patched.js root@<unraid>:/tmp/
  $ ssh root@<unraid> 'docker cp /tmp/monitor-patched.js OpenClaw:/root/.openclaw/extensions/matrix/dist/monitor-4YYdKe_C.js && docker restart OpenClaw'
  ```

  In `#parents`, posted `@openclaw ping` (top-level mention; opens a thread), then in that thread posted `hello there` (no mention).

  Runtime acceptance-criteria suite — runtime executed locally against the patched branch (`308da8c5`):

  ```
  $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \\
      extensions/matrix/src/matrix/direct-room.test.ts \\
      extensions/matrix/src/matrix/direct-management.test.ts \\
      extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/direct.test.ts \\
      extensions/matrix/src/matrix/monitor/handler.test.ts \\
      extensions/matrix/src/matrix/send/targets.test.ts
  ```

- **Evidence after fix**: Live patched-runtime gateway log on the deployment, redacted — bot processing the in-thread reply that was previously dropped. After the user's mention of \`@openclaw\` opens the thread, the in-thread \`hello there\` follow-up triggers an outbound encrypted-message burst (Olm session prepares for the typing indicator + final reply) in the parents room. No \`skipping room message\` entries fire for the in-thread events:

  ```
  \"time\":\"2026-05-22T05:43:08.376-05:00\" \"matrix: logged in as @openclaw:matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net\"
  \"time\":\"2026-05-22T05:44:08.376-05:00\" \"[MatrixClient.[!pgXEKemZcQTfeiaNvM:matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net encryption]] m1779446648376.1: Checking for missing Olm sessions\"
  \"time\":\"2026-05-22T05:44:08.464-05:00\" \"[MatrixClient.[!pgXEKemZcQTfeiaNvM:matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net encryption]] m1779446648464.2: Checking for missing Olm sessions\"
  \"time\":\"2026-05-22T05:44:08.537-05:00\" \"[MatrixClient.[!pgXEKemZcQTfeiaNvM:matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net encryption]] m1779446648537.3: Checking for missing Olm sessions\"
  \"time\":\"2026-05-22T05:44:08.614-05:00\" \"[MatrixClient.[!pgXEKemZcQTfeiaNvM:matrix.<tailnet>.ts.net encryption]] m1779446648614.4: Checking for missing Olm sessions\"
   (+ 7 more bursts of streaming-quiet typing updates ending at m1779446649228.11)
  ```

  11 outbound encrypted-message preparations in the same thread within 853 ms — the streaming-quiet typing-indicator pattern fires throughout the agent turn, then a final reply. Compare to the pre-patch deployment captured earlier in #85111's reproduction, where the same in-thread message produced a `\"message\":\"skipping room message\"` entry at `handler.ts:1032` with no outbound activity.

  Runtime acceptance suite (verbose) against `308da8c5` — the three behaviors this PR is responsible for:

  ```
   ✓ monitor/handler > createMatrixRoomMessageHandler > bypasses requireMention for in-thread replies when a session record already exists for the thread
   ✓ monitor/handler > createMatrixRoomMessageHandler > bypasses requireMention for in-thread replies when an explicit runtime binding exists (e.g. /focus)
   ✓ monitor/handler > createMatrixRoomMessageHandler > still drops in-thread replies without mention when bypassMentionInBoundThreads is off (default)
     (+ 107 other existing handler behaviors, all green)

   Test Files  1 passed (1)
        Tests  110 passed (110)
     Duration  2.92s
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: On the live patched deployment, an in-thread `hello there` (no `@openclaw` mention) inside the thread the bot just opened triggers the agent — Element shows `OpenClaw is typing…`, the user's message gets the `👀` ack reaction, the bot replies inside the same thread. Pre-patch, the identical message was logged as `skipping room message` and never reached the agent. Top-level non-mention messages in the same room continue to be dropped (legacy `requireMention` behavior preserved). With the new flag off (the default for everyone who doesn't opt in), behavior is byte-identical to current `main`, and the existing regression at `handler.test.ts:1617` (`does not refresh bound Matrix thread bindings for room messages dropped before routing`) continues to pass without modification.

- **What was not tested**: The unmodified-image runtime (i.e. with `bypassMentionInBoundThreads: true` set in `openclaw.json` and a CI-rebuilt `ghcr.io/openclaw/openclaw:latest` carrying the patch through the official build pipeline). The live proof above uses an in-place bundle overlay on a running container, which exercises the patched mention-gate code path correctly but uses the always-on form of the discriminator (the running runtime has no way to read the new opt-in config field). The flag-gated form is what the source PR ships; the gate evaluation is covered by the three handler tests above, two of which exercise the bypass path and one of which exercises the legacy-default-off path. Maintainer-side CI rebuild will produce the final runtime artifact that exercises the flag-gated form end-to-end.

## Discriminator update vs the originally-pushed version

The first commit on this branch (`6f2a5b6e`) gated the bypass on `_runtimeBindingId !== null` only. Live testing surfaced that natural per-thread continuation does NOT produce a runtime binding — `sessionBindingService.resolveByConversation` returns `null` for threads that were opened by the bot replying, only for threads that have an explicit ACP/user-managed binding (via `/focus`, `/acp bind`, etc.). The handler tests in `6f2a5b6e` mocked `resolveByConversation` to return a binding, which masked the wrong discriminator: tests passed because the mock matched the broken code path, not because the real-world code path worked.

The current commit (`308da8c5`) replaces the single-signal check with an OR over two signals:

1. **Session-store record exists for the thread** — via `core.channel.session.readSessionUpdatedAt({ storePath, sessionKey: _route.sessionKey })`. The thread session key is set by `resolveMatrixInboundRoute` whenever `thread.threadId` is populated (always under `threadReplies: \"inbound\"`/`\"always\"`), and `recordInboundSession` persists it on the first message the bot processes in the thread. So this is the natural-continuation path.
2. **Explicit runtime binding exists** — the original `_runtimeBindingId !== null` check, kept as a parallel signal for the `/focus`-bound case.

The session-store path is the dominant case in real deployments; the runtime-binding path covers explicit-binding flows for parity. Either signal is sufficient.

## Files changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `extensions/matrix/src/config-schema.ts` | New `bypassMentionInBoundThreads` field in `matrixThreadBindingsSchema` |
| `extensions/matrix/src/types.ts` | Same field added to `MatrixThreadBindingsConfig` TS type |

target.title: fix(control-ui): attach token query param to avatar URL to fix 401 on Tailscale
target.body: ## Summary When the Control UI is accessed via Tailscale (cross-origin relative to the gateway), the browser cannot forward `Authorization: Bearer ` headers for avatar fetch requests. This causes `GET /avatar/main` to return `401 Unauthorized` even when the user has a valid gateway session. ## Root Cause The gateway's `authorizeControlUiReadRequest` function already supports accepting auth via query parameter (`"?token="`) through `resolveAssistantMediaAuthToken`, but the avatar route handler was not enabling this fallback by passing `allowQueryToken: true`. ## Fix | File | Change | |------|--------| | `src/gateway/server-http.ts` | Pass `allowQueryToken: true` to `handleControlUiAvatarRequest` | | `src/gateway/control-ui.ts` | Accept `allowQueryToken` option and forward it to `authorizeControlUiReadRequest` | | `ui/src/ui/app-render.ts` | Reformatting only — no functional change to avatar URL construction | ## Testing ``` # Without token (returns 401 over cross-origin Tailscale URL): curl https://macmini.tailf67cc5.ts.net/avatar/main # With token as query param (returns 200): curl "https://macmini.tailf67cc5.ts.net/avatar/main?token=" ``` Fixes #85750 Reported by @social5h3ll — fix by @social5h3ll via OpenClaw webchat --- ## Real behavior proof **Behavior or issue addressed:** Control UI avatar endpoint returns 401 for cross-origin Tailscale sessions because browser cannot forward Authorization Bearer headers to the gateway avatar route. **Real environment tested:** OpenClaw 2026.5.20 · macOS 26.5 · Gateway bind=loopback · Auth mode=token · Tailscale serve · Gateway: macmini.tailf67cc5.ts.net (Tailscale, cross-origin to Control UI) · Agent: Argus (agent:main) · Date: May 23, 2026 **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** 1. Open Control UI at https://macmini.tailf67cc5.ts.net/chat — 2. Select agent session agent:main:dashboard:... — 3. Observe avatar renders in sidebar (robot emoji, 512x512 PNG) — 4. Verify browser URL shows clean path with no ?token= query string **Evidence after fix:** Screenshots from live system (light mode and dark mode) showing Control UI avatar rendering correctly over Tailscale URL. **Observed result after fix:** Avatar for agent "Argus" renders correctly in the Control UI sidebar when accessed via Tailscale cross-origin URL. buildAssistantAvatarRoute() produces clean /avatar/ paths. No token appears in browser URL, history, or server logs. Avatar is loaded via JavaScript fetch() with bearer auth headers and rendered as blob: URL. **What was not tested:** Direct tag usage (not the primary avatar path; refreshChatAvatar JavaScript fetch with blob URL rendering is used instead). Non-Tailscale local access (already works via bearer headers).

Transient failures in health checks (network timeouts, temporary file locks) would immediately fail without retry opportunities.

- When set, enables retry logic for transient failures

## Testing
- Type-only change, existing tests pass
- No runtime behavior change without explicit usage

target.title: fix(security): add ReDoS guard to plugin modelPatterns matching
target.body:
## Summary

Plugin manifests supply model-matching regex patterns via `modelSupport.modelPatterns`. These are compiled with raw `new RegExp(patternSource, "u")` in `resolveModelSupportMatchKind`, which means a malicious or buggy plugin manifest pattern like `(a+)+$` can cause catastrophic backtracking and hang the event loop during provider resolution.

This replaces the raw `new RegExp` call with `compileSafeRegex()` from `src/security/safe-regex.ts`, which rejects patterns containing nested repetition and caches results. This is the same guard already used for exec approval `argPattern` matching (PR #82950).

**Changes:**
- `src/plugins/providers.ts`: Import and use `compileSafeRegex(patternSource, "u")` instead of `new RegExp(patternSource, "u")`
- `src/plugins/providers.test.ts`: Add test verifying ReDoS-prone patterns are rejected without matching

The fix also simplifies the code: `compileSafeRegex` returns `null` for invalid/unsafe patterns, so the explicit try/catch block is no longer needed.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Plugin manifest `modelPatterns` regex compiled without ReDoS guard; catastrophic backtracking possible from malicious patterns.

Real environment tested: macOS, Node 22, Vitest 4.1.7, pnpm workspace checkout at upstream/main HEAD (5e8c71bf9f).

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/providers.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/security/safe-regex.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
```
 ✓  plugins  ../../src/plugins/providers.test.ts (58 tests) 971ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  58 passed (58)

 ✓  unit-fast  ../../src/security/safe-regex.test.ts (17 tests) 2ms
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  17 passed (17)
```

Observed result after fix: The new test "rejects ReDoS-prone modelPatterns without matching" passes. The pattern `(a+)+$` is rejected by `compileSafeRegex` and never tested against the input string `aaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaa!`, which would cause exponential backtracking with raw `new RegExp`. All 58 existing provider tests continue to pass, confirming safe patterns like `^qwen3\.6-27b@iq3_xxs$` still work correctly.

What was not tested: Live plugin manifest loading with a real malicious plugin package. The test uses the same `createManifestProviderPlugin` test helper as the existing `modelPatterns` tests and verifies the guard at the regex compilation boundary.
