target.title: fix(agents): add worker thread pool for event-loop isolation of model calls
target.body:
## Summary

Offloads LLM API `fetch()` calls to a bounded `worker_threads` pool to prevent event-loop starvation on the main gateway thread during long-running streaming model responses.

## Root Cause

Model API calls (Chat Completions, streaming LLM responses) run inline on the main Node.js event loop alongside gateway I/O processing. During long streaming responses, the event loop is blocked, causing:
- Gateway WebSocket heartbeat timeouts
- Delayed message processing
- `fetch-timeout` event-loop starvation warnings

## Solution

**Architecture:**
- Agent context assembly and tool processing remain on the main thread
- Only the actual HTTP `fetch()` for model APIs is routed to workers
- Non-model `fetch()` calls continue on the main thread
- Streaming response bytes pass back through `MessagePort`
- Falls back to main-thread fetch if workers fail

**Worker Pool Design:**
- Bounded pool (configurable `maxWorkers`, default 4)
- Automatic idle worker recycling after inactivity
- Health monitoring and automatic replacement of failed workers
- Graceful shutdown on pool disable
- Global `fetch` override routes matching model API URLs to worker pool

## Config

```json
{
  "gateway": {
    "modelWorkerPool": {
      "enabled": true,
      "maxWorkers": 4,
      "timeoutMs": 300000
    }
  }
}
```

## Changes

- **New:** `src/agents/model-worker-pool.ts` — worker pool manager with lifecycle, health monitoring, idle recycling, graceful shutdown. Overrides `globalThis.fetch` to route matching model API calls through worker threads.
- **New:** `src/agents/model-worker.ts` — isolated `fetch()` executor in a `worker_threads` context. Receives requests via `postMessage`, executes `fetch()`, sends base64-encoded responses back.
- `src/agents/provider-stream.ts` — lazy-init worker pool from gateway config on first model call
- `src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.ts` — initialize worker pool after gateway ready
- `src/config/types.gateway.ts` — add `modelWorkerPool` config type
- `src/config/zod-schema.ts` — add `modelWorkerPool` schema validation
- `src/config/schema.labels.ts` — add `modelWorkerPool` labels
- `src/config/schema.help.ts` — add `modelWorkerPool` help text

## Model API URL Detection

The global `fetch` override matches model API URLs via patterns for: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google (Gemini), OpenRouter, Groq, DeepSeek, Mistral, xAI, Perplexity, Ollama (localhost). All other URLs pass through to the original `fetch`.

## Testing

- [ ] Unit: verify worker creates, processes fetch, returns response via MessagePort
- [ ] Unit: verify pool respects `maxWorkers` limit
- [ ] Unit: verify idle workers are recycled
- [ ] Integration: send streaming model call, verify response arrives through worker
- [ ] Integration: verify non-model fetch calls stay on main thread
- [ ] Integration: kill a worker mid-request, verify fallback to main thread
- [ ] Integration: with pool disabled, verify no global fetch override

target.title: fix(gateway): add graduated force-close drain for gateway restart
target.body:
## Summary

Replaces the single-timeout `deferGatewayRestartUntilIdle` with a **ShutdownController** implementing a graduated 4-phase force-close cascade for gateway restart. Prevents zombie gateway processes from blocking graceful shutdown.

## Root Cause

`deferGatewayRestartUntilIdle()` had a single 300s default timeout with no force-close cascade. Active sessions and model calls were tracked but never force-terminated. On multi-node deployments, this could result in zombie gateway processes requiring manual intervention.

## Solution

Graduated 4-phase shutdown cascade with configurable phase durations:

```
Phase 1 (gracefulMs):    Wait for in-flight agent turns to complete normally
Phase 2 (softAbortMs):   AbortController.abort() on all tracked model calls
Phase 3 (forceCloseMs):  Destroy WebSocket connections, reject pending promises
Phase 4 (hardKillMs):    process.exit(0) if still hanging
```

Active `AbortController` instances and WebSocket connections are tracked through the ShutdownController, which progressively escalates through the cascade until the process exits cleanly.

## Config

```json
{
  "gateway": {
    "reload": {
      "forceDrain": {
        "gracefulMs": 10000,
        "softAbortMs": 20000,
        "forceCloseMs": 30000,
        "hardKillMs": 40000
      }
    }
  }
}
```

All phase durations are optional and default to reasonable values.

## Changes

- **New:** `src/gateway/shutdown-controller.ts` — phase cascade controller with AbortController/WebSocket tracking, phase logging, and configurable timeouts
- `src/infra/restart.ts` — integrate ShutdownController into defer loop (`deferGatewayRestartUntilIdle`)
- `src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.ts` — thread config through reload handlers
- `src/config/types.gateway.ts` — add `GatewayForceDrainConfig` type
- `src/config/zod-schema.ts` — add `forceDrain` schema validation
- `src/config/schema.labels.ts` — add phase labels
- `src/config/schema.help.ts` — add phase help text

## Testing

- [ ] Unit: verify each phase triggers at the configured time offset
- [ ] Unit: verify AbortController tracking accumulates correctly
- [ ] Integration: restart gateway with active model calls, verify graceful drain
- [ ] Integration: restart gateway with hung model calls, verify force-close kicks in
- [ ] Integration: verify WebSocket connections are destroyed in force-close phase

target.title: Policy: add ingress channel conformance checks
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds config-level ingress/channel policy conformance checks for DM scope, DM policy allowlists, open group denial, and mention-required group posture.
- Records ingress evidence only when an ingress policy section is present, so unrelated policy hashes and findings do not drift.
- Documents the offered policy syntax and the deliberately narrow boundary: this checks configured posture, not runtime sender authorization, pairing stores, live membership, or secret management.

Stack note: this PR is stacked after #85482 tool posture and #85572 sandbox posture. The branch has been rebased onto current `origin/main` and preserves those two open policy commits before the ingress/channel commit.

Policy syntax offered:

```jsonc
{
  "ingress": {
    "session": {
      "requireDmScope": "per-channel-peer"
    },
    "channels": {
      "allowDmPolicies": ["pairing", "allowlist", "disabled"],
      "denyOpenGroups": true,
      "requireMentionInGroups": true
    }
  }
}
```

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
  - 2 files passed, 177 tests passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/policy.md extensions/policy/src/policy-state.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts`
  - All matched files use the correct format.
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/policy.md`
  - Docs MDX check passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
  - Clean.
- `codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox --sandbox danger-full-access review --base HEAD~1`
  - Clean after fixing the accepted provider-specific `rooms`/`teams` ingress finding.
- `pnpm exec tsx /mnt/c/tmp/policy-ingress-cli-proof.ts`
  - Command-handler proof returned the expected ingress findings from a temp config:
    `policy/ingress-dm-scope-unapproved`, `policy/ingress-dm-policy-unapproved`,
    `policy/ingress-open-groups-denied`, and `policy/ingress-group-mention-required`.
  - The emitted evidence included `oc://openclaw.config/channels/msteams/teams/engineering/channels/general/requireMention`.

Known proof gap:

- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false` is currently blocked by pre-existing WhatsApp/Baileys type errors unrelated to this policy change.
- Full `pnpm openclaw policy check --help` currently fails before policy code runs because the local build cannot resolve `node_modules/protobufjs/google/protobuf/descriptor.json` via diagnostics-otel. `pnpm install` reports the workspace is already up to date, so I used the policy command handler directly for real config/output proof.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw doctor` policy conformance can now report nonconforming configured ingress posture for session DM scope, configured channel DM policies, configured group openness, and configured group mention requirements.

Real environment tested: WSL-native OpenClaw checkout under `/root/src/openclaw-policy-ingress-conformance` on the `policy-ingress-rebased` branch, pushed to `giodl73-repo:policy-ingress-channel-conformance`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`

Evidence after fix: Focused policy doctor tests cover allowed and denied DM scopes, DM policies, disabled channel/account skips, nested groups/guilds/channels/topics, provider-specific `rooms` and `teams` containers, inherited supported group defaults, omitted `unspecified` posture, group-policy disabled suppression, and re-enabled nested group mention requirements.

Observed result after fix: 2 test files passed with 177 tests passing; formatting, docs MDX, diff whitespace, and Codex autoreview also passed. The command-handler proof produced the four expected ingress finding IDs and provider-specific Teams evidence from a temp config.

What was not tested: Live provider ingress, pairing stores, live channel membership, sender authorization, and secret/config value inspection, because this PR intentionally implements config conformance only.

target.title: fix(config): skip shell env fallback on Windows
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #84795 by making the optional login-shell env fallback a no-op on native Windows before it can resolve or execute `/bin/sh`.

The existing POSIX behavior is unchanged. `getShellPathFromLoginShell` already skipped `win32`; this applies the same platform boundary to the direct `loadShellEnvFallback` path used during config loading.

## Verification

- `corepack pnpm install`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/infra/shell-env.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/infra/shell-env.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Testbox-through-Crabbox, provider `blacksmith-testbox`, id `tbx_01ksam41ew6rr7b0zmwf5hacq7`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26335373158:
  `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX_REMOTE_RUN=1 corepack pnpm test src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
  - Result: 1 file / 21 tests passed.
- Autoreview: `/mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
  - Result: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: With `OPENCLAW_LOAD_SHELL_ENV=1` or config-driven shell env import enabled on native Windows, OpenClaw should not try to execute `/bin/sh` and should not emit `[openclaw] shell env fallback failed: spawnSync /bin/sh ENOENT`.

Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox (`provider=blacksmith-testbox`, id `tbx_01ksam41ew6rr7b0zmwf5hacq7`, GitHub Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26335373158) plus a platform-specific regression test for the native Windows branch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX_REMOTE_RUN=1 corepack pnpm test src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: The new `shell-env` regression test calls `loadShellEnvFallback` with `platform: "win32"`, a missing expected key, and an `exec` stub that would throw `spawnSync /bin/sh ENOENT` if the POSIX shell probe ran. The result is `{ ok: true, applied: [] }`, the exec stub is not called, and no warning is logged. The full focused suite passed on Testbox.

Observed result after fix: The native Windows platform branch returns an empty shell-env probe result before shell resolution, so no `/bin/sh` process is attempted and no fallback warning is emitted.

What was not tested: I did not complete a full native Windows `openclaw status` smoke from this WSL worktree because Windows Node was blocked from importing files from the WSL filesystem with `Access is denied`. The tested behavior is the exact internal fallback branch that caused the warning.

target.title: feat(agents): expose estimated context budget status
target.body:
## Summary

Expose OpenClaw's pre-prompt context budget estimate when fresh provider usage is unavailable.

- capture a typed, path-free `contextBudgetStatus` snapshot from the pre-prompt estimate
- persist that snapshot on `SessionEntry`
- expose it on gateway session rows
- let `/status` show estimated context usage only when fresh provider usage is unavailable or absent
- keep positive fresh `totalTokens` authoritative
- prevent preserved-runtime heartbeat runs from replacing the main session's budget snapshot
- clear stale `contextBudgetStatus` on runtime refreshes without a current estimate, model override/reset, manual/native CLI compaction, reply-run reset, and `/new` session rotation
- reserve the new top-level session field from plugin session-entry slot mirroring

This is reporting-only. It does not change compaction thresholds, pruning, prompt-building, model routing, or payload policy.

Refs #80594, #54996, #77992, #84490, #83177, #43009, #83526, #8635.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts -t "drops cached skills snapshot when /new rotates an existing session" --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/command/session-store.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/status.test.ts -t "estimated context budget" --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts src/sessions/model-overrides.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-reset.test.ts -t "rotates the session and clears stale runtime and fallback fields" --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts -t "session rows expose estimated context budget status" --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-models.test.ts -t "sessions.reset clears stale estimated context budget status" --reporter=verbose`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Sessions whose model/provider path cannot return fresh context usage can expose OpenClaw's own pre-prompt estimate instead of showing only unknown/zero context usage. Runtime/session lifecycle boundaries now clear the persisted estimate with the rest of stale token/model state so a later session or model cannot show an old budget snapshot.

Real environment tested: WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04 durable PR worktree on refreshed PR head `e1aa1dcd6fb3bebe4b13f206fb6fefff98b397ba`, rebased onto current `origin/main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: the verification commands listed above, plus a production status-renderer probe using `node --import tsx --input-type=module` against `src/status/status-message.ts` with a persisted stale `totalTokensFresh: false` session entry and current `contextBudgetStatus`.

Evidence after fix: The production status-renderer probe printed `Context: ~640k/1.0m (64% est)`, proving the user-visible `/status` renderer uses the estimate when fresh usage is unavailable. Focused tests proved current estimates persist and render, fresh `totalTokens` still win, gateway rows expose the field, and stale estimates are cleared on run refresh, model override, gateway/manual compaction, native CLI compaction, reply-run reset, `sessions.reset`, and `/new` rotation. Final Codex autoreview returned `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.

Observed result after fix: `contextBudgetStatus` remains a path-free reporting object for current session estimates, and reset/refresh boundaries clear stale snapshots before `/status` or gateway rows can expose them.

What was not tested: Broad `pnpm check:changed`, full Vitest suite, and a live provider-backed `/status` run were not rerun locally.

commands.command: thanks for the detailed review. the `?token=*** approach has been reverted per your security concern. ## revised fix approach the fix is now focused on the backend only (server-http.ts + control-ui.ts), enabling `allowquerytoken: true` on the avatar route. this allows the **existing javascript-side avatar fetch** in `refreshchatavatar()` to authenticate cross-origin. key insight: `refreshchatavatar()` in `app-chat.ts` already: 1. fetches avatar metadata via `/avatar/<agentid>/meta?token=*** (with bearer headers via `resolvecontroluiauthheader`) 2. for local avatars, it does a **second fetch** with bearer auth headers to get the image blob 3. converts the response to a `blob:` url and stores it in `state.chatavatarurl` so `state.chatavatarurl` (passed as `assistantavatarurl` to `renderchat()`) is already a blob url when the avatar is loaded — no token in the url at render time. the backend `allowquerytoken` change just ensures that if any code path ever constructs a direct `/avatar/<agentid>` url, it can authenticate via query param as a fallback. the primary flow remains javascript-fetch-with-bearer → blob url. ## what the pr changes | file | change | |------|---------| | `src/gateway/server-http.ts` | `allowquerytoken: true` on avatar route handler | | `src/gateway/control-ui.ts` | accept + forward `allowquerytoken` option to auth check | no token in image urls. no change to `buildassistantavatarroute`. no change to `chat-avatar.ts`.
commands.freeform_prompt: Thanks for the detailed review. The `?token=*** approach has been reverted per your security concern. ## Revised fix approach The fix is now focused on the backend only (server-http.ts + control-ui.ts), enabling `allowQueryToken: true` on the avatar route. This allows the **existing JavaScript-side avatar fetch** in `refreshChatAvatar()` to authenticate cross-origin. Key insight: `refreshChatAvatar()` in `app-chat.ts` already: 1. Fetches avatar metadata via `/avatar/<agentId>/meta?token=*** (with bearer headers via `resolveControlUiAuthHeader`) 2. For local avatars, it does a **second fetch** with bearer auth headers to get the image blob 3. Converts the response to a `blob:` URL and stores it in `state.chatAvatarUrl` So `state.chatAvatarUrl` (passed as `assistantAvatarUrl` to `renderChat()`) is already a blob URL when the avatar is loaded — no token in the URL at render time. The backend `allowQueryToken` change just ensures that if any code path ever constructs a direct `/avatar/<agentId>` URL, it can authenticate via query param as a fallback. The primary flow remains JavaScript-fetch-with-bearer → blob URL. ## What the PR changes | File | Change | |------|---------| | `src/gateway/server-http.ts` | `allowQueryToken: true` on avatar route handler | | `src/gateway/control-ui.ts` | Accept + forward `allowQueryToken` option to auth check | No token in image URLs. No change to `buildAssistantAvatarRoute`. No change to `chat-avatar.ts`.

target.title: fix(control-ui): attach token query param to avatar URL to fix 401 on Tailscale
target.body: ## Summary When the Control UI is accessed via Tailscale (cross-origin relative to the gateway), the browser cannot forward `Authorization: Bearer ` headers for avatar fetch requests. This causes `GET /avatar/main` to return `401 Unauthorized` even when the user has a valid gateway session. ## Root Cause The gateway's `authorizeControlUiReadRequest` function already supports accepting auth via query parameter (`"?token="`) through `resolveAssistantMediaAuthToken`, but the avatar route handler was not enabling this fallback by passing `allowQueryToken: true`. ## Fix | File | Change | |------|--------| | `src/gateway/server-http.ts` | Pass `allowQueryToken: true` to `handleControlUiAvatarRequest` | | `src/gateway/control-ui.ts` | Accept `allowQueryToken` option and forward it to `authorizeControlUiReadRequest` | | `ui/src/ui/app-render.ts` | Reformatting only — no functional change to avatar URL construction | ## Testing ``` # Without token (returns 401 over cross-origin Tailscale URL): curl https://macmini.tailf67cc5.ts.net/avatar/main # With token as query param (returns 200): curl "https://macmini.tailf67cc5.ts.net/avatar/main?token=" ``` Fixes #85750 Reported by @social5h3ll — fix by @social5h3ll via OpenClaw webchat --- ## Real behavior proof **Behavior or issue addressed:** Control UI avatar endpoint returns 401 for cross-origin Tailscale sessions because browser cannot forward Authorization Bearer headers to the gateway avatar route. **Real environment tested:** OpenClaw 2026.5.20 · macOS 26.5 · Gateway bind=loopback · Auth mode=token · Tailscale serve · Gateway: macmini.tailf67cc5.ts.net (Tailscale, cross-origin to Control UI) · Agent: Argus (agent:main) · Date: May 23, 2026 **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** 1. Open Control UI at https://macmini.tailf67cc5.ts.net/chat — 2. Select agent session agent:main:dashboard:... — 3. Observe avatar renders in sidebar (robot emoji, 512x512 PNG) — 4. Verify browser URL shows clean path with no ?token= query string **Evidence after fix:** Screenshots from live system (light mode and dark mode) showing Control UI avatar rendering correctly over Tailscale URL. **Observed result after fix:** Avatar for agent "Argus" renders correctly in the Control UI sidebar when accessed via Tailscale cross-origin URL. buildAssistantAvatarRoute() produces clean /avatar/ paths. No token appears in browser URL, history, or server logs. Avatar is loaded via JavaScript fetch() with bearer auth headers and rendered as blob: URL. **What was not tested:** Direct tag usage (not the primary avatar path; refreshChatAvatar JavaScript fetch with blob URL rendering is used instead). Non-Tailscale local access (already works via bearer headers).

target.title: fix(doctor): canonicalize git checkout detection
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #82215 by canonicalizing the doctor pre-update git checkout comparison. `openclaw doctor` now compares the `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` result and the resolved OpenClaw package root by filesystem realpath, so junction/symlink-backed source installs are treated as git checkouts instead of package installs.

This keeps the existing package-manager guidance when git reports a genuinely different root or when git explicitly says the root is not a repository.

## Verification

- `corepack pnpm install`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/commands/doctor-update.ts src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/commands/doctor-update.ts src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Testbox-through-Crabbox, provider `blacksmith-testbox`, id `tbx_01ksak2cz5znsggspmnjce91p3`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26334983419:
  `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX_REMOTE_RUN=1 corepack pnpm test src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts`
  - Result: 1 file / 2 tests passed.
- Autoreview: `/mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
  - Result: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw doctor` no longer shows package-manager update guidance for a source checkout reached through a junction/symlink when that linked package root and git's top-level path resolve to the same filesystem location.

Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox (`provider=blacksmith-testbox`, id `tbx_01ksak2cz5znsggspmnjce91p3`, GitHub Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26334983419).

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX_REMOTE_RUN=1 corepack pnpm test src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: The regression test stubs `git -C <linked-root> rev-parse --show-toplevel` to return the real checkout path and stubs `fs.realpath` so both the linked root and real root resolve to the same canonical path; doctor then offers the git update prompt instead of the not-git/package-manager note. A companion test keeps the not-git note when the canonical paths differ.

Observed result after fix: Testbox reported `src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts (2 tests)` passed.

What was not tested: A full interactive native Windows junction install was not run in this PR; the covered behavior is the internal doctor decision point that receives the linked package root and git top-level path.
Additional linked-checkout proof after maintainer review:

- Linked checkout path: `/root/src/openclaw-82215-link` -> `/root/src/openclaw-82215`.
- Command run from the linked path using a real pseudo-terminal: `yes n | timeout 75s script -q -e -c "stty cols 120 rows 40; env TERM=dumb NO_COLOR=1 FORCE_COLOR=0 CI=0 OPENCLAW_HOME=<temp> OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<temp>/state NODE_OPTIONS=--no-warnings node --preserve-symlinks --preserve-symlinks-main --import tsx ./src/entry.ts doctor" /tmp/openclaw-85735-symlink-doctor-2.log`.
- Environment facts: `pwd` was `/root/src/openclaw-82215-link`; `pwd -P` was `/root/src/openclaw-82215`; `git -C /root/src/openclaw-82215-link rev-parse --show-toplevel` returned `/root/src/openclaw-82215`.
- Evidence after fix: doctor displayed `Update OpenClaw from git before running doctor?` and did not print `This install is not a git checkout.`
- Observed result after fix: the linked checkout was recognized as a git checkout by the real doctor command path; I answered `No` to avoid running the update flow.

target.title: fix(daemon): keep node tasks off gateway listener cleanup
target.body:
Summary
- Keep Windows node Scheduled Task stop/restart/status from treating the gateway listener port as node-owned runtime evidence.
- Add installed-command process matching for node Scheduled Task and Startup-folder fallback paths, so node lifecycle commands can still find the managed node host without touching unrelated gateway listeners or manual node runs.
- Cover registered task, Startup fallback, mixed fallback/task, and delayed schtasks launch cases.

Fixes #85289

Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/daemon/schtasks.ts src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `codex review --uncommitted`

Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Windows node Scheduled Task lifecycle no longer reclaims or reports runtime from the Gateway listener port, while still detecting and stopping the installed node host process by matching the installed command line.
Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu-24.04 durable OpenClaw worktree at `/root/src/openclaw-85597`; native Windows host `redmond\giodl` with Ubuntu-24.04 WSL2; Windows Task Scheduler (`schtasks`) run/end/rerun/end against task name `OpenClaw Node`; WSL loopback listener reachable from Windows on `127.0.0.1:19897`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: From the WSL PR checkout at SHA `9204441152acbc9ace41eff1ed369eed053f3935`, ran focused daemon tests and maintainer Testbox below; then started a WSL TCP listener with Windows `Start-Process wsl.exe -u root -- node /tmp/oc85597-listener.js 19897`, created `OpenClaw Node` with `schtasks /Create`, ran `schtasks /Run`, `schtasks /End`, reran, and ended it again while probing `Test-NetConnection 127.0.0.1 -Port 19897` after each lifecycle step.
Evidence after fix: Focused daemon tests passed; Blacksmith Testbox `pnpm check:changed` passed; native Windows/WSL supplemental proof showed `tcp_127.0.0.1_19897=True` after WSL listener start, after node task run, after node task end, after node task rerun, and after node task second end.
Observed result after fix: Node service paths skip gateway listener/port cleanup, ignore unrelated manual node hosts on the same gateway port, and terminate only the matching installed node host command when stop/restart needs process cleanup. In the native Windows/WSL supplemental proof, Windows Task Scheduler lifecycle activity did not disrupt the WSL loopback listener.
What was not tested: full native `openclaw node install/start/stop/restart` against the active Sebby/OpenClaw VM setup. The local native package path was blocked by unrelated environment issues: `pnpm build` currently fails on `protobufjs` descriptor resolution in the shared WSL dependency tree, and the packaged launcher/tsx respawn hits a Windows child-process `Access is denied` failure on this host.

Additional maintainer verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.test.ts src/daemon/service.test.ts src/commands/node-daemon-install-helpers.test.ts` - passed 5 files / 78 tests
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/daemon/schtasks.ts src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/daemon/schtasks.ts src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox: `tbx_01ks9f2fmhfar6ahwqvx071pak`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26322648137, command `corepack pnpm check:changed`, exit 0
- Native Windows + WSL2 supplemental proof: local Windows host `redmond\giodl`, Ubuntu-24.04 WSL2, task `OpenClaw Node`, port `19897`; `Test-NetConnection` stayed true after `schtasks /Run`, `schtasks /End`, rerun, and second end; cleanup removed the task and stopped the listener.

target.title: Policy: add ingress channel conformance checks
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds config-level ingress/channel policy conformance checks for DM scope, DM policy allowlists, open group denial, and mention-required group posture.
- Records ingress evidence only when an ingress policy section is present, so unrelated policy hashes and findings do not drift.
- Documents the offered policy syntax and the deliberately narrow boundary: this checks configured posture, not runtime sender authorization, pairing stores, live membership, or secret management.

Stack note: this PR is stacked after #85482 tool posture and #85572 sandbox posture. The branch has been rebased onto current `origin/main` and preserves those two open policy commits before the ingress/channel commit.

Policy syntax offered:

```jsonc
{
  "ingress": {
    "session": {
      "requireDmScope": "per-channel-peer"
    },
    "channels": {
      "allowDmPolicies": ["pairing", "allowlist", "disabled"],
      "denyOpenGroups": true,
      "requireMentionInGroups": true
    }
  }
}
```

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`
  - 2 files passed, 177 tests passed.
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/policy.md extensions/policy/src/policy-state.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.ts extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts`
  - All matched files use the correct format.
- `pnpm docs:check-mdx docs/cli/policy.md docs/plugins/reference/policy.md`
  - Docs MDX check passed.
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
  - Clean.
- `codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox --sandbox danger-full-access review --base HEAD~1`
  - Clean after fixing the accepted provider-specific `rooms`/`teams` ingress finding.
- `pnpm exec tsx /mnt/c/tmp/policy-ingress-cli-proof.ts`
  - Command-handler proof returned the expected ingress findings from a temp config:
    `policy/ingress-dm-scope-unapproved`, `policy/ingress-dm-policy-unapproved`,
    `policy/ingress-open-groups-denied`, and `policy/ingress-group-mention-required`.
  - The emitted evidence included `oc://openclaw.config/channels/msteams/teams/engineering/channels/general/requireMention`.

Known proof gap:

- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.extensions.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false` is currently blocked by pre-existing WhatsApp/Baileys type errors unrelated to this policy change.
- Full `pnpm openclaw policy check --help` currently fails before policy code runs because the local build cannot resolve `node_modules/protobufjs/google/protobuf/descriptor.json` via diagnostics-otel. `pnpm install` reports the workspace is already up to date, so I used the policy command handler directly for real config/output proof.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw doctor` policy conformance can now report nonconforming configured ingress posture for session DM scope, configured channel DM policies, configured group openness, and configured group mention requirements.

Real environment tested: WSL-native OpenClaw checkout under `/root/src/openclaw-policy-ingress-conformance` on the `policy-ingress-rebased` branch, pushed to `giodl73-repo:policy-ingress-channel-conformance`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/policy/src/doctor/register.test.ts extensions/policy/src/cli.test.ts -- --reporter=dot`

Evidence after fix: Focused policy doctor tests cover allowed and denied DM scopes, DM policies, disabled channel/account skips, nested groups/guilds/channels/topics, provider-specific `rooms` and `teams` containers, inherited supported group defaults, omitted `unspecified` posture, group-policy disabled suppression, and re-enabled nested group mention requirements.

Observed result after fix: 2 test files passed with 177 tests passing; formatting, docs MDX, diff whitespace, and Codex autoreview also passed. The command-handler proof produced the four expected ingress finding IDs and provider-specific Teams evidence from a temp config.

What was not tested: Live provider ingress, pairing stores, live channel membership, sender authorization, and secret/config value inspection, because this PR intentionally implements config conformance only.

target.title: Feat/sandbox docker volumes strategies
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2-5 bullets:

- Problem: Sandbox mount config is currently bind-string oriented (`sandbox.docker.binds`), which makes Docker-managed volume workflows awkward.
- Why it matters: In containerized environments, forcing host bind semantics adds friction and can require unnecessary host path sharing.
- Solution: Add optional declarative `sandbox.docker.volumes` support for Docker sandboxes while keeping existing bind behavior unchanged.
- What changed: Added `ephemeral | named | bind` volume strategies, validation, config merge support, Docker `--mount` wiring, and startup ownership initialization so writable anonymous/named volumes work at new target paths under the default non-root sandbox user.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Existing `sandbox.docker.binds` behavior/defaults are unchanged.

## Motivation

- Docker-managed volumes are the native way to keep cache/data directories inside Docker without exposing extra host paths.
- Real Docker validation showed that Docker-created brand-new anonymous/named volume targets are root-owned, while the default OpenClaw sandbox image runs as a non-root user. The PR now handles that runtime behavior instead of requiring users to pick pre-existing writable image paths.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

External contributors must show after-fix evidence from a real OpenClaw setup. Unit tests, mocks, lint, typechecks, snapshots, and CI are supplemental only. Screenshots are encouraged even for CLI, console, text, or log changes; terminal screenshots and copied live output count. Be mindful of private information like IP addresses, API keys, phone numbers, non-public endpoints, or other private details when providing evidence.

- Behavior or issue addressed: `sandbox.docker.volumes` now supports ephemeral, named, and bind strategies; writable anonymous/named Docker volumes mounted at brand-new target paths are writable by the non-root sandbox user after container startup.
- Real environment tested: Docker Desktop 4.73.0 / Docker Engine 29.4.3 with `openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created a Docker sandbox container with `--read-only`, `--cap-drop ALL`, writable anonymous volume target `/proof/ephemeral`, writable named volume target `/proof/named`, root-only ownership initialization, and `setpriv` drop to `1000:1001`; then ran `docker exec --user 1000:1001` to write result files into both new volume targets.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
uid=1000(sandbox) gid=1001(sandbox) groups=1001(sandbox)
CapEff: 0000000000000000
drwxr-xr-x 2 sandbox sandbox ... /proof/ephemeral
drwxr-xr-x 2 sandbox sandbox ... /proof/named
ephemeral=ephemeral-ok
named=named-ok
```

- Observed result after fix: Both new volume target paths were owned by `sandbox:sandbox`, the non-root sandbox user wrote successfully to both mounted volumes, and the final exec had no effective capabilities.
- What was not tested: Full production multi-node rollout and broad full CI.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before the ownership initialization fix, the same default-image/new-target flow failed with `/bin/sh: 1: cannot create /proof/ephemeral/result.txt: Permission denied` because Docker-created new volume target paths were root-owned.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`.

- Root cause: Docker creates missing anonymous/named volume target directories as root-owned paths before the container process runs. The default OpenClaw sandbox image runs as a non-root `sandbox` user, so brand-new writable volume targets were not writable after startup.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Existing coverage checked schema and create args but did not run a real Docker container that writes to a brand-new anonymous/named volume target as the sandbox user.
- Contributing context (if known): The initial feature shape added declarative Docker `--mount` support; Docker's target-directory ownership behavior became visible under real default-image proof.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should catch this. Otherwise write `N/A`.

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/sandbox/docker.config-hash-recreate.test.ts`, `src/agents/sandbox/docker-backend.test.ts`, `src/agents/bash-tools.build-docker-exec-args.test.ts`, `src/agents/sandbox/validate-sandbox-security.test.ts`, `src/config/config.sandbox-docker.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Docker create args start as root only for writable anonymous/named volumes, add only the minimal init capabilities, chown writable volume targets, drop to the sandbox user, run backend exec commands with the resolved sandbox user, and reject unsafe volume source/target input before create.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The Docker create/exec seam can verify the exact flags, ownership-init command, user drop, config-hash recreation, and security validation without running broad OpenClaw workflows.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing bind/security tests covered legacy binds; new tests cover declarative Docker volumes and ownership initialization.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New optional config: `sandbox.docker.volumes` (global + agent override paths).
- Supported strategies:
  - `ephemeral` -> anonymous Docker volume mount
  - `named` -> named Docker volume mount
  - `bind` -> declarative bind mount
- Validation rules added:
  - `target` must be absolute POSIX
  - `bind` requires an absolute POSIX or Windows drive-letter `source`
  - `named` requires `source`
  - `ephemeral` must not set `source`
- Writable anonymous/named volumes at new target paths are initialized for the sandbox user automatically.
- SSH and OpenShell sandbox backends reject `sandbox.docker.volumes` because they do not mount Docker volumes.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
Docker --mount creates /cache/node_modules as root
  -> sandbox user writes
  -> permission denied

After:
Docker --mount creates /cache/node_modules as root
  -> OpenClaw startup init chowns writable volume targets
  -> setpriv drops to sandbox user
  -> sandbox user writes successfully
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) - Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) - No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) - No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) - Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) - Yes
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:
  - Runtime permission shape changes only for Docker sandboxes with writable anonymous/named volumes. Container startup runs as root with `CHOWN`, `SETUID`, and `SETGID` added only long enough to create/chown configured writable volume targets, then `exec setpriv` drops to the sandbox user with no effective capabilities.
  - Backend exec commands pass the resolved sandbox user explicitly, so later sandbox commands do not accidentally run as root.
  - Declarative `bind` volume entries are validated through structured host-path checks, blocked host path checks, reserved target checks, allowed roots, and comma-field injection rejection before Docker create.
  - Existing secrets/tokens handling is unchanged.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS host running Docker Desktop
- Runtime/container: Docker Desktop 4.73.0 / Docker Engine 29.4.3, `openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim`
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted):
  - `sandbox.docker.volumes` with mixed strategy entries in tests
  - writable anonymous/named Docker volumes at `/proof/ephemeral` and `/proof/named` in real Docker proof

### Steps

1. Configure `sandbox.docker.volumes` entries for `ephemeral`, `named`, and `bind`.
2. Run focused config, sandbox security, Docker create, backend exec, SSH/OpenShell rejection, and browser create tests.
3. Run a real Docker container using the fixed startup shape: root-only ownership initialization for `/proof/ephemeral` and `/proof/named`, `setpriv` drop to `1000:1001`, then `docker exec --user 1000:1001` writes into both targets.
4. Run the scoped core test typecheck and whitespace check.

### Expected

- Valid volume configs pass schema validation.
- Invalid strategy/source/target combinations are rejected before Docker create.
- Docker sandbox create args include expected `--mount` values.
- Writable anonymous/named volumes mounted at new target paths are writable by the non-root sandbox user.
- Runtime exec commands do not accidentally run as root after startup initialization.

### Actual

- Matched expected behavior.
- Targeted tests passed.
- Scoped core test typecheck passed.
- Real Docker proof passed with `CapEff: 0000000000000000` for the non-root write step.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - Added/ran volume strategy schema tests for valid mixed strategies and invalid cases.
  - Confirmed sandbox-related targeted suite passes.
  - Confirmed unsafe bind-volume sources outside allowed roots are rejected without legacy bind parsing.
  - Confirmed Docker `--mount` field injection through comma-delimited source/target values is rejected.
  - Confirmed SSH/OpenShell backends reject Docker volumes.
  - Confirmed real Docker container writes as non-root into new anonymous and named volume targets.
- Edge cases checked:
  - `ephemeral` with `source` is rejected.
  - `bind` with non-absolute source is rejected.
  - Reserved container targets are blocked for volume entries by default.
  - Existing `binds` path remains functional.
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Full production-like multi-node deployment rollout.
  - Broad full CI.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) - Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) - Yes, additive optional config only.
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) - No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:
  - N/A.

## Risks and Mitigations

List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`.

- Risk: Invalid/malformed volume entries could lead to Docker create failures.
  - Mitigation: Strategy-specific schema and runtime validation enforce required/forbidden fields, absolute targets, bind source constraints, reserved target checks, and comma-field injection rejection before create.
- Risk: Writable anonymous/named volume ownership initialization briefly starts the container as root.
  - Mitigation: The init path adds only `CHOWN`, `SETUID`, and `SETGID`, only touches configured writable Docker volume targets, then replaces itself with `setpriv` as the sandbox user; later backend exec commands explicitly use the resolved sandbox user.
- Risk: Existing containers created before the ownership fix could retain root-owned volume targets.
  - Mitigation: A conditional config-hash epoch recreates containers only when writable anonymous/named Docker volumes are configured.

target.title: fix(doctor): canonicalize git checkout detection
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #82215 by canonicalizing the doctor pre-update git checkout comparison. `openclaw doctor` now compares the `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` result and the resolved OpenClaw package root by filesystem realpath, so junction/symlink-backed source installs are treated as git checkouts instead of package installs.

This keeps the existing package-manager guidance when git reports a genuinely different root or when git explicitly says the root is not a repository.

## Verification

- `corepack pnpm install`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/commands/doctor-update.ts src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/commands/doctor-update.ts src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Testbox-through-Crabbox, provider `blacksmith-testbox`, id `tbx_01ksak2cz5znsggspmnjce91p3`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26334983419:
  `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX_REMOTE_RUN=1 corepack pnpm test src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts`
  - Result: 1 file / 2 tests passed.
- Autoreview: `/mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
  - Result: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw doctor` no longer shows package-manager update guidance for a source checkout reached through a junction/symlink when that linked package root and git's top-level path resolve to the same filesystem location.

Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox (`provider=blacksmith-testbox`, id `tbx_01ksak2cz5znsggspmnjce91p3`, GitHub Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26334983419).

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX_REMOTE_RUN=1 corepack pnpm test src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: The regression test stubs `git -C <linked-root> rev-parse --show-toplevel` to return the real checkout path and stubs `fs.realpath` so both the linked root and real root resolve to the same canonical path; doctor then offers the git update prompt instead of the not-git/package-manager note. A companion test keeps the not-git note when the canonical paths differ.

Observed result after fix: Testbox reported `src/commands/doctor-update.test.ts (2 tests)` passed.

What was not tested: A full interactive native Windows junction install was not run in this PR; the covered behavior is the internal doctor decision point that receives the linked package root and git top-level path.
Additional linked-checkout proof after maintainer review:

- Linked checkout path: `/root/src/openclaw-82215-link` -> `/root/src/openclaw-82215`.
- Command run from the linked path using a real pseudo-terminal: `yes n | timeout 75s script -q -e -c "stty cols 120 rows 40; env TERM=dumb NO_COLOR=1 FORCE_COLOR=0 CI=0 OPENCLAW_HOME=<temp> OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=<temp>/state NODE_OPTIONS=--no-warnings node --preserve-symlinks --preserve-symlinks-main --import tsx ./src/entry.ts doctor" /tmp/openclaw-85735-symlink-doctor-2.log`.
- Environment facts: `pwd` was `/root/src/openclaw-82215-link`; `pwd -P` was `/root/src/openclaw-82215`; `git -C /root/src/openclaw-82215-link rev-parse --show-toplevel` returned `/root/src/openclaw-82215`.
- Evidence after fix: doctor displayed `Update OpenClaw from git before running doctor?` and did not print `This install is not a git checkout.`
- Observed result after fix: the linked checkout was recognized as a git checkout by the real doctor command path; I answered `No` to avoid running the update flow.

target.title: fix(config): skip shell env fallback on Windows
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #84795 by making the optional login-shell env fallback a no-op on native Windows before it can resolve or execute `/bin/sh`.

The existing POSIX behavior is unchanged. `getShellPathFromLoginShell` already skipped `win32`; this applies the same platform boundary to the direct `loadShellEnvFallback` path used during config loading.

## Verification

- `corepack pnpm install`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/infra/shell-env.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/infra/shell-env.ts src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- Testbox-through-Crabbox, provider `blacksmith-testbox`, id `tbx_01ksam41ew6rr7b0zmwf5hacq7`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26335373158:
  `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX_REMOTE_RUN=1 corepack pnpm test src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`
  - Result: 1 file / 21 tests passed.
- Autoreview: `/mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`
  - Result: clean, no accepted/actionable findings.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: With `OPENCLAW_LOAD_SHELL_ENV=1` or config-driven shell env import enabled on native Windows, OpenClaw should not try to execute `/bin/sh` and should not emit `[openclaw] shell env fallback failed: spawnSync /bin/sh ENOENT`.

Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox (`provider=blacksmith-testbox`, id `tbx_01ksam41ew6rr7b0zmwf5hacq7`, GitHub Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26335373158) plus a platform-specific regression test for the native Windows branch.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX_REMOTE_RUN=1 corepack pnpm test src/infra/shell-env.test.ts`

Evidence after fix: The new `shell-env` regression test calls `loadShellEnvFallback` with `platform: "win32"`, a missing expected key, and an `exec` stub that would throw `spawnSync /bin/sh ENOENT` if the POSIX shell probe ran. The result is `{ ok: true, applied: [] }`, the exec stub is not called, and no warning is logged. The full focused suite passed on Testbox.

Observed result after fix: The native Windows platform branch returns an empty shell-env probe result before shell resolution, so no `/bin/sh` process is attempted and no fallback warning is emitted.

What was not tested: I did not complete a full native Windows `openclaw status` smoke from this WSL worktree because Windows Node was blocked from importing files from the WSL filesystem with `Access is denied`. The tested behavior is the exact internal fallback branch that caused the warning.

target.title: fix(memory-core): avoid double bulleting promoted snippets
target.body:
## Summary
- strip one leading markdown bullet marker from promoted short-term snippets before writing MEMORY.md entries
- add regression coverage for snippets that already start with `- `

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Short-term memory promotion used to render snippets that already started with `- ` as `- - ...` in `MEMORY.md`. The patch strips one leading markdown bullet marker from the snippet before adding the promotion list marker.
- **Real environment tested**: David's live OpenClaw workspace on macOS, running the locally patched OpenClaw checkout that backs the active install.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Verified the installed OpenClaw version, verified the active runtime source contains the bullet-strip expression, and searched the live `MEMORY.md` for double-bullet promoted output.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal capture from the live workspace:

```text
$ openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.5.20 (c81ce46)

$ git show --no-patch --format="local runtime patch: %h %s" HEAD
local runtime patch: c81ce46562 fix(memory-core): preserve promoted bullet formatting

$ rg -n "snippet\.replace\(/\^- \+/" extensions/memory-core/src/short-term-promotion.ts
```

target.title: fix(ui): preserve WebChat messages across session switches
target.body:
## Summary
- Fixes #80855.
- Cache visible WebChat messages per session when switching conversations, then restore cached messages immediately when switching back before chat.history returns.
- Cache final assistant messages for switched-away sessions so subagent/report events are not dropped while another conversation is selected.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior addressed: WebChat session switching should not leave the restored conversation blank, and final assistant/subagent messages for a switched-away session should not be silently dropped.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on Linux with production TypeScript UI modules executed through Node 22.22.2 and tsx; no test runner or mocks were used for the proof commands.
- Exact steps or command run after the patch: Ran two node --import tsx commands from the repository root. One imported switchChatSession from ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.ts and switched main -> other -> main with real session state. The other imported handleChatEvent from ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts and delivered a final event for agent:main:webchat while main was selected.
- Evidence after fix:

```text
$ node --import tsx -e '<imports switchChatSession from ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.ts and switches main -> other -> main>'
{
  "activeSession": "main",
  "visibleMessagesAfterSwitchBack": [
    "main report before switch"
  ],
  "cachedOtherMessages": [
    "other reply before switch back"
  ]
}
```

```text
$ node --import tsx -e '<imports handleChatEvent from ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.ts and sends final event for agent:main:webchat while main is selected>'
{
  "result": null,
  "currentSession": "main",
  "currentMessages": [
    "main visible"
  ],
  "cachedWebchatMessages": [
    "subagent report while switched away"
  ]
}
```

- Observed result after fix: Switching back to main immediately restored "main report before switch" instead of an empty message list, and a final message for the switched-away WebChat session was retained in cachedWebchatMessages while the currently visible main session stayed unchanged.
- What was not tested: No browser screenshot or live multi-channel WSL2 deployment was captured; the proof exercises the production UI state-transition modules directly.

## Verification
- RED: corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts failed on the new switch-back preservation test before implementation.
- RED: corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts failed on the new switched-away final-event cache test before implementation.
- GREEN: corepack pnpm test ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts

target.title: fix(agents): preserve latest thinking replay signatures
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #85557.

`stripInvalidThinkingSignatures` now preserves the latest assistant message by default while continuing to strip invalid thinking signatures from older replay turns. That matches the existing `dropThinkingBlocks` invariant and Anthropic's latest-turn replay contract for thinking blocks.

The normal run path now wraps preserve-signature Anthropic-compatible replay in the existing thinking recovery retry, so an opaque provider-side signature rejection retries once without thinking replay instead of wedging the session. Recovery state is request-local, so each provider call can recover once.

Compaction opts out of latest-assistant preservation because it appends a new compaction prompt before provider replay. In that path, stored assistant turns are older history again and invalid signatures are stripped before the compaction request.

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts src/agents/transcript-policy.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts` - passed 7 files / 525 tests on rebased SHA `d53c7f4cd9148389c3baaad160647040ff7dd15c`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/replay-history.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test-harness.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test-harness.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/replay-history.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox: `tbx_01ks9mfvrdqkqjh5kt01m3tgt9`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26324478275, command `corepack pnpm check:changed`, exit 0

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Anthropic-compatible replay no longer mutates the latest assistant `thinking` or `redacted_thinking` blocks when invalid-signature stripping runs, avoiding provider rejections that require latest assistant thinking content to be replayed unchanged. Older invalid thinking blocks still normalize, and provider-side opaque signature failures retry once without thinking replay.

Real environment tested: WSL2 OpenClaw worktree `/root/src/openclaw-85557` at rebased SHA `d53c7f4cd9148389c3baaad160647040ff7dd15c`, plus Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox on Linux.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts src/agents/transcript-policy.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts`; `corepack pnpm check:changed` on Testbox `tbx_01ks9mfvrdqkqjh5kt01m3tgt9`.

Evidence after fix: focused tests passed 7 files / 525 tests on rebased SHA `d53c7f4cd9148389c3baaad160647040ff7dd15c`, covering latest-assistant preservation, older invalid-signature stripping, compaction opt-out for appended replay prompts, Bedrock-style invalid-signature recovery, and request-local recovery. Testbox `tbx_01ks9mfvrdqkqjh5kt01m3tgt9` changed gate passed core typecheck, core test typecheck, oxlint, import cycles, runtime sidecar, changelog, dependency, and guard lanes.

Observed result after fix: older assistant turns with missing or blank thinking signatures are still stripped or replaced with the omitted-reasoning fallback; the latest assistant turn is preserved unchanged for normal continuation; compaction strips invalid signatures from all stored history before adding its compaction prompt; Anthropic/Bedrock-style thinking signature errors retry once with sanitized recovery history.

What was not tested: live Anthropic or Bedrock API replay with real provider credentials; the proof is source-level plus focused local behavior tests and Linux Testbox changed-gate validation.

commands.author: jason-allen-oneal

target.title: fix(sandbox): mount workspace skills read-only
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #17931.

This PR protects skill paths exposed to Docker sandboxes from in-container mutation.

For `workspaceAccess: "rw"`, project skill roots are mounted read-only over the writable workspace:

- `<workspace>/skills` -> `/workspace/skills:ro,z`
- `<workspace>/.agents/skills` -> `/workspace/.agents/skills:ro,z`

For `workspaceAccess: "ro"` and `"none"`, this PR does not add a separate `/workspace/skills` bind. `/workspace` is already mounted read-only in those modes, so synced skills inherit the read-only workspace mount. This avoids stale nested bind mounts when skill sync removes and recreates the staged `skills/` directory.

## What changed

- Added read-only Docker overlays for project skill roots in `rw` mode.
- Kept `ro` and `none` protection on the existing read-only `/workspace` mount instead of adding a nested `/workspace/skills` bind.
- Removed the chmod-based copied-skill protection path because chmod is not a sandbox boundary and can interfere with refresh.
- Added stable read-only skill mount hash state so `rw` containers created before skill directories exist are not considered current after those directories appear.
- Bumped `SANDBOX_MOUNT_FORMAT_VERSION` from `2` to `3`.
- Added deterministic mount tests for `rw`, `ro`, and `none` behavior.

## Security impact

This prevents sandboxed Docker agents from rewriting the skill instructions they are executing under.

For `rw` users, normal workspace writes still work, but writes under protected skill paths now fail. This is an intentional compatibility-affecting hardening for workspace skill directories.

For `ro` and `none`, the synced skill staging tree remains refreshable from the host side, while the container sees those files through the existing read-only `/workspace` mount.

## Validation

Current PR head proofed:

```
460c1d647ac15e2d03d54bdf6778937f0f2f7e95
```

Targeted Vitest command:

```
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  src/agents/sandbox/workspace-mounts.test.ts \
  src/agents/sandbox/config-hash.test.ts \
  src/agents/sandbox.resolveSandboxContext.test.ts \
  src/agents/skills.build-workspace-skills-prompt.syncs-merged-skills-into-target-workspace.test.ts
```

## Real behavior proof

Redacted after-fix Docker proof from a local checkout using `openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim`.

The proof shows:

- `rw` keeps `/workspace` writable, but overlays `skills/` and `.agents/skills/` as read-only.
- Writes to `/workspace/skills/demo/SKILL.md` fail in `rw` mode.
- Writes to `/workspace/.agents/skills/demo/SKILL.md` fail in `rw` mode.
- `ro` has no separate `/workspace/skills` nested bind.
- A reused `ro` container sees refreshed synced skills after the host removes and recreates the staged `skills/` directory.
- Writes to `/workspace/skills/demo/SKILL.md` fail in `ro` mode through the read-only `/workspace` mount.

```
head=460c1d647ac15e2d03d54bdf6778937f0f2f7e95
image=openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim

=== mode=rw write protection ===
+ docker create --name openclaw-skill-proof-rw-1779553355 --workdir /workspace -v /redacted/agent-rw:/workspace:z -v /redacted/agent-rw/skills:/workspace/skills:ro,z -v /redacted/agent-rw/.agents/skills:/workspace/.agents/skills:ro,z openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim sleep infinity
6a64388fc419abddcb77b06109fcdf6956a68c051cabb0bdc3f6b55abea14815
+ docker start openclaw-skill-proof-rw-1779553355
openclaw-skill-proof-rw-1779553355
+ set +x
/redacted/agent-rw -> /workspace RW= true
/redacted/agent-rw/skills -> /workspace/skills RW= false
/redacted/agent-rw/.agents/skills -> /workspace/.agents/skills RW= false

ok
sh: 1: cannot create /workspace/skills/demo/SKILL.md: Read-only file system
OK: /workspace/skills write failed
sh: 1: cannot create /workspace/.agents/skills/demo/SKILL.md: Read-only file system
OK: /workspace/.agents/skills write failed

=== mode=ro refresh/reuse proof ===
+ docker create --name openclaw-skill-proof-ro-1779553355 --workdir /workspace -v /redacted/sandbox-ro:/workspace:ro,z -v /redacted/agent-ro:/agent:ro,z openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim sleep infinity
f5f66d5d84ba0518a27bf7930c9524f2e858a5bf198526a1dde15bff76a6c92b
+ docker start openclaw-skill-proof-ro-1779553355
openclaw-skill-proof-ro-1779553355
+ set +x
/redacted/sandbox-ro -> /workspace RW= false
/redacted/agent-ro -> /agent RW= false

initial:
# Demo
version=one
after host refresh without container recreate:
# Demo
version=two
sh: 1: cannot create /workspace/skills/demo/SKILL.md: Read-only file system
OK: ro /workspace/skills write failed via read-only /workspace
```

## Summary
- stop sending `payload.thinking` for the Foundry-hosted DeepSeek V4 route we reproduced locally
- keep Xiaomi/MiMo on the existing native `thinking` field behavior

- preserve the helper default behavior unless a caller opts out explicitly

## Why
This PR is now intentionally scoped to the **Foundry-hosted DeepSeek V4 OpenAI-compatible route** because that is the only route I reproduced locally.

Local repro before fix on `microsoft-foundry/deepseek-v4-pro` failed with:
- `400 Unrecognized request argument supplied: thinking`

My earlier wording overstated the proof surface by implying native DeepSeek API behavior was verified. It was not.

## What this patch actually changes
- the shared helper now supports an explicit opt-out of `payload.thinking`
- the current opt-out is wired at the DeepSeek plugin call site
- Xiaomi/MiMo keep their prior `payload.thinking` behavior

## Verification
### Real runtime proof
After the local runtime patch, these succeeded and returned `OK`:
- `openclaw agent --local --agent main --model microsoft-foundry/deepseek-v4-pro --thinking off --message 'Reply with exactly OK' --json`
- `openclaw agent --local --agent main --model microsoft-foundry/deepseek-v4-pro --thinking high --message 'Reply with exactly OK' --json`

### Scope caveat
I do **not** have direct live proof for the native `deepseek/...` provider from this Telegram-bound main session because direct DeepSeek model override was rejected by local agent policy. So this PR should be reviewed as a fix for the **Foundry-proven route**, not as verified native DeepSeek API behavior.

## Follow-up to prior PR
This supersedes the earlier narrower framing in #85683. That PR is closed in favor of this one.

target.title: fix(telegram): serialize topic dispatch replies
target.body:
## What
Fixes Telegram forum-topic dispatch races where a user-visible turn could collide with an active reply operation and finish without a visible final response, and where recovered Telegram topic replies could lose the effective topic id before final delivery.

## Why
Live Telegram testing showed a heartbeat/background turn could occupy the reply-operation lane for a topic-scoped session, then a real user message could continue without owning that operation and end silently. The same investigation found that final Telegram delivery could preserve `threadId` late while still carrying stale General/topic 1 facts in the message context, which is fragile before layering this with outbound context recording.

## Changes
- Serialize visible topic dispatch
- Skip heartbeat lock collisions
- Add abortable reply wait
- Normalize Telegram topic context
- Cover race regressions

## Testing
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.acp-abort.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Telegram forum-topic user turns no longer continue ownerless after an active reply-operation collision, and final replies for topic-scoped turns preserve internally consistent `message_thread_id` facts instead of falling back to General/topic 1.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw worktree on `fix/telegram-dispatch-races-main`, based on `upstream/main` `e0405ecc9b`; regression tests model the live Telegram topic `3731` race and recovered-topic delivery shape.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.acp-abort.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`; `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts`; `git diff --check`.

Evidence after fix: The targeted Vitest run passed 3 files / 240 tests, including the new heartbeat collision and Telegram topic-context regression coverage; formatting and diff whitespace checks passed.

Observed result after fix: User-visible Telegram topic dispatch waits for active reply operation ownership while heartbeat/background collisions skip; durable Telegram delivery receives `threadId=3731` plus corrected `ctxPayload.MessageThreadId=3731` and `ctxPayload.TransportThreadId=3731`.

What was not tested: No live Telegram Bot API send was run from this isolated main-based PR branch after the patch; live observation happened on the local gateway branch before this branch was prepared.

Refs #64810, #76920.

target.title: fix(sessions): honor explicit default model pins
target.body:
## Summary
- Persist explicit user selections of the configured default model as exact session model pins instead of clearing them as if they were unpinned defaults.
- Keep `/model default`, `session_status(model=default)`, and `sessions.patch model:null` as the explicit clear/unpin paths.
- Carry the exact selection through reply, queued-run, agent-command, reset, status, and sessions.patch paths so channel/parent/config fallback policy cannot silently replace user intent.

## Verification
Behavior addressed: Explicit `/model <configured default>` selections no longer lose user intent just because they match the configured primary; fallback policy treats them as strict user session selections, while `/model default` still clears back to unpinned behavior.
Real environment tested: local Codex worktree on beatrice.local, branch commit 87986ba3bfb7b6948ef4ce9fa5c9bc81cc7a603b atop upstream/main f2365053d330b100c024bdbe9159ea824b1d6243.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/reset-preserved-selection.test.ts src/sessions/model-overrides.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/model-selection.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/commands/status.summary.test.ts src/status/status-message.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts src/auto-reply/status.test.ts src/commands/status.command-sections.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/sessions/model-overrides.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/directive-handling.model.test.ts src/gateway/sessions-patch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/agents/agent-command.live-model-switch.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts src/config/sessions/reset-preserved-selection.test.ts src/auto-reply/status.test.ts src/commands/status.command-sections.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/session-reset-model.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-models.test.ts src/commands/status.summary.test.ts`; `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --write --threads=1 ...`; `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD`; `codex review --base upstream/main`

Observed result after fix: Explicit configured-default selections persist provider/model/source as user session overrides, survive resets, suppress configured/channel/parent fallback policy, and prevent fallback persistence from replacing user intent; `/model default` clears the override for unpinned behavior.
