target.title: Add session-scoped continuation lease workflow API
target.body:
## Summary

- add a plugin host workflow API for requesting one same-session continuation lease
- add a matching clear API for terminal/paused states
- pass trusted session context through the API instead of accepting model-selected session routing
- expose the workflow API through the plugin SDK
- add focused scheduled-turn contract coverage

## Why

Agents can already continue work when the model stays on target and the runtime path is lucky. The missing runtime primitive is a narrow, reviewable way for a feature to ask for one follow-up turn in the same trusted session, with host-owned routing and an explicit clear path.

This PR adds that primitive without adding a new autonomous product surface. A bundled plugin can ask the host to enqueue the next turn for the same trusted session, without introducing cross-session targeting, fanout, or model-selected routing.

This is intentionally narrower than #79925. It does not add silent wake modes, cross-session delegation, trusted insertion into sibling sessions, or host-wide fanout. It is also complementary to #85232: that PR recovers an already-queued stuck session; this API gives a plugin a narrow way to create one same-session follow-up turn in the first place.

## Safety

- session identity comes from host-owned command/tool context
- callers pass `session: ctx`; the model does not provide a target `sessionKey`
- continuation leases are same-session only
- replacement is best-effort on the existing scheduled-turn host hook
- clear removes the matching same-session lease when a feature reaches a stop state
- the SDK test helper exposes no-op defaults for both workflow APIs, so downstream plugin tests can use the new facade without custom stubs

## Tests

```text
pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/scheduled-turns.contract.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.bundle.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts

pnpm tsgo:core:test
pnpm build
```

Also verified as part of the stacked branch:

```text
pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/scheduled-turns.contract.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-index.bundle.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-subpaths.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/package-manifest.contract.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/plugin-entry-guardrails.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/plugin-tool-contracts.test.ts
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Plugin code can request and clear a same-session continuation lease through the new SDK workflow facade without taking a model-provided session target.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout `tmp/upstream-prs/openclaw-goal` on branch `goal-command-minimal`, commit `1e24225457`, with the scaffold commit `325f4c1f27` underneath.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a live `pnpm exec tsx` command in the checkout that imports `createTestPluginApi()` from `src/plugin-sdk/plugin-test-api.ts`, then calls `api.session.workflow.requestSessionContinuationLease(...)` and `api.session.workflow.clearSessionContinuationLease(...)` with `sessionKey: "agent:proof:session"` and `leaseKey: "active-goal"`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from that command:

```text
{"request":{"enqueued":false,"reason":"test plugin api"},"clear":{"removed":0,"failed":0}}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The SDK test API exposes both workflow methods and they return structured results instead of throwing or being absent. The contract tests cover the real scheduled-turn host hook replacement/clear behavior; this live command verifies the public SDK facade is callable from a real checkout.
- **What was not tested:** No production gateway was started for this scaffold-only PR; the follow-up `/goal` PR includes a live command-handler proof for the user-facing flow.

## Follow-up

A stacked follow-up PR adds the user-facing `/goal` command on top of this API: #85723.

That PR is the concrete product proof for this runtime primitive: humans get lifecycle controls and lightweight observability, while the model only gets bounded same-session continuation through `goal_status`.

I am happy to keep the two PRs stacked for easier review or combine them if maintainers prefer one review thread.

target.title: fix(ui): add agent selector to dreaming tab
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: the Control UI Dreaming tab had no agent selector, so users could not switch Dreaming context between agent workspaces.
- Why it matters: Dreaming artifacts (`DREAMS.md`, short-term recall, schedules, memory state) are agent-specific, so the tab was effectively stuck to the current session context.
- What changed: added an agent selector to the Dreaming tab chrome and wired it to the same preferred-session-per-agent logic used by chat session controls.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no backend dreaming behavior, routing behavior, or storage behavior was changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #63558
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Dreaming tab lacked an agent selector, preventing users from switching agent contexts there.
- Real environment tested: local checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` on Linux.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `cd ui && pnpm vitest run src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - `pnpm tsgo:core`
  - `pnpm lint:core`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
```text
✓ src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts > dreaming view > renders an agent selector and switches agent contexts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       16 passed (16)
```
- Observed result after fix: the Dreaming tab now renders an agent selector when multiple agents are available, preselects the active agent, and triggers agent-context switching through the same preferred-session resolution used elsewhere.
- What was not tested: end-to-end browser interaction against a live multi-agent gateway.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Dreaming used its own tab chrome and never reused the shared agent-selection/session-switching logic used in chat.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Dreaming view tests did not assert presence of an agent selector for multi-agent setups.
- Contributing context (if known): the rest of Control UI already assumed agent-specific contexts for many views, so Dreaming lagged behind that UX pattern.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `ui/src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: when multiple agents are available, Dreaming shows an agent selector, reflects the active agent, and calls back when the selection changes.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the missing feature is entirely in the Dreaming tab view/chrome layer.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): branch adds `renders an agent selector and switches agent contexts`.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

The Control UI Dreaming tab now shows an agent selector when multiple agent contexts are available, so users can switch Dreaming context directly from the tab.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Area: Control UI / Dreaming tab

### Steps

1. Render Dreaming with multiple available agent options.
2. Verify an agent selector is present.
3. Verify the current agent is selected.
4. Change the selection and verify the callback fires with the target agent id.

### Expected

- Dreaming shows the current agent and allows agent switching.

### Actual

- Dreaming view test passes with the new selector behavior.
- `tsgo:core` passes.
- `lint:core` passes.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified the selector appears in the Dreaming tab chrome.
- Verified the selected agent is reflected in the UI.
- Verified agent changes route through preferred-session-per-agent logic.
- Verified view test, `tsgo:core`, and `lint:core` all pass.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: the selector could route users to an unexpected session for an agent.
  - Mitigation: it uses the same preferred-session-per-agent resolution logic already used in chat controls.
- Risk: single-agent setups could get cluttered.
  - Mitigation: the selector renders only when more than one agent option is available.



---

## Real behavior proof

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.5 /tmp/pincer-openclaw

 ✓  gateway-methods  src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts (27 tests) 89ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  27 passed (27)
    Start at  00:25:12
    Duration  470ms
```

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Selecting an agent in the Dreaming tab called `switchChatSession()` but `loadDreamingStatus` and `loadDreamDiary` still sent `{}` to `doctor.memory.*`, showing the default agent's data regardless of selection. The tab could display DREAMS.md and memory state for the wrong agent.
**Real environment tested:** Node.js v24.14.0, vitest v4.1.5, Linux.
**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts`
**After-fix evidence:** 27/27 gateway method tests pass. TypeScript `tsc --noEmit` reports no errors across core and extensions configs.
**Observed result after fix:** `onSelectAgent` sets `selectedAgentId` on state, immediately reloads `dreamingStatus` and `dreamDiary` scoped to the selected agent. Gateway handlers accept optional `agentId` param with backward-compatible fallback to `resolveDefaultAgentId` when omitted.
**What was not tested:** Live multi-agent Control UI browser session; gateway handler routing is verified at the unit level through the existing 27 gateway method tests.

target.title: Doctor: continue structured repair conversion
target.body:
## Summary

This is the next Doctor repair-stack PR after #85566.

It moves plugin registry, configured plugin install, and legacy plugin manifest repairs onto the internal normalized Doctor runner path while keeping the public plugin health contract unchanged:

- legacy plugin manifests remain split detect/repair because file discovery and file rewrite are naturally separable
- configured plugin install repair runs as an internal core runner check at its existing positional contribution point
- plugin registry refresh runs as an internal core runner check at its existing positional contribution point

The public plugin-facing health contract remains split `detect(ctx, scope?)` plus optional `repair(ctx, findings)`. This PR does not add public SDK `run(ctx)` support and does not update generated plugin SDK baselines.

## Review Notes

This PR keeps the legacy Doctor order isomorphic by routing these repairs through their existing contribution positions instead of adding one broad early repair pass. Lint remains findings-oriented; fix owns mutation; dry-run/diff are derived from returned changes, effects, and diffs.

## Verification

- `git diff --check fork/doctor-core-run-contract-migration...HEAD`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental false --pretty false`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental false --pretty false`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-plugin-manifests.test.ts src/commands/doctor-plugin-manifests.ts src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.test.ts src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/health-check-runner-types.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run src/commands/doctor-plugin-manifests.test.ts src/commands/doctor-plugin-registry.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot --pool=threads --fileParallelism=false`

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior addressed: Plugin registry, configured plugin install, and legacy plugin manifest Doctor repairs now return structured runner data for findings, changes, effects, diffs, warnings, and validation while preserving their existing repair positions.

Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu 24.04 source checkout at `/root/src/openclaw-84290-doctor-next`, stacked on #85566 head `d64ff361f73129b7dd50809fdaee307a9c9b3d36`, current PR head `42238690404d10b3d5a79332c80fac7577f8a480`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Replayed only the two plugin-registry PR commits onto current PR2, resolved the old public-contract conflicts in favor of internal runner types, ran core/test typecheck, checked formatting, and ran focused Vitest coverage for plugin manifest repair, plugin registry repair, core health checks, doctor health contributions, repair flow, and lint flow.

Evidence after fix: Focused Vitest passed for 6 files / 79 tests; core test-project `tsgo` passed; core production `tsgo` passed; oxfmt passed on all 10 touched files; git diff checks passed.

Observed result after fix: The plugin repair checks are structured through the internal runner, positional Doctor contribution order is preserved, and no public plugin SDK health surface or generated SDK baseline changes are included.

What was not tested: No full `pnpm check`, no full Vitest suite, no remote Crabbox/Testbox run for this PR update yet.

target.title: fix(agents): preserve latest thinking replay signatures
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #85557.

`stripInvalidThinkingSignatures` now preserves the latest assistant message by default while continuing to strip invalid thinking signatures from older replay turns. That matches the existing `dropThinkingBlocks` invariant and Anthropic's latest-turn replay contract for thinking blocks.

The normal run path now wraps preserve-signature Anthropic-compatible replay in the existing thinking recovery retry, so an opaque provider-side signature rejection retries once without thinking replay instead of wedging the session. Recovery state is request-local, so each provider call can recover once.

Compaction opts out of latest-assistant preservation because it appends a new compaction prompt before provider replay. In that path, stored assistant turns are older history again and invalid signatures are stripped before the compaction request.

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts src/agents/transcript-policy.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts` - passed 7 files / 525 tests on rebased SHA `d53c7f4cd9148389c3baaad160647040ff7dd15c`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 CHANGELOG.md src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/replay-history.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test-harness.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test-harness.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/replay-history.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox: `tbx_01ks9mfvrdqkqjh5kt01m3tgt9`, Actions run https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26324478275, command `corepack pnpm check:changed`, exit 0

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Anthropic-compatible replay no longer mutates the latest assistant `thinking` or `redacted_thinking` blocks when invalid-signature stripping runs, avoiding provider rejections that require latest assistant thinking content to be replayed unchanged. Older invalid thinking blocks still normalize, and provider-side opaque signature failures retry once without thinking replay.

Real environment tested: WSL2 OpenClaw worktree `/root/src/openclaw-85557` at rebased SHA `d53c7f4cd9148389c3baaad160647040ff7dd15c`, plus Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox on Linux.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/thinking.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner.sanitize-session-history.test.ts src/agents/transcript-policy.test.ts src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.test.ts`; `corepack pnpm check:changed` on Testbox `tbx_01ks9mfvrdqkqjh5kt01m3tgt9`.

Evidence after fix: focused tests passed 7 files / 525 tests on rebased SHA `d53c7f4cd9148389c3baaad160647040ff7dd15c`, covering latest-assistant preservation, older invalid-signature stripping, compaction opt-out for appended replay prompts, Bedrock-style invalid-signature recovery, and request-local recovery. Testbox `tbx_01ks9mfvrdqkqjh5kt01m3tgt9` changed gate passed core typecheck, core test typecheck, oxlint, import cycles, runtime sidecar, changelog, dependency, and guard lanes.

Observed result after fix: older assistant turns with missing or blank thinking signatures are still stripped or replaced with the omitted-reasoning fallback; the latest assistant turn is preserved unchanged for normal continuation; compaction strips invalid signatures from all stored history before adding its compaction prompt; Anthropic/Bedrock-style thinking signature errors retry once with sanitized recovery history.

What was not tested: live Anthropic or Bedrock API replay with real provider credentials; the proof is source-level plus focused local behavior tests and Linux Testbox changed-gate validation.

target.title: fix(daemon): keep node tasks off gateway listener cleanup
target.body:
Summary
- Keep Windows node Scheduled Task stop/restart/status from treating the gateway listener port as node-owned runtime evidence.
- Add installed-command process matching for node Scheduled Task and Startup-folder fallback paths, so node lifecycle commands can still find the managed node host without touching unrelated gateway listeners or manual node runs.
- Cover registered task, Startup fallback, mixed fallback/task, and delayed schtasks launch cases.

Fixes #85289

Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/daemon/schtasks.ts src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_SERIAL=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts --reporter=dot`
- `codex review --uncommitted`

Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Windows node Scheduled Task lifecycle no longer reclaims or reports runtime from the Gateway listener port, while still detecting and stopping the installed node host process by matching the installed command line.
Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu-24.04 durable OpenClaw worktree at `/root/src/openclaw-85597`; native Windows host `redmond\giodl` with Ubuntu-24.04 WSL2; Windows Task Scheduler (`schtasks`) run/end/rerun/end against task name `OpenClaw Node`; WSL loopback listener reachable from Windows on `127.0.0.1:19897`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: From the WSL PR checkout at SHA `9204441152acbc9ace41eff1ed369eed053f3935`, ran focused daemon tests and maintainer Testbox below; then started a WSL TCP listener with Windows `Start-Process wsl.exe -u root -- node /tmp/oc85597-listener.js 19897`, created `OpenClaw Node` with `schtasks /Create`, ran `schtasks /Run`, `schtasks /End`, reran, and ended it again while probing `Test-NetConnection 127.0.0.1 -Port 19897` after each lifecycle step.
Evidence after fix: Focused daemon tests passed; Blacksmith Testbox `pnpm check:changed` passed; native Windows/WSL supplemental proof showed `tcp_127.0.0.1_19897=True` after WSL listener start, after node task run, after node task end, after node task rerun, and after node task second end.
Observed result after fix: Node service paths skip gateway listener/port cleanup, ignore unrelated manual node hosts on the same gateway port, and terminate only the matching installed node host command when stop/restart needs process cleanup. In the native Windows/WSL supplemental proof, Windows Task Scheduler lifecycle activity did not disrupt the WSL loopback listener.
What was not tested: full native `openclaw node install/start/stop/restart` against the active Sebby/OpenClaw VM setup. The local native package path was blocked by unrelated environment issues: `pnpm build` currently fails on `protobufjs` descriptor resolution in the shared WSL dependency tree, and the packaged launcher/tsx respawn hits a Windows child-process `Access is denied` failure on this host.

Additional maintainer verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.test.ts src/daemon/service.test.ts src/commands/node-daemon-install-helpers.test.ts` - passed 5 files / 78 tests
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/daemon/schtasks.ts src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/daemon/schtasks.ts src/daemon/schtasks.stop.test.ts src/daemon/schtasks.startup-fallback.test.ts`

target.title: fix(sandbox): mount workspace skills read-only
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #17931.

This PR protects skill paths exposed to Docker sandboxes from in-container mutation.

For `workspaceAccess: "rw"`, project skill roots are mounted read-only over the writable workspace:

- `<workspace>/skills` -> `/workspace/skills:ro,z`
- `<workspace>/.agents/skills` -> `/workspace/.agents/skills:ro,z`

For `workspaceAccess: "ro"` and `"none"`, this PR does not add a separate `/workspace/skills` bind. `/workspace` is already mounted read-only in those modes, so synced skills inherit the read-only workspace mount. This avoids stale nested bind mounts when skill sync removes and recreates the staged `skills/` directory.

## What changed

- Added read-only Docker overlays for project skill roots in `rw` mode.
- Kept `ro` and `none` protection on the existing read-only `/workspace` mount instead of adding a nested `/workspace/skills` bind.
- Removed the chmod-based copied-skill protection path because chmod is not a sandbox boundary and can interfere with refresh.
- Added stable read-only skill mount hash state so `rw` containers created before skill directories exist are not considered current after those directories appear.
- Bumped `SANDBOX_MOUNT_FORMAT_VERSION` from `2` to `3`.
- Added deterministic mount tests for `rw`, `ro`, and `none` behavior.

## Security impact

This prevents sandboxed Docker agents from rewriting the skill instructions they are executing under.

For `rw` users, normal workspace writes still work, but writes under protected skill paths now fail. This is an intentional compatibility-affecting hardening for workspace skill directories.

For `ro` and `none`, the synced skill staging tree remains refreshable from the host side, while the container sees those files through the existing read-only `/workspace` mount.

## Validation

Current PR head proofed:

```
460c1d647ac15e2d03d54bdf6778937f0f2f7e95
```

Targeted Vitest command:

```
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run \
  src/agents/sandbox/workspace-mounts.test.ts \
  src/agents/sandbox/config-hash.test.ts \
  src/agents/sandbox.resolveSandboxContext.test.ts \
  src/agents/skills.build-workspace-skills-prompt.syncs-merged-skills-into-target-workspace.test.ts
```

## Real behavior proof

Redacted after-fix Docker proof from a local checkout using `openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim`.

The proof shows:

- `rw` keeps `/workspace` writable, but overlays `skills/` and `.agents/skills/` as read-only.
- Writes to `/workspace/skills/demo/SKILL.md` fail in `rw` mode.
- Writes to `/workspace/.agents/skills/demo/SKILL.md` fail in `rw` mode.
- `ro` has no separate `/workspace/skills` nested bind.
- A reused `ro` container sees refreshed synced skills after the host removes and recreates the staged `skills/` directory.
- Writes to `/workspace/skills/demo/SKILL.md` fail in `ro` mode through the read-only `/workspace` mount.

```
head=460c1d647ac15e2d03d54bdf6778937f0f2f7e95
image=openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim

=== mode=rw write protection ===
+ docker create --name openclaw-skill-proof-rw-1779553355 --workdir /workspace -v /redacted/agent-rw:/workspace:z -v /redacted/agent-rw/skills:/workspace/skills:ro,z -v /redacted/agent-rw/.agents/skills:/workspace/.agents/skills:ro,z openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim sleep infinity
6a64388fc419abddcb77b06109fcdf6956a68c051cabb0bdc3f6b55abea14815
+ docker start openclaw-skill-proof-rw-1779553355
openclaw-skill-proof-rw-1779553355
+ set +x
/redacted/agent-rw -> /workspace RW= true
/redacted/agent-rw/skills -> /workspace/skills RW= false
/redacted/agent-rw/.agents/skills -> /workspace/.agents/skills RW= false

ok
sh: 1: cannot create /workspace/skills/demo/SKILL.md: Read-only file system
OK: /workspace/skills write failed
sh: 1: cannot create /workspace/.agents/skills/demo/SKILL.md: Read-only file system
OK: /workspace/.agents/skills write failed

=== mode=ro refresh/reuse proof ===
+ docker create --name openclaw-skill-proof-ro-1779553355 --workdir /workspace -v /redacted/sandbox-ro:/workspace:ro,z -v /redacted/agent-ro:/agent:ro,z openclaw-sandbox:bookworm-slim sleep infinity
f5f66d5d84ba0518a27bf7930c9524f2e858a5bf198526a1dde15bff76a6c92b
+ docker start openclaw-skill-proof-ro-1779553355
openclaw-skill-proof-ro-1779553355
+ set +x
/redacted/sandbox-ro -> /workspace RW= false
/redacted/agent-ro -> /agent RW= false

initial:
# Demo
version=one
after host refresh without container recreate:
# Demo
version=two
sh: 1: cannot create /workspace/skills/demo/SKILL.md: Read-only file system
OK: ro /workspace/skills write failed via read-only /workspace
```

commands.author: PollyBot13

target.title: Add /goal session continuation command
target.body:
## Summary

- add a bundled `goal` extension with `/goal help`, `/goal start`, `/goal status`, `/goal events`, `/goal pause`, `/goal resume`, `/goal done`, and `/goal clear`
- add a `goal_status` tool so the active session can report `continue`, `done`, `blocked`, `paused`, or `waiting_approval`
- store one visible goal state per trusted session
- use the continuation lease API from the scaffold PR to enqueue one same-session follow-up turn
- add stop gates, terminal states, and a continuation cap

## Dependency

Depends on the scaffold PR: #85722.

This PR is stacked on the scaffold branch locally so maintainers can review the runtime contract separately from the user-facing feature. On GitHub it targets `main`; until #85722 merges, the diff may include the scaffold changes as well. I am happy to keep the two PRs stacked, retarget after #85722 lands, or combine them if maintainers prefer one review thread.

## Why

The scaffold PR is deliberately small, but a runtime primitive is hard to judge without a real user flow. `/goal` is that user flow: a human starts an objective, inspects what happened, pauses or resumes continuation, and finishes or clears the goal while the model can request only bounded same-session continuation through `goal_status`.

The user experience is not only "let the agent continue." It is also "let the human see whether the agent is behaving well enough to keep continuing." That matters across model tiers: a frontier model may stay on target most of the time, but cheaper or smaller models need tighter rails, clearer stop states, and a visible decision trail so users can trust, pause, resume, or stop work without guessing.

This is why the command surface includes both lifecycle controls and lightweight observability. `/goal status` shows the current state; `/goal events` shows the recent decision trail. The user does not need a dashboard or debug mode to answer the basic question: "is this still doing the right thing?"

## Safety

- slash-command session context is host-owned
- `goal_status` uses trusted tool context, not model-supplied `sessionKey` or `goalId`
- model-driven `continue` is accepted only while the current goal is already in `continue`
- `done` and `blocked` are terminal for `/goal resume`; start a new goal to continue
- `paused` stops continuation until a human resumes
- `clear` removes the active user-facing goal state and clears any matching continuation lease
- continuation count is capped; hitting the cap moves the goal to `waiting_approval`, clears the lease, and tells the user to start a new goal
- no cross-session targeting, no fanout, no silent wake modes, no sub-agent continuation

## Commands

```text
/goal help
/goal start <objective>
/goal status
/goal events [n]
/goal pause
/goal resume
/goal done [note]
/goal clear [note]
```

## Tests

```text
pnpm exec vitest run extensions/goal/index.test.ts
pnpm test:extensions:batch -- goal
pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
pnpm exec oxlint extensions/goal/index.ts extensions/goal/index.test.ts \
  extensions/goal/src/command.ts extensions/goal/src/state.ts \
  extensions/goal/src/tool.ts extensions/goal/src/workflow.ts
pnpm build
```

Plugin guardrails also passed on the stacked branch:

```text
pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/package-manifest.contract.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/plugin-entry-guardrails.test.ts \
  src/plugins/contracts/plugin-tool-contracts.test.ts
```

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** A user can start a session goal, inspect status, inspect the recent event trail, pause continuation, clear the goal, and the command handler only uses the trusted session context.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout `tmp/upstream-prs/openclaw-goal` on branch `goal-command-minimal`, commit `3abbf4a999`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a live `pnpm exec tsx` command in the checkout that imports `handleGoalCommand` from `extensions/goal/src/command.ts`, uses a temporary in-memory store and fake workflow host API, then executes `/goal start`, `/goal status`, `/goal events`, `/goal pause`, `/goal events`, and `/goal clear` against trusted session key `agent:proof:session`.
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from that command:

```text
$ /goal start prove goal continuation
Goal started: prove goal continuation
Continuation: scheduled
$ /goal status
Goal: prove goal continuation
Status: continue
Continuations: 1
$ /goal events 5
2026-05-23T13:49:28.260Z created continue
2026-05-23T13:49:28.260Z lease_scheduled continue
$ /goal pause reviewer checkpoint
Goal: prove goal continuation
Status: paused
Continuations: 1
Note: reviewer checkpoint
$ /goal events 5
2026-05-23T13:49:28.260Z created continue
2026-05-23T13:49:28.260Z lease_scheduled continue
2026-05-23T13:49:28.261Z status paused - reviewer checkpoint
$ /goal clear finished proof
Goal cleared: prove goal continuation
workflow calls [["request","active-goal","agent:proof:session"],["clear","active-goal","agent:proof:session"],["clear","active-goal","agent:proof:session"]]
remaining state 0
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The command flow scheduled one same-session continuation lease, exposed current state via `/goal status`, exposed the decision trail via `/goal events`, cleared continuation on pause/clear, and removed visible goal state on clear (`remaining state 0`).
- **What was not tested:** No real Discord/WhatsApp slash-command adapter was exercised locally; the proof runs the actual bundled extension command handler and workflow calls in-process.

## Known local check noise

After ClawSweeper review, the branch was amended to use a flat string enum schema for `goal_status.status` and to return the declared `scheduled` continuation-test-helper result shape.

Full `pnpm check` reached the repo lint phase and failed on an unrelated pre-existing lint issue in `extensions/signal/src/daemon.ts` (`__testing` dangling underscore). The touched `/goal` and workflow files pass targeted lint, typecheck, plugin guardrails, and build.

target.title: fix(ui): add agent selector to dreaming tab
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: the Control UI Dreaming tab had no agent selector, so users could not switch Dreaming context between agent workspaces.
- Why it matters: Dreaming artifacts (`DREAMS.md`, short-term recall, schedules, memory state) are agent-specific, so the tab was effectively stuck to the current session context.
- What changed: added an agent selector to the Dreaming tab chrome and wired it to the same preferred-session-per-agent logic used by chat session controls.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no backend dreaming behavior, routing behavior, or storage behavior was changed.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #63558
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Dreaming tab lacked an agent selector, preventing users from switching agent contexts there.
- Real environment tested: local checkout of `openclaw/openclaw` on Linux.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `cd ui && pnpm vitest run src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - `pnpm tsgo:core`
  - `pnpm lint:core`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
```text
✓ src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts > dreaming view > renders an agent selector and switches agent contexts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       16 passed (16)
```
- Observed result after fix: the Dreaming tab now renders an agent selector when multiple agents are available, preselects the active agent, and triggers agent-context switching through the same preferred-session resolution used elsewhere.
- What was not tested: end-to-end browser interaction against a live multi-agent gateway.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Dreaming used its own tab chrome and never reused the shared agent-selection/session-switching logic used in chat.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Dreaming view tests did not assert presence of an agent selector for multi-agent setups.
- Contributing context (if known): the rest of Control UI already assumed agent-specific contexts for many views, so Dreaming lagged behind that UX pattern.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `ui/src/ui/views/dreaming.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: when multiple agents are available, Dreaming shows an agent selector, reflects the active agent, and calls back when the selection changes.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the missing feature is entirely in the Dreaming tab view/chrome layer.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): branch adds `renders an agent selector and switches agent contexts`.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

The Control UI Dreaming tab now shows an agent selector when multiple agent contexts are available, so users can switch Dreaming context directly from the tab.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: local repo checkout
- Area: Control UI / Dreaming tab

### Steps

1. Render Dreaming with multiple available agent options.
2. Verify an agent selector is present.
3. Verify the current agent is selected.
4. Change the selection and verify the callback fires with the target agent id.

### Expected

- Dreaming shows the current agent and allows agent switching.

### Actual

- Dreaming view test passes with the new selector behavior.
- `tsgo:core` passes.
- `lint:core` passes.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified the selector appears in the Dreaming tab chrome.
- Verified the selected agent is reflected in the UI.
- Verified agent changes route through preferred-session-per-agent logic.
- Verified view test, `tsgo:core`, and `lint:core` all pass.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: the selector could route users to an unexpected session for an agent.
  - Mitigation: it uses the same preferred-session-per-agent resolution logic already used in chat controls.
- Risk: single-agent setups could get cluttered.
  - Mitigation: the selector renders only when more than one agent option is available.



---

## Real behavior proof

```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.5 /tmp/pincer-openclaw

 ✓  gateway-methods  src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts (27 tests) 89ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
       Tests  27 passed (27)
    Start at  00:25:12
    Duration  470ms
```

**Behavior or issue addressed:** Selecting an agent in the Dreaming tab called `switchChatSession()` but `loadDreamingStatus` and `loadDreamDiary` still sent `{}` to `doctor.memory.*`, showing the default agent's data regardless of selection. The tab could display DREAMS.md and memory state for the wrong agent.
**Real environment tested:** Node.js v24.14.0, vitest v4.1.5, Linux.
**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/doctor.test.ts`
**After-fix evidence:** 27/27 gateway method tests pass. TypeScript `tsc --noEmit` reports no errors across core and extensions configs.
**Observed result after fix:** `onSelectAgent` sets `selectedAgentId` on state, immediately reloads `dreamingStatus` and `dreamDiary` scoped to the selected agent. Gateway handlers accept optional `agentId` param with backward-compatible fallback to `resolveDefaultAgentId` when omitted.
**What was not tested:** Live multi-agent Control UI browser session; gateway handler routing is verified at the unit level through the existing 27 gateway method tests.

target.title: Doctor: normalize repair checks inside runner
target.body:
## Summary

This PR narrows the first doctor repair migration step to the core doctor runner.

- Adds an internal normalized health-check shape so the repair runner can execute either the existing split `detect()` / `repair()` checks or an internal single-flow `run(ctx)` check.
- Keeps the public plugin SDK health contract unchanged: plugin authors still see `detect()` plus optional `repair()`, and no plugin SDK baseline is regenerated.
- Preserves the old split-check repair sequencing so detected findings are still reported if `repair()` throws.
- Threads dry-run, diff, repair effects, warnings, config updates, and post-repair validation through the normalized runner path.
- Adds focused lint and repair-flow tests covering run-mode checks, split-check adaptation, dry-run/diff preview behavior, repair status mapping, repair failure reporting, and validation after successful repair.

The goal is to make the runner easier to migrate safely without asking every existing doctor check author to split one boolean repair flow immediately. Existing split checks still work through the adapter; internal checks can move to one flow when that is clearer.

## Verification

Local/WSL proof:

- `git diff --check`

- Earlier focused Vitest after adding the split repair-failure regression test: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts` — 2 files / 14 tests passed

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Doctor repair runner now has one internal normalized execution path while preserving existing split-check repair error behavior, lint behavior, repair behavior, dry-run/diff previews, effects, warnings, config updates, and validation behavior.

Real environment tested: AWS Crabbox Linux VM (`cbx_cb0b70795a87`) from current PR head `d139d39e884019ce5de521660ba382a0499a1575`.

target.title: fix: preserve internal handoff status attribution [AI-assisted]
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: internal inter-session handoffs such as `subagent_announce` can run on the requester Telegram topic session and, after fallback, overwrite the same model/fallback/usage fields that `/status` presents as user-facing session state.
- Solution: make run/session usage persistence provenance-aware so internal handoff runs preserve the user-facing session model, context, usage, cost, and fallback notice fields.
- What changed: shared the agent-mediated completion source-tool vocabulary with Peter's #85089 subagent completion handoff path, then applied that provenance at the parent/requester session accounting boundary.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not reintroduce child-side `message` tool delivery or change the native subagent completion handoff behavior from #85089.

## Motivation

Fixes the concrete Telegram topic confusion in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/85082: an internal announce/recovery turn can use `google/gemini-2.5-flash` fallback while the normal topic session remains `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`, but `/status` and activity can make the topic look like it switched to Gemini.

The implementation is intentionally in the same direction as https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/85089: subagent completion stays mediated through the parent/requester session, and this PR only prevents internal handoff provenance from being recorded as the user-facing topic model/status state.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [x] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #85082
- Related #85089
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Internal subagent announce/recovery or agent-mediated completion turns that use fallback no longer replace the requester topic's user-facing model/fallback/usage state shown by `/status`.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Windows 11, Node 24.13.0, branch `fix/issue-85082-status-provenance`; plus reporter's live OpenClaw VPS Telegram instance running OpenClaw 2026.5.19 with this PR temporarily applied to the built `dist` files for proof capture.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. In Telegram forum topic `telegram:group:-1003871627242:topic:6823`, the reporter sent a prompt requiring `sessions_spawn` with `runtime="subagent"`, `agentId="codex"`, `context="isolated"`, `mode="run"`, `cleanup="delete"`, and `runTimeoutSeconds=120`.
  2. The child task was to reply exactly `PR85726 child subagent OK`.
  3. The parent used `sessions_yield` to wait for completion.
  4. After the internal completion handoff, the reporter ran `/status` in the same Telegram topic.
- Evidence after fix:
  - Gateway log accepted the spawn and wait: run `65a76f01-dfb2-4125-a0f1-07d80668f0aa`, child session `agent:codex:subagent:de83144a-492b-41e4-8820-effd80752265`.
  - Child Codex harness session `db366f78-a600-4237-b311-739053d8d331` ran under `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` and returned exactly `PR85726 child subagent OK`.
  - Parent transcript then received an inter-session message with `sourceTool=subagent_announce`, `sourceSession=agent:codex:subagent:de83144a-492b-41e4-8820-effd80752265`, task `reply exactly "PR85726 child subagent OK"`, and child result `PR85726 child subagent OK`.
  - The parent processed that internal announce run as `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` and returned `NO_REPLY`; this exposed a separate visible-delivery behavior already in the #85089/Peter handoff area, but it did not contaminate the topic's status attribution.
  - Backend session store after the handoff remained `modelProvider=openai-codex`, `model=gpt-5.5`, `lastTo=telegram:-1003871627242:topic:6823`, with no fallback notice fields set.
  - Reporter-provided `/status` screenshot from the same Telegram topic after the handoff showed: `Model: openai/gpt-5.5`, `Fallbacks: google/gemini-2.5-flash` configured, `Session: agent:main:telegram:group:-1003871627242:topic:6823 ? updated just now`, and `Execution: direct ? Runtime: OpenAI Codex`.
- Local regression commands run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts src/sessions/input-provenance.test.ts --reporter dot`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts --reporter dot`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts --reporter dot`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts --reporter dot`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/status.test.ts --reporter dot`; plus focused `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` cases for #85089's parent handoff path.
- Local evidence after fix: `src/sessions/input-provenance.test.ts` 18 passed, `src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts` 88 passed, `src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts` 46 passed, `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts` 51 passed, `src/auto-reply/status.test.ts` 74 passed, and focused `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` handoff cases 5 passed / 54 skipped. `oxfmt --check`, `oxlint`, and `git diff --check` also completed with no output/errors.
- Observed result after fix: In the live Telegram run, the real Codex harness subagent completed and the parent `subagent_announce` handoff did not rewrite the requester topic status. Same-topic `/status` after the handoff showed `Model: openai/gpt-5.5`, `Session: agent:main:telegram:group:-1003871627242:topic:6823 ? updated just now`, and `Execution: direct ? Runtime: OpenAI Codex`; the backend session store matched `modelProvider=openai-codex`, `model=gpt-5.5`, with no fallback notice fields set. In local regression tests, the forced internal Gemini fallback case preserves the requester session state.
- Fallback-specific guardrail: The new regression coverage seeds a user-facing `openai-codex/gpt-5.5` requester session, simulates an internal `subagent_announce` fallback to `google/gemini-2.5-flash`, and confirms the requester session keeps its visible model, context, token/cost accounting, and fallback notice fields instead of showing the internal Gemini run as the topic state.
- What was not tested: I did not deliberately break or slow OpenAI/Codex on the reporter's live install to force a Gemini fallback during the proof run. The live VPS proof exercises the real Codex harness subagent plus `subagent_announce` handoff path; the forced-fallback case is covered by targeted regression tests.
- Before evidence: Issue #85082 includes redacted live VPS trajectory/log output showing internal announce run `announce:v1:agent:codex:subagent:c34fca91-...` using `google/gemini-2.5-flash` with about 40k/122 tokens while a later normal topic turn returned to `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `subagent_announce` and adjacent internal handoff turns carry `inputProvenance`, but the reply/session accounting path treated their fallback provider/model/usage as ordinary requester session state.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no regression asserting that internal inter-session provenance must preserve user-facing session model/status fields.
- Contributing context (if known): #85089 simplified subagent completion delivery by keeping completion handoff mediated through the parent/requester. That leaves status attribution as the right boundary for this issue.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/sessions/input-provenance.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner.runreplyagent.e2e.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: an internal `subagent_announce` fallback to Gemini must not rewrite the requester topic's visible OpenAI/Codex model, fallback notices, context, token usage, or estimated cost.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it exercises the provenance, direct reply-run, queued followup, and persisted session usage boundaries without needing live Telegram credentials.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing status/fallback tests covered ordinary user-facing fallback, but not internal handoff attribution.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`/status` and activity state should no longer make a Telegram topic look like it switched to an internal fallback model merely because a subagent announce/recovery handoff used that fallback.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[subagent announce fallback] -> [requester session usage fields] -> [/status shows internal fallback as topic state]

After:
[subagent announce fallback + provenance] -> [preserve user-facing requester state] -> [/status keeps topic model attribution]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Windows 11 local Codex checkout; reporter live OpenClaw VPS for Telegram proof
- Runtime/container: Node 24.13.0 locally; live OpenClaw 2026.5.19 on VPS with temporary built-dist patch for proof
- Model/provider: OpenAI Codex primary with Google Gemini fallback attribution
- Integration/channel (if any): Telegram forum topic `telegram:group:-1003871627242:topic:6823`
- Relevant config (redacted): fallback provider configured as `google/gemini-2.5-flash`; secrets omitted

### Steps

1. Seed a requester session as `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`.
2. Run an internal inter-session `subagent_announce` path whose actual runtime metadata reports `google/gemini-2.5-flash` fallback.
3. Persist usage/fallback state and inspect the session entry/status-facing fields.
4. On the live VPS, spawn a real Codex harness subagent from the Telegram topic and observe the internal `subagent_announce` handoff.
5. Run `/status` in the same Telegram topic after the handoff.

### Expected

- The requester session keeps user-facing model/status attribution for `openai-codex/gpt-5.5`.
- Internal handoff fallback usage does not write the topic's visible fallback notice/token/cost fields.
- After the live Codex subagent handoff, `/status` still presents the requester topic as OpenAI/Codex rather than Gemini.

### Actual

- After this patch, local regression tests confirm the expected preserved state for the forced-fallback case.
- Live VPS Telegram proof confirmed the real Codex harness subagent and `subagent_announce` path, and `/status` after the handoff still showed `Model: openai/gpt-5.5` for the same topic session.

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [x] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: provenance classification, direct reply-run fallback preservation, queued followup preservation, status rendering coverage, #85089-style parent handoff source-tool classification, and a live Telegram forum-topic proof using a real `agentId="codex"` subagent.
- Edge cases checked: heartbeats still preserve only displayed model while keeping their usage updates; ordinary user-facing fallback notices still persist and render; generated media source tools still use the parent/message-tool evidence logic from #85089.
- What you did **not** verify: I did not deliberately force provider failure on the live VPS; fallback-specific preservation is covered by regression tests.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

  - Mitigation: the change preserves user-facing session state only for explicit internal/agent-mediated provenance; ordinary user-facing runs and heartbeat usage behavior remain covered by existing and new tests.

target.title: fix(web-ui): improve light theme visibility for select arrows and bubble hover
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes light theme visibility issues for select dropdown arrows and chat bubble hover highlights.

## Problem

1. Select dropdown arrow uses hardcoded `#888` SVG stroke, barely visible on white/light backgrounds
2. Chat bubble hover border uses 28% accent blend, too subtle in light theme for meaningful visual feedback

## Fix

- Darken dropdown arrow SVG stroke from `#888` to `#444` in light theme (`.cfg-select`)
- Increase chat-bubble:hover accent blend from 28% to 48% in light theme
- Add subtle box-shadow on bubble hover for clearer feedback

## Files Changed

| File | Changes |
|------|---------|
| `ui/src/styles/config.css` | +1 | Darker dropdown arrow SVG |
| `ui/src/styles/chat/grouped.css` | +5 | Stronger hover border + shadow |

Fixes: #85713

  - Mitigation: the change preserves user-facing session state only for explicit internal/agent-mediated provenance; ordinary user-facing runs and heartbeat usage behavior remain covered by existing and new tests.

target.title: Browser: harden noVNC bootstrap headers
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the loopback-only `/sandbox/novnc` bootstrap page relied on `no-store` and `no-referrer`, but it did not send a document CSP, `nosniff`, or frame-deny headers.
- Why it matters: this page carries the short-lived observer redirect flow, so it should be locked down as tightly as possible even though the route is loopback-only and token-gated.
- What changed: generate a per-response script nonce, attach a restrictive CSP, and add `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff` plus `X-Frame-Options: DENY`.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): the existing one-time observer token URL flow and noVNC redirect behavior stay the same.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #42476

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

None.

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22 + Vitest
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Browser bridge / sandbox noVNC observer
- Relevant config (redacted): loopback browser bridge with auth token and short-lived noVNC observer token

### Steps

1. Start the browser bridge with `resolveSandboxNoVncToken` enabled.
2. Request `/sandbox/novnc?token=<valid-token>`.
3. Inspect response headers and bootstrap HTML.

### Expected

- The bootstrap page still redirects to the local noVNC target.
- The response includes no-store/no-referrer plus strict document hardening headers.
- The password remains in the URL fragment, not the query string or `Location` header.

### Actual

- The response now includes a nonce-based CSP, `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff`, and `X-Frame-Options: DENY` while preserving the existing redirect flow.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: `/sandbox/novnc` still serves bootstrap HTML, still embeds the target noVNC fragment URL, and now emits the expected CSP / frame / nosniff headers.
- Edge cases checked: the CSP nonce in the header matches the nonce attached to the inline bootstrap script; token helper tests still pass.
- What you did **not** verify: end-to-end browser navigation in a real noVNC session.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:

- Files/config to restore: `src/browser/bridge-server.ts`, `src/browser/bridge-server.auth.test.ts`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: `/sandbox/novnc` returns 200 but the bootstrap script is blocked by CSP, or headers regress to being absent.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: an incorrect CSP could block the bootstrap redirect script.

target.title: fix(auth-profiles): canonicalize legacy default oauth session override
target.body:
## Summary

Describe the problem and fix in 2–5 bullets:

- Problem: sessions with `authProfileOverride=provider:default` could remain pinned to the legacy default id even when a canonical oauth alias (for example `provider:user@example.com`) existed.
- Why it matters: override drift makes profile identity less durable and can cause confusing auth/profile behavior across compaction/session lifecycle.
- What changed: `resolveSessionAuthProfileOverride` now canonicalizes legacy default ids to preferred oauth aliases (including fallback preference for non-`*:default` oauth aliases), with cooldown-aware selection.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no transport/auth provider protocol changes, no new permissions, no config schema changes.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor

- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Auto session overrides that were previously stored as `provider:default` now resolve to canonical oauth aliases when available, reducing legacy-default persistence.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No

### Steps

1. Create auth profile store containing `openai-codex:default` and non-default oauth alias entries.
2. Set `sessionEntry.authProfileOverride = "openai-codex:default"` with source `auto`.
3. Call `resolveSessionAuthProfileOverride(...)`.

### Expected

- Resolution prefers canonical non-default oauth alias where available.

### Actual

- After patch, resolution returns alias (`openai-codex:josh@example.com` or fallback oauth alias) and updates session override accordingly.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets

- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

Local verification:

```bash
pnpm vitest run src/agents/auth-profiles/session-override.test.ts
# PASS: 3 tests
```

Added regression coverage:
- canonicalizes legacy `:default` auto override to email profile when available
- prefers non-default oauth alias even when alias email metadata is missing

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:

target.title: feat(agents): expose estimated context budget status
target.body:
## Summary

Expose OpenClaw's pre-prompt context budget estimate when fresh provider usage is unavailable.

- capture a typed, path-free `contextBudgetStatus` snapshot from the pre-prompt estimate
- persist that snapshot on `SessionEntry`
- expose it on gateway session rows
- let `/status` show estimated context usage only when fresh provider usage is unavailable or absent
- keep positive fresh `totalTokens` authoritative
- prevent preserved-runtime heartbeat runs from replacing the main session's budget snapshot
- clear stale `contextBudgetStatus` on runtime refreshes without a current estimate, model override/reset, manual/native CLI compaction, reply-run reset, and `/new` session rotation
- reserve the new top-level session field from plugin session-entry slot mirroring

This is reporting-only. It does not change compaction thresholds, pruning, prompt-building, model routing, or payload policy.

Refs #80594, #54996, #77992, #84490, #83177, #43009, #83526, #8635.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/session.test.ts -t "drops cached skills snapshot when /new rotates an existing session" --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/command/session-store.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/status.test.ts -t "estimated context budget" --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts src/sessions/model-overrides.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-session-reset.test.ts -t "rotates the session and clears stale runtime and fallback fields" --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts -t "session rows expose estimated context budget status" --reporter=verbose`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server.sessions.reset-models.test.ts -t "sessions.reset clears stale estimated context budget status" --reporter=verbose`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Sessions whose model/provider path cannot return fresh context usage can expose OpenClaw's own pre-prompt estimate instead of showing only unknown/zero context usage. Runtime/session lifecycle boundaries now clear the persisted estimate with the rest of stale token/model state so a later session or model cannot show an old budget snapshot.

Real environment tested: WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04 durable PR worktree on refreshed PR head `e1aa1dcd6fb3bebe4b13f206fb6fefff98b397ba`, rebased onto current `origin/main`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: the verification commands listed above, plus a production status-renderer probe using `node --import tsx --input-type=module` against `src/status/status-message.ts` with a persisted stale `totalTokensFresh: false` session entry and current `contextBudgetStatus`.

Evidence after fix: The production status-renderer probe printed `Context: ~640k/1.0m (64% est)`, proving the user-visible `/status` renderer uses the estimate when fresh usage is unavailable. Focused tests proved current estimates persist and render, fresh `totalTokens` still win, gateway rows expose the field, and stale estimates are cleared on run refresh, model override, gateway/manual compaction, native CLI compaction, reply-run reset, `sessions.reset`, and `/new` rotation. Final Codex autoreview returned `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`.

Observed result after fix: `contextBudgetStatus` remains a path-free reporting object for current session estimates, and reset/refresh boundaries clear stale snapshots before `/status` or gateway rows can expose them.

What was not tested: Broad `pnpm check:changed`, full Vitest suite, and a live provider-backed `/status` run were not rerun locally.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): fast path text control commands
target.body:
## Summary
- Add a strict fast path for whole-message text control commands such as `/status`, `/thinking`, `/id`, `/usage`, `/send`, and `/activation`.
- Mark Feishu/Lark whole-message text slash commands as structured text command turns so the core fast path sees the actual channel context shape.
- Reuse the existing status, directive, and local command handlers so these replies can return before workspace/bootstrap or Codex runtime execution.
- Keep risky or prompt-bearing commands on the normal path, including `/new`, `/reset`, `/compact`, `/model`, `/queue`, `/stop`, `/exec`, `/trace`, inline command tokens, and commands with trailing prompt text.

## Root Cause / Impact
Feishu/Lark plain-text slash commands are not native slash commands. They can arrive as authorized text command messages, and Codex/GPT-backed sessions may otherwise route those local controls through the slower normal agent path. That can make `/status` or `/thinking` slow or fail to produce a visible operational reply even though they should be handled locally.

## Review Follow-up
- Addressed ClawSweeper P1 by having Feishu/Lark attach a structured `CommandTurn` with `kind: "text-slash"` for whole-message text slash commands.
- The core fast path now consumes structured text command turns and prefers `CommandTurn.body`, which covers normalized Feishu/Lark command bodies such as mention-stripped group commands.
- Added regression coverage for the actual Feishu-built context shape and for structured text command turns without relying only on legacy `CommandSource: "text"`.

## Related Issues
- Related to #82356, the canonical Feishu/Lark Codex/GPT slash-command `delivered=false` report. This PR addresses the low-risk local control-command subset by returning those replies before runtime execution.
- Related to #84155, which was closed as a duplicate of #82356 and contains newer gpt-5.5 Feishu command no-response logs.
- Adjacent to #78689, but this PR does not claim to fix the broader native Codex Feishu direct-session/message-tool HTTP 400 delivery evidence.
- Adjacent to #79409, but this PR intentionally excludes stateful/destructive/complex commands such as `/compact`, `/new`, and `/stop`, so it does not close that broader authorization/command-handling issue.

## Safety
- Requires the normal text-command enablement check.
- Requires authorized senders before returning local command replies.
- Only handles single-line, whole-message commands from a small whitelist.
- Does not mark inline command tokens such as `please check /status` as text command turns.
- Falls back to the existing reply flow when any prompt text remains.

## Validation
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply.fast-path.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/feishu/src/bot.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/bot.broadcast.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/auto-reply/command-turn-context.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/source-reply-delivery-mode.test.ts src/channels/inbound-event/context.test.ts`
- Extra WhatsApp regression attempt: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/process-message.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/inbound-dispatch.test.ts` partially ran; `inbound-dispatch.test.ts` passed, but `process-message.test.ts` could not load locally because `@protobufjs/aspromise` was missing from the interrupted local install.
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test` was attempted but did not complete locally because `pnpm exec`/hook dependency status checks repeatedly stalled on registry downloads for optional platform packages.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: Feishu/Lark whole-message text control commands such as `/status` should return a local operational reply instead of being routed through the slow Codex/GPT agent path and completing with no visible reply.

Real environment tested: Local macOS OpenClaw gateway started from this PR source at `c81dbf2f13c4`, OpenClaw `2026.5.22`, with the Feishu plugin loaded from `/Users/alex/Code/openclaw/extensions/feishu/index.ts`. The runtime used a real Feishu `feishu2` WebSocket account and a real p2p Feishu DM. Secrets and user/chat IDs are redacted below.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started a Feishu-only proof gateway from the PR source:

```sh
NODE_DISABLE_COMPILE_CACHE=1 \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr85616-feishu2-minimal.json \
OPENCLAW_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=/Users/alex/Code/openclaw/extensions \
OPENCLAW_TEST_TRUST_BUNDLED_PLUGINS_DIR=1 \
OPENCLAW_DISABLE_PERSISTED_PLUGIN_REGISTRY=1 \
OPENCLAW_SKIP_STARTUP_MODEL_PREWARM=1 \
OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_STARTUP_TRACE=1 \
node --import tsx src/entry.ts gateway run --bind loopback --port 18789 --force --ws-log compact --verbose
```

Then sent `/status` from the real Feishu `feishu2` DM.

Evidence after fix: Redacted runtime log excerpt from `/tmp/openclaw/openclaw-2026-05-23.log`:

```text
2026-05-23T21:49:35.639+08:00 [gateway] http server listening (1 plugin: feishu; 29.9s)
2026-05-23T21:49:39.831+08:00 [feishu] starting feishu[feishu2] (mode: websocket)
2026-05-23T21:49:56.165+08:00 [feishu] feishu[feishu2]: WebSocket client started
2026-05-23T21:49:58.334+08:00 [info]: [ '[ws]', 'ws client ready' ]
2026-05-23T21:52:22.137+08:00 [feishu] feishu[feishu2]: received message from <redacted-open-id> in <redacted-chat-id> (p2p)
2026-05-23T21:52:22.150+08:00 [feishu] feishu[feishu2]: Feishu[feishu2] DM from <redacted-open-id>: /status
2026-05-23T21:52:25.438+08:00 [feishu] feishu[feishu2]: dispatch complete (queuedFinal=true, replies=1)
```

For comparison, before this patch on installed OpenClaw `2026.5.20`, the same real Feishu2 `/status` path reached dispatch but produced no visible reply:

```text
2026-05-23T19:45:43.331+08:00 [feishu] feishu[feishu2]: Feishu[feishu2] DM from <redacted-open-id>: /status
2026-05-23T19:45:44.119+08:00 [feishu] feishu[feishu2]: dispatch complete (queuedFinal=false, replies=0)
```

Observed result after fix: The real Feishu2 `/status` DM now exits through the local control-command fast path and produces one queued final reply (`queuedFinal=true, replies=1`) instead of the previous no-reply completion (`queuedFinal=false, replies=0`).

What was not tested: This runtime proof covers the safe local text-control subset via Feishu p2p DM. It does not claim coverage for destructive/stateful commands (`/new`, `/reset`, `/compact`, `/stop`), prompt-bearing slash commands, native slash-command payloads, or group mention routing.

target.title: feat: PluginStatusProvider plugin API for TUI footer status (resolves #64086)
target.body:
Resolves #64086. Coordinates with #53774 (broader footer-customization tracker).

## What this PR does

Adds a typed `PluginStatusProvider` interface and `registerStatusProvider` method to `OpenClawPluginApi`, following the same registration shape as `registerTool` / `registerHook`. Providers are stored in the plugin registry under a new `statusProviders` field.

```ts
export type PluginStatusProvider = {
  /** A unique identifier for this provider. */
  id: string;
  /** Function returning the status string, or null/undefined to hide it. */
  getStatus: () => string | null | undefined;
};
```

Plugin author usage:

```ts
api.registerStatusProvider({
  id: "contextclaw.savings",
  getStatus: () => `cc: -${savedPercent}%`,
});
```

## Scope (deliberately small)

Per @clawsweeper's review on #64127 — *"narrow core/plugin-API design for TUI status contributions: define a typed `PluginStatusProvider`, expose `registerStatusProvider` through `OpenClawPluginApi`, store providers in the plugin registry"* — this PR lands exactly that surface and nothing else.

**Included:**
- `PluginStatusProvider` type + `registerStatusProvider` API entry on `OpenClawPluginApi`
- Plugin registry storage (`statusProviders: PluginStatusProviderRegistration[]`)
- Plumbing through `api-builder.ts`, registry, plugin-entry SDK
- Test helper updates so existing fixtures keep compiling

**Explicitly NOT included (and why):**
- **TUI render-path wire-up** — leaving this for a small follow-up PR so this API surface can stabilize and the maintainer team can shape the rendering UX (placement, ordering, theme-dim, truncation rules, etc.) on its own merits. Happy to send the follow-up as soon as this lands, or roll it in here if reviewers prefer one PR.
- **Cost-display footer drive-by** — was bundled into #64127, removed there in the split, and not added here. Can be revisited under #53774's broader template direction once that scope is settled.

## Plugin-author motivation

I'm shipping [ContextClaw](https://github.com/dodge1218/contextclaw) (a context-engine plugin) which currently has to monkeypatch the bundled TUI to surface live stats — that breaks on every OpenClaw release. A first-class `registerStatusProvider` seam gives plugins a supported path. Real demand, not speculative.

## Coordination with #53774

#53774 is the broader tracker for custom footer fields (cost / balance / rate-limit status, plus a footer template). This PR is **the plugin-API piece** of that vision — it's the seam plugins (and a future `tui.footerTemplate` config feature) can read from. Doesn't preempt #53774's template decisions.

## Files (11, +37 / -0)

- `src/plugins/types.ts` — `PluginStatusProvider` type + `registerStatusProvider` field on `OpenClawPluginApi`
- `src/plugins/registry-types.ts` — `PluginStatusProviderRegistration`, `statusProviders` field on `PluginRegistry`
- `src/plugins/registry.ts` — registry plumbing (`registerStatusProvider` handler)
- `src/plugins/registry-empty.ts` — empty registry init
- `src/plugins/api-builder.ts` — wiring through API builder
- `src/plugin-sdk/plugin-entry.ts` — SDK exposure
- 4 test helpers updated to include the new field
- `src/plugins/status.test-helpers.ts` — minimal helper used by future render-path tests

## Open questions for reviewers

- **Naming**: `getStatus` vs `render` vs `getText` — happy to follow your preference.
- **Return shape**: currently `string | null | undefined`. Should it support `{ text, color }` upfront for theming, or keep it minimal and add color later?
- **Multiple providers per plugin**: registry holds an array, so a single plugin can register multiple providers. Worth documenting / restricting?

Open to design feedback before this lands. Cheers.

target.title: infra: globalize unhandled rejection state (AI-assisted)
target.body:
## What
- Make installUnhandledRejectionHandler() idempotent so repeated calls do not attach multiple process.on("unhandledRejection", …) listeners.
- Move the unhandled rejection install flag and handler registry into a shared globalThis state keyed by Symbol.for("openclaw.unhandled-rejections.state"), and route registerUnhandledRejectionHandler / isUnhandledRejectionHandled through that shared state.

## Why
- Prevent double-installing unhandled rejection listeners, which can otherwise cause duplicated logs and confusing “why did it exit twice?” behavior if the handler is wired up more than once (tests, embedding, future refactors).
- Centralize unhandled rejection state so different entrypoints share a single source of truth, keeping behavior consistent while remaining a behavior-preserving change in the common case.
​
## AI Assistance
- AI-assisted: Codex
- Testing degree: fully tested
- Screenshots: N/A (non-UI change)

## Validation
- `pnpm build`
- `pnpm check`
- `pnpm test`
- `codex review --base origin/main` (no actionable findings)

## Prompts / Session Context
- Prompt summary: "Use above contribution guidelines as reference and create a PR."
- Work done: analyzed the existing diff, validated gates, and prepared this PR.

## Understanding Confirmation
- This change only refactors unhandled-rejection state ownership and access.
